025 | 2026-05-07 | changepoint-perf | changepoint pays seven allocations per Update (021 reported six — missed the line-287 defensive copy), runs two textually-duplicated `for r := 0; r < n; r++` passes that each call `math.Log(b.p[r])` on the same slot (~3 µs of redundant Log at R_max=500), invokes `b.hazard(r)` and `math.Log(1.0-h)`/`math.Log(h)` per r despite both being constant under constant hazard (hoist saves 4 LOC), pays two divisions per r in the NIG update when one `invDenom = 1.0/(kappa+1)` would suffice (~3.5 µs at R_max=500), maintains a dense `[]float64` posterior with no `epsPrune` threshold even though under stationary lambda << R_max only ~5×lambda slots carry mass > 1e-12 (10-50× speedup from sparse `(rMin, rMax)` window per bocpdms / Knoblauch-Damoulas 2018), ships zero benchmarks across all three test files, ships no `Offline(x []float64)` batch path that would exploit cumulative-sums-of-squares precompute (the bcp v2→v4 60× trick agent 023 named, ~60 LOC), and the in-place backwards-iteration NIG update would eliminate four of the seven allocations for free since the data-dependency is `b.mu[r] := f(b.mu[r-1])`; SoA-vs-AoS for the four NIG fields is fine as-is (cache-line-equivalent on the predictive pass; SoA wins for any future SIMD broadcast); only one branch hazard worth fixing (`a > b` in `logSumExp`, branchless via `math.Max + min := a+b-max`); for the missing detectors (slot 022 cross-ref) the topic-named complexity ceilings are PELT/FPOP O(n) average via inequality+functional pruning, BinSeg/WBS O(n log n) via SAT, e-divisive O(n² + nK log n) via SAT+segment-tree, MMD-CPD O(n)/O(n log n) via NEWMA (Keriven 2018) / MMDEW (Kalinke 2025) using RFF — the FFT speedup the topic asks about lives in the RFF / kernel-mean-embedding construction, not in MMD itself; fix-set is ~298 LOC of pure additions + 40 LOC refactor across 10 ranked items, would cut wall-time 3-5× and allocations 7→1 per Update at full backwards compatibility.
026 | 2026-05-07 | chaos-numerics | chaos is single-method (RK4 + forward Euler) with **no embedded-RK pair, no symplectic integrator, no Wolf/Benettin/Sano-Sawada Lyapunov spectrum estimator, no QR/Gram-Schmidt tangent-vector renormalisation, no adaptive step-size, no stiff solver, no Hénon map, no IEEE-754 edge cases, and golden-file coverage of 1/9 functions at 10/20 vectors** — every "energy/Hamiltonian conserved" test (`TestRK4_HarmonicOscillatorEnergy`, `TestLotkaVolterra_HamiltonianConserved`) is actually measuring non-symplectic RK4 secular drift, hidden by 1e-8 tolerance over 10⁴ steps and guaranteed to fail at the 10⁶-step horizons the docstring sells to Pistachio/Pulse/Oracle/Muse/Horizon; `RK4Step` `make()`s 5 fresh `[]float64` per call (~28 MB/s GC pressure at 60 FPS × 1000 particles × n=6) and the doc-comment hand-waves with "callers should implement the method inline" instead of providing the workspace; `LyapunovExponent` is 1D-iterated-map only (cannot analyse the Lorenz/Rössler/Lotka-Volterra/Van-der-Pol flows the same package defines), uses a hardcoded ε=1e-10 central difference where ∛ε_machine≈6e-6 is optimal (loses ~3 decimal digits per call), runs no warmup, and seeds `log(eps)=-23` for super-stable fixed points (silent wrong answer at logistic r=2 x=0.5); `euclideanDist` uses naïve `Σd²` (catastrophic cancellation on Lorenz |x|≈40); RK4's `(dt/6)·(k1+2k2+2k3+k4)` is uncompensated (loses 1-ULP increments at 10⁶-step horizons); fix-set is ~700 LOC of pure additions (Benettin spectrum + DOPRI5(4) + Leapfrog/Forest-Ruth + 7 golden files + Hénon + ensemble Lyapunov) plus ~100 LOC of bugfixes (workspace, ε constant, warmup, Kahan, Hypot, log-zero), all citation-grounded against Hairer-Nørsett-Wanner / Wolf 1985 / Benettin-Strelcyn 1980 / Sano-Sawada 1985, all backwards-compatible, closes every named gap in the topic prompt.

029 | 2026-05-07 | chaos-api | chaos's derivative signature is correct (`func(t, y, dydt []float64)`, in-place, CLAUDE.md rule-3 compliant) but every consumer of it violates the rule — `RK4Step` allocates 5 slices/call, `EulerStep` 1, `SolveODE` allocates one fresh `[]float64` *per step* (10⁶-step Lorenz = 10⁶ separate heap allocs, ~100 MB churn for a 24 MB result), and the `RK4Step` doc-comment literally tells consumers "implement the method inline" because the workspace pattern is missing — meanwhile `signal/fft.go` ships `FFT(real, imag []float64)` workspace-style and `optim/lbfgs.go` carries an explicit `state` struct so the precedent is already in the repo; every API axis the topic asked about is **absent** — no callbacks during integration (no per-step hook for energy guard, NaN guard, walltime budget, snapshot-every-N), no event detection (no zero-crossing for Poincaré sections — 028-axis-11 — no root-bracket-then-Brent on dense output, no terminal-event short-circuit), no dense output (RK4's `(k1, k4)` are discarded after each step instead of stashed for cubic-Hermite interpolation `y(t_n+θ·dt) = (1-θ)y_n + θy_{n+1} + θ(1-θ)[(1-2θ)Δy + (θ-1)dt·k1 + θ·dt·k4]` which is **free** — no integrator allocation, just keep `(k1,k4)` on the workspace), no `Problem{F, Y0, T0, T1, DT, Params}` struct (5 positional args is a foot-gun and is exactly why `BifurcationDiagram` at `analysis.go:76` doesn't even use `SolveODE` — it copies its own iterate loop), no `Stepper` interface (integrator switching is copy-paste-and-rename, not a one-liner), no streaming `Trajectory` iterator (Pulse/Oracle/Pistachio cannot do online statistics over the simulated horizon), no ensemble integration (Pistachio's 1000 particles = 1000 separate `SolveODE` calls each with its own workspace alloc); all 5 named systems (Lorenz, Rössler, LV, SIR, VdP) ignore the `t` argument so they're autonomous yet there's no autonomous fast-path and no idiom for forced VdP `+A·cos(ωt)`; cross-package parity check shows `chaos/` is the **only** computational package without workspace + problem-struct + algorithm-interface + streaming variant — `optim/` already has all four; recommended A1-A8 ladder ~355 LOC where A1+A2+A3+A4 (workspace + Stepper + Problem + Trajectory iterator, ~185 LOC) is the keystone refactor — events (A5), dense output (A6), ensemble (A8) all plug into that trio; backwards-compatible shims keep `RK4Step`/`SolveODE` working so no consumer breaks; out-of-scope (covered by 026/027/028): RK4 numerical accuracy, missing systems registry, DynamicalSystem first-class type, Lyapunov spectrum, RQA struct.
030 | 2026-05-07 | chaos-perf | chaos has zero benchmark files in the entire repo (`grep -rn "^func Benchmark" → 0`) so every cost number below is first-principles arithmetic — beyond the 026/029 5-make-per-step finding, the next layer of perf gaps is (1) **`f(t, y, dydt)` is an indirect closure call** that the Go compiler cannot inline (every `LorenzSystem(σ,ρ,β)` returns a heap-funcval capture; 4× indirect dispatch per RK4 step + caller-saved-register spill = ~12-20 ns of pure dispatch / 25-50% of post-026-fix wall time at n=3), (2) **no fixed-n RK4 specialisation** even though the package itself defines five n=2/3 systems — `RK4Step3` with stack `[3]float64` arrays + unrolled accumulators wins **3-4× on Lorenz** per Boost.Numeric.Odeint's documented `boost::array<double,3>` vs `std::vector<double>(3)` benchmark, (3) **`linalg/` ships no `Axpy`/`Gemv`/`AxpyInto` BLAS-1/2 primitive** (verified — `vector.go` has `VectorAdd/VectorScale/DotProduct/L2Norm` but no fused `y+=α·x`) which **blocks every implicit/stiff solver chaos will eventually ship** because the Newton inner loop has nowhere clean to land — fix is a 30-LOC PR to `linalg/` not chaos, but chaos is the consumer that exposes the gap, (4) **chaos has no goroutine/channel/ensemble primitive at all** despite Lyapunov-from-trajectory, basin-of-attraction, and IC/parameter Monte Carlo being the canonical embarrassingly-parallel workloads — `SolveEnsemble(f, ics, t0, tEnd, dt, workers)` with per-worker `RK4Workspace` is ~80 LOC and DiffEq.jl's matching `EnsembleProblem`+`EnsembleThreads()` benches at N-thread-linear scaling on n=3 Lorenz to 64 cores, (5) **leapfrog/Störmer-Verlet is exactly 2× faster than RK4 per step for separable Hamiltonian systems and the package neither ships it nor advertises it** — the LV/Hénon-Heiles/orbital consumers are silently paying 2× CPU + secular energy drift for the wrong method, (6) **`SolveODE` materialises trajectory as `[][]float64` (one heap alloc per step)** — flat `[]float64` of length `(steps+1)·n` with stride-n is one allocation total (~25× alloc reduction on n=3 × 10⁴ steps) and SoA layout (`[][]float64` of `n` components × `steps+1` long each) wins on the analysis-tool consumer side, plus `IntegrateStream(emit)` for zero-materialisation streaming, (7) **`RecurrencePlot` calls `math.Sqrt` ~N²/2 times unnecessarily** — comparing `d ≤ θ` ⇔ `d² ≤ θ²` lets the inner loop call zero Sqrts for **~25% wall-time reduction** at N=10⁴ via a 5-line edit, (8) **`BifurcationDiagram` materialises `[]BifurcationPoint` of size rSteps·samples** (~3.2 MB at default density) when consumers want a histogram or disk-stream — `BifurcationStream(emit)` callback variant is ~30 LOC, (9) DOPRI5(4) error-norm cost is negligible (~5 ns at n=3) so adaptive step is purely upside (5-7× total speedup at fixed tolerance), (10) `Stepper` interface dispatch (per 029-A2) is ~3-5 ns/step = 1-5% overhead, well within budget — perf is not a reason to avoid the interface, (11) two minor inner-loop branch nits (`GameOfLife` 8-neighbour unroll ~5%, `LyapunovExponent` `Log(Max(absDeriv, eps))` branchless), (12) **the SOTA µs/step for Lorenz: hand-C ~30-40 ns, DiffEq.jl `Tsit5()` ~50-70 ns, Boost.odeint generic-vec ~80-120 ns — reality post-026/029-fix would be ~40-60 ns/step at n=3 with the P2 fixed-n specialisation, matching DiffEq.jl and within 1.5× of the C reference**; ranked fix-set is ~430 LOC of pure additions across 12 items where P1 (`bench_test.go`) must ship first as the regression-pin baseline before any other claim is measurable, P3 (Axpy in `linalg/`) and P4 (SolveEnsemble) are the two highest user-facing wins, and the audit explicitly does not duplicate 026-N1 (5 makes), 029-A1 (workspace API), 029-A2 (Stepper interface), or 026-N2 (secular drift) — those findings are owned and this report goes one layer deeper on each.

second missing axis is **typed `Color{V,CS}` value with encoding state** (palette's `Srgb` vs `LinSrgb` are types, linebender's `OpaqueColor<CS>`/`PremulColor<CS>` separate the premul-α state, CoreImage's `CIColor(red:green:blue:colorSpace:)` matches) so sRGB-blended-as-if-linear and straight-α-vs-premul become compile-time errors; second public impl in any language), ACES 2.0 Output Transform (preview in OCIO 2.4 Sep 2024, ~250 LOC; no zero-dep Go impl exists), AgX as Blender-4-parity default tonemapper. Recommended fused commit C1=`Color{V,CS}` + Floyd-Warshall graph + `Transform` registry + `Convert` + `DescribePath` (~700 LOC) is load-bearing for everything else and unblocks every Tier-1 catalog item from 032 (each becomes a `RegisterEdge` call instead of an N²-explicit-converter expansion); total proposed engineering-refactor surface ~1,690 LOC across 9 commits, all zero-dep, all golden-file-testable, no YAML/GPU/JIT. Non-overlap with 031 (numerics) and 032 (catalog) is the orthogonal interface-design axis; the graph engine is the architectural commit that makes 032's 67 catalog items tractable. four wrong-space bug classes are uncatchable today (sRGB-fed-to-linear, white-point conflation D50-vs-D65 PCS, ΔE-on-non-Lab, Lab L=50-vs-0.5 scale); white-point handling is the single worst axis — `LinearRGBToXYZ` is silently D65 only via docstring, `XYZToLab` accepts arbitrary `(Xn,Yn,Zn)` so the standard-tutorial D50 PCS gives a silently-wrong Lab against the D65 sRGB matrix, and the only D50/D65/A xy values in the package are in **comment text** at adapt.go (no exported `D65`, `D50`, no `WhitePoint` type, no `XYWhitePoint(x,y)` helper despite the body of `BradfordAdapt` containing exactly that math at lines 35-37); `BradfordAdapt(X,Y,Z,sx,sy,dx,dy)` is **7 positional float64** with src/dst order undocumented in the signature; sRGB→Lab requires 4 calls + 9 named locals + 3 magic floats today, would be 1 call with 033 graph engine; `image/color` stdlib interop is **zero** and `audio/spectrogram/visualise.go:134` (the only first-party `image/color` caller) hard-codes `A: 255` and bypasses `reality/color` entirely with hand-coded byte-LUT — wrong-space bug class A in production inside the same monorepo; uint8/float discipline absent — no `SRGBToLinearU8` LUT means PNG decode at Pistachio's 60FPS×1920×1080×3ch = 372 M `math.Pow`/s where 0 is achievable with 256-entry LUT (12 LOC); alpha does not exist anywhere in the package — no `Mix`, no Porter-Duff `Over`, no `Premul` distinction; `BlackbodyToXYZ` returns Y=1 normalisation (wrong for color-temperature comparison; chromaticity-invariant `(x,y)` is the SOTA shape) and `PlanckRadiance(λ,T)` is buried unexported at spectral.go:44-48 blocking 033-axis-2.6 SpectralDistribution; `ToneMapReinhard` is per-channel only (hue-shift bug, Reinhard 2002 documents the luminance-preserving variant) and the `whitePoint` parameter is the HDR luminance ceiling — same English term as the *chromatic* white point, two unrelated physical concepts collide; cluster-comparison: `color` and `chaos` are the only two reality packages with **zero exported types** (`prob`/`control`/`crypto` all have method receivers, `geometry`/`linalg` use named-convention typed arrays); recommended fix-set ~317 LOC across E1-E10 all strictly additive (E1=`WhitePoint` type + 11 illuminant constants + `XYZToLabW` shim ~30 LOC ships first and closes 80% of bugs alone; E3 LUT, E4 stdlib interop, E2 BradfordAdaptW, E5 Mix/Over, E6 BlackbodyChromaticity+PlanckRadiance, E7 ToneMapReinhardLum, E8 LabLCH+HSL, E9 Validate, E10 docstring tightening), backwards-compatible without 033 architectural refactor — the per-call paper-cut layer 033 graph engine cannot reach. 038 | 2026-05-07 | combinatorics-sota | reality/combinatorics scores 0/13 on the engineering-design axes the SOTA combinatorial-software ecosystem (FLINT 3.5, Sage `sage.combinat` 10.6, Mathematica 14 + Combinatorica, Maple combstruct/gfun, NAUTY 2.9.3 + bliss + Traces, sympy.combinatorics + python-flint) converged on — eleven are pure-interface wins requiring zero JIT/IR (typed exact-int boundary à la FLINT `fmpz` with `*Big`/`*Exact` companions parallel to existing `float64` API; lazy `EnumeratedSet` + `iter.Seq[T]` iteration replacing materialised slices à la Sage `Partitions(n)` with `IntegerListsLex` Hivert-Thiery-2000-Gillespie-Schilling-Thiery-2015 backend that yields one element per O(1) memory; `FiniteEnumerated[T any]` Go-interface abstraction mirroring Sage's `EnumeratedSets()` / `FiniteEnumeratedSets()` / `InfiniteEnumeratedSets()` category framework with default Cardinality+All+Rank+Unrank+Random+Contains methods; complete `Next*` family (Knuth TAOCP 4A §7.2.1 universal pattern) — 037-T1.G1-G5 already sized; rank/unrank bijection to `{0..|S|-1}` per Lehmer-code/factorial-base-1964/combinatorial-number-system enabling Random=Unrank-of-rng.Intn-of-cardinality for-free without Fisher-Yates or rejection sampling; **OEIS A-number annotation in source comments and emission in golden-file JSON as `"oeis"` field** — every Sage combinat fn carries A-number, every python-flint/sympy fn carries A-number, OEIS itself maintains Works-Citing-OEIS as core infrastructure, A-number is a globally-unique stable identifier like a DOI/CVE so a `// OEIS A000110` in BellNumber lets any cross-language port resolve to the same canonical sequence — current reality has zero; first-class `Permutation` / `Partition` / `Composition` typed wrappers with Inverse/Sign/CycleType/Compose/Lehmer/Rank/Conjugate/HookLength/Dominates/IsValid methods replacing the bare `[]int` slices that force every consumer to re-derive Sign/Cycles; truncated GF/EGF symbolic engine via `[]float64` or `[]big.Rat` coefficient slice with multiply/divide/compose/inverse/log/exp/D/integrate ~250 LOC making all counting fns derived as `[z^n] f` from one unifying primitive — gfun-Salvy-Zimmermann-1994-Maple parallel; `Is*` predicate symmetry pairing every constructive `Generate*` with a validator (current=zero) enabling slices.Filter(IsValidPartition,...) one-liners and property-based tests; seed-deterministic `Random*` family (current=only RandomSubset) via Unrank for closed-form classes plus Nijenhuis-Wilf-1975-hook-walk for partitions and Greene-Nijenhuis-Wilf-1979 for Young tableaux; cardinality-vs-iteration separation via the `FiniteEnumerated[T]` interface — Sage's hard-line `Partitions(n).cardinality()` returns p(n) by Hardy-Ramanujan-Rademacher without iterating, `for x in Partitions(n)` iterates lazily, the two operations are typed-distinct; **cross-language exact-integer wire format** — FLINT base-62, Sage decimal-string, Mathematica FullForm, all encode big-ints as strings, but reality's `binomial_coeff.json` uses JSON `float64` numbers which is wrong for any value past 2^53 breaking CLAUDE.md design-rule-1 ("golden files are the proof") for Catalan(34)/Bell(26)/Fibonacci(94) — fix is `"expected": "14544636039226909116"` string-form with type-branch in test harness ~30 LOC); two correctly out-of-scope items are full lazy-infinite combinatorial-species framework (Sage `combinat.species` + `LazyPowerSeriesRing(QQ)` ~6000 LOC over 5 years — defer to truncated GF) and NAUTY-quality canonical-form individualisation-refinement for graph isomorphism (~2000 LOC research-grade — ship 50-LOC `Canonicalize` interface stub with brute-force-permute-adjacency-matrix backend for n≤8 instead, leaving NAUTY-quality backend to separate effort); highest-leverage commit is W1=OEIS A-numbers in 10 existing docstrings + golden JSON `"oeis"` field (~30 minutes — A-numbers already enumerated in 037 tables: A000142 factorial / A000110 Bell / A008275 Stirling-1st / A008277 Stirling-2nd / A000045 Fibonacci / A000108 Catalan / A000041 partitions / A000166 derangements / A007318 binomial / A008292 Eulerian) + W2=`Permutation`/`Partition` typed wrappers (~200 LOC, axis #7) as one focused PR closing OEIS traceability and unblocking every 037-T2/T3 item; W1-W7 (~1,000 LOC across ~3 days) takes the engineering-design score from 0/13 to 7/13; non-overlap with 036 (numerical contracts on existing fns) and 037 (missing primitives catalog) is the orthogonal interface-design axis — same input function appears under all three lenses but with three different framings (036 says "Multinomial is missing", 037 sizes Multinomial at 8 LOC, 038 says Multinomial signature should be variadic+Big-companion+OEIS-annotated); 039 | 2026-05-07 | combinatorics-api | Go-shape API audit (orthogonal to 036/037/038): the 14-fn surface fails seven Go-specific ergonomics tests sibling reality packages already pass — (1) zero `math/big` interop anywhere in the repo despite CLAUDE.md authorising it, combinatorics is the natural first adopter for Catalan(34)/Bell(26)/Fibonacci(94) cliff cases via parallel `*Big` family (FactorialBig/BinomialCoeffBig/CatalanBig/FibonacciBig/BellBig/StirlingFirstBig/StirlingSecondBig/PartitionsBig/PermutationsBig/DerangementBig ~120 LOC, plus workspace-accepting `*BigInto(dst *big.Int, ...) *big.Int` per signal/fft.go pattern for hot-path consumers); (2) zero `iter.Seq[T]` use despite go.mod declaring go 1.24 (Go 1.23 shipped range-over-func Aug 2024) — Permute/Combine/PartitionsOf/SetPartitionsOf/Subsets as `iter.Seq[[]int]` ~120 LOC closes the GeneratePermutations(15)→1.3T-allocation OOM gap with zero-alloc-per-emit semantics, and `iter.Pull` adaptor collapses Sage's four-function {Next*, *All, *Iter, *Stream} family to one Go function; (3) `Permutations(n,k)` count-fn vs `GeneratePermutations(items)` generator collision — same noun, opposite return types, autocomplete cannot disambiguate — fix is verb-form lazy generators `Permute`/`Combine` (matching stdlib slices.Sort verb idiom) leaving count fns unsuffixed; (4) zero exported types — `Permutation`/`Combination`/`Partition`/`Composition` are all bare `[]int`, the only computational reality package alongside color/ with no canonical-object types — fix is 6-line type-alias file (`type Permutation = []int` etc.) which is strictly additive and compatible with every existing call site; (5) RandomSubset takes anonymous `interface{ Intn(int) int }` not matching math/rand v1 OR v2 OR crypto/rng.go's `Uint64()` convention — fix is named `RNG` interface + Random{Permutation,Combination,Partition} companions; (6) GeneratePermutations(13)→6.2B []int silent-OOM — no MaxN const, no panic, no error return — fix is documented MaxN constant + recoverable panic so caller's error boundary catches it; (7) NextPermutation is the *only* Next* in the package, not a *pattern* — Knuth TAOCP 4A §7.2.1 wants the full family; (8) zero OEIS A-numbers in source despite godoc auto-linkifying `https://oeis.org/A000110` — 10 docstring edits ~30min for A000142/A007318/A000108/A000045/A008275/A008277/A000110/A000041/A000166 and golden-file `"oeis"` field per 038. Cross-package call-site analysis: combinatorics has *one* internal consumer (prob/ → Factorial only), 21 of 22 reality packages don't import it, so all proposed fixes are strictly additive without breaking any caller. Recommended A1-A9 commit ladder ~420 LOC takes API-ergonomics score from "no types/no big-int/no iter/no named RNG/no error contract" to "all five conventions present and matching reality's linalg/signal/optim/prob/crypto sibling-package idioms"; A1 (OEIS docstrings) + A2 (type aliases) + A3 (named RNG interface) is the trivial 25-LOC hygiene first commit, A4 (counting_big.go) + A5 (generate_iter.go) is the ~240-LOC keystone. Non-overlap with 036 (numerical contracts on existing fns) / 037 (missing-primitive math catalogue) / 038 (FLINT/Sage engineering-design crosswalk) — this report is the orthogonal Go-shape lens (math/big interop convention, iter.Seq Go-1.23 idiom, type aliases vs definitions, named RNG interface, MaxN-panic vs silent OOM, godoc URL auto-linking). Report at agents/039-combinatorics-api.md, 392 lines.
040 | 2026-05-07 | combinatorics-perf | Runtime-cost lens orthogonal to 036/037/038/039: package has zero benchmarks, zero memoization across calls, zero allocation reuse in DP loops, zero amortisation across multi-call patterns; eight perf items ranked P1-P8 ~390 LOC + ~80 LOC bench. P1 (highest impact) — five DP fns (StirlingFirst/StirlingSecond/Bell/IntegerPartitions/Catalan) discard intermediate tables on every call, so callers computing S(n, k) for k=1..n do n× O(n²) work when one O(n²) call suffices; FLINT arith_bell_number and Sage @cached_function maintain process-global table extending lazily, ~250 LOC for *Memo variants gives 100-1000× multi-call speedup (Pistachio BellNumber(0..30)@60fps: 1.67M float ops/s collapses to 1,860 lookups/s, 900× reduction). P6 — GeneratePermutations(10) allocates 3.6M result slices + 3.6M []int headers + 36 result-slice growth reallocations (~370 MB resident, ~173 MB transient): fix is pre-size via known n! count (eliminates 36 grow-reallocs) + single backing []int(n×n!) sliced into n! windows (3.6M allocations → 2), allocator pressure drops 1.8M× and GC pauses disappear from call. P5 — Lgamma+Exp+Round used in Factorial(n>20)/BinomialCoeff/Permutations costs ~260 ns/call vs k-multiplication product loop at ~k ns; for typical k≤20 product is 13× faster, crossover at k~80, overflow at k~30 forces Lgamma; recommended hybrid (product for n≤60, Lgamma for n>60) gives 10-15× speedup on typical Bayesian-PMF callers and eliminates round-correction-masks-precision-drift bug class. P7 — zero multi-call amortisation: Catalan(0..N) is 3N Lgamma calls when recurrence C_{n+1}=C_n×2(2n+1)/(n+2) is N multiplications (~50× faster); same for FibonacciSequence (N×O(log N) matrix-exp → O(N) two-element rolling), FactorialSequence (running product), BellSequence (Bell triangle naturally produces diagonal in one O(n²) sweep), IntegerPartitionsSequence (current dp[] already holds p(0..n) and discards all but dp[n] — just return slice); five *Sequence companions ~50 LOC, 5-50× speedups. P8 (implementation strategy for 039.A4 *Big) — naive left-fold big.Int factorial is O(n²) bit-ops because result grows linearly so each Mul is O(bits(result)); FLINT/GMP/Python math.factorial all use balanced binary product tree (treeProduct(xs[:mid]) × treeProduct(xs[mid:])) at log n levels giving 10× faster at n=1000, 100× at n=10000; even better Schönhage 2000 split-and-prime-swing O(n log³n) ~3× over binary tree but ~200 LOC defer; same tree shape for BinomialCoeffBig (k consecutive ints / k!) and MultinomialBig. P2 — DP loops call make([]float64, k+1) inside outer loop (n allocations of which n-1 are immediately garbage), fix is two-buffer ping-pong: 4-line edit per fn, n+1→1 alloc/call, two rows fit L1 for k<4000. P3 — Pascal/Stirling/Bell tables have triangular property s(n,k)=0 for k>n, packed triangular layout for memoization tables (with P1) halves memory at n=200 (320 KiB→161 KiB) and gives perfect cache-line walk per row. P4 — sole positive: all five DP fns + Fibonacci + GeneratePermutations use iterative loops with explicit work buffers, zero recursion in counting.go, no stack-growth risk (Sage older versions used recursive generators, modern Sage moved to iterative for same reason — reality already there). Ranked impact table P1>P6>P5>P7>P8>P2>P3>P4; total speedup envelope: multi-call workloads (normal combinatorics use case — almost always tabulated) 100-1000×, single-call hot path (per-frame BinomialCoeff) 10-15×, generation hot paths get GC-pause-elimination not just speed. Cross-ref: builds on 036's correctness fixes (must precede memoization), depends on 039.A4's *Big API (P8 is implementation strategy), implements 038's lazy EnumeratedSet runtime side via P6's iter.Seq. Nine benchmarks proposed (~80 LOC) — package currently has zero. Report at agents/040-combinatorics-perf.md, 246 lines.
041 | 2026-05-07 | compression-numerics | Numerical-correctness audit of compression/ surfaces one P0 documentation defect (CLAUDE.md+README.md+ARCHITECTURE.md all advertise Huffman & LZ77 but `grep -r 'Huffman\|LZ77' compression/` = zero matches; entropy.go's package doc correctly says only RLE/delta/quantize, so three top-level docs are out of sync; agents 042-400 read current CLAUDE.md and may design downstream code assuming these primitives exist) plus four S2 numerical defects in what *is* implemented: (1) plug-in `-Σ p log p` ships without Miller-Madow correction or even a docstring caveat, biases downward by (k−1)/(2N ln 2) bits — for Pistachio's 256-level/1024-sample embedding-quantize use case that's ~3% relative underestimate at typical H≈6 bits — package never sees raw counts so cannot apply MM/JS even if it wanted to, fix is additive `MillerMadowCorrection` + `EntropyFromCounts` companions ~25 LOC; (2) ConditionalEntropy = JointEntropy − ShannonEntropy(marginalX) uses the canonical-but-numerically-worst identity-form subtraction, loses ~6 decimal digits when joint is near-deterministic with off-diagonal ε~1e-7, recommend internal direct-form double-sum H(Y|X) = -Σ p(i,j) log2(p(i,j)/p_X(i)) keeping identity in the docstring ~12 LOC; (3) MutualInformation silently accepts ragged joint matrices and produces theoretically-meaningless numbers (computes maxCols via outer pass then sums what's there), no rectangular-shape validation; same bug in ConditionalEntropy; (4) DeltaEncode docstring claims "Precision: exact (lossless, assuming no int64 overflow in differences)" but the function actually wraps mod 2^64 and *because the inverse op also wraps* round-trip is preserved — semantics are correct but documented inaccurately, reword to "lossless via two's-complement wraparound; deltas are residues mod 2^64". Test-suite bug: TestKLDivergence_Asymmetric uses p=[0.9,0.1]/q=[0.1,0.9] which is the swap-mirror case so KL(P||Q)==KL(Q||P) by symmetry, then asserts equality and labels the test "Asymmetric" — actual asymmetry contract has zero coverage, swap to [0.5,0.5]/[0.25,0.75] (KL(P||Q)≈0.2075 vs KL(Q||P)≈0.1887). RLE encode-buffer pre-alloc is `len(data)` but comment says worst-case is 2x → forces append-grow on no-runs input, free perf fix. Golden-file coverage is the worst of any audited package so far: 1 file × 10 cases = 50% of CLAUDE.md's "min 20 per fn" floor for ShannonEntropy alone, zero golden files for the other 8 fns (Joint/Cond/MI/KL/CrossEntropy/RLE/Delta/Quantize), required floor is 200 vectors across 9 fns vs current 10 = 95% gap, recommend Bernoulli(p) sweep + 2&3-state Markov stationary entropy + truncated geometric for theoretical-source fixtures plus IEEE-754 edge vectors (+Inf/-Inf/NaN/-0.0/subnormal currently zero coverage). RLE max-run logic correct (splits at 255), `count=0` reachable on decode but never on encode (undocumented invariant). ScalarQuantize NaN-data corrupts output (min/max become NaN, bins all zero, dequant all NaN); +Inf-data corrupts dequant via min+0×Inf=NaN; document or reject. Bits-only throughout (hardcoded log2), no `*Nats` companions for ML-loss consumers — minor footgun but not a bug. Recommended commit ladder: P0 = three single-line edits to CLAUDE.md/README.md/ARCHITECTURE.md removing the Huffman/LZ77 claims (must ship before any other agent reads CLAUDE.md again — 5min fix); P1 = numerical-correctness items C2-C8 ~60 LOC + docstring updates; P2 = author 9 JSON files × 20-30 vectors ≈ 210 vectors; P3 = implement the missing Huffman/LZ77/LZ78 primitives ~600 LOC + golden as a separate sub-package issue (defer to topic 042). Topic-specified Huffman edge cases pre-resolved on paper for whoever picks up implementation: single-symbol→length-1 codeword convention (length-0 is mathematically optimal but unrepresentable), ties→stable lex tie-break for cross-language reproducibility, weight=0→exclude (Huffman undefined for p=0), all-equal-weights→verify L ∈ {⌈log2 n⌉, ⌈log2 n⌉−1} within Kraft slack; LZ77 start-of-stream→emit (0,0,lit) literals when window is empty. Report at agents/041-compression-numerics.md, 382 lines.
042 | 2026-05-07 | compression-missing | Canonical compression-primitive surface circa Brotli RFC 7932 (errata 2024) + Zstd RFC 8878 (ref-impl 1.5.7 Feb-2025) + JPEG XL ISO/IEC 18181-1:2022 (2024 amd) + AV2/AVM trunk Mar-2026 + Burtscher/Lindstrom/Lemire float-and-integer-compression literature 2009-2025 is ~80-90 named primitives; reality ships 3 (RLE, Delta, ScalarQuantize) = ~3-4% coverage, and CLAUDE.md/README.md/ARCHITECTURE.md lie about Huffman+LZ77 (041's P0 finding) — this report sizes the implementation work 041 deferred. Tier 1 = 20 primitives + 8 estimators / ~2,500 LOC strictly-additive: T1.1-T1.3 VarInt+ZigZag+ULEB128/SLEB128 (Protobuf/DWARF/Wasm), T1.4-T1.5 canonical Huffman + Larmore-Hirschberg 1990 length-limited package-merge tree builder (closes the CLAUDE.md lie, 370 LOC), T1.6-T1.8 LZ77+LZ78+LZW (Ziv-Lempel 1977/1978 + Welch 1984, 570 LOC, closes the second half of the lie), T1.9-T1.10 arithmetic coding (Witten-Neal-Cleary 1987 + Moffat-Neal-Witten 1998 carry-lazy) + rANS/tANS/uABS (Duda 2009/2013-2014 = the modern entropy coder used by zstd FSE / LZFSE / AV1 / JXL — 5x faster than arithmetic at same compression ratio, the rare compression primitive containing non-trivial mathematics), T1.11-T1.12 BWT + MTF (Burrows-Wheeler 1994 SRC + Bentley-Sleator-Tarjan-Wei 1986, SA-IS Nong-Zhang-Chan 2009 for O(n) suffix-array construction, 240 LOC), T1.13-T1.14 FrameOfReference + PFOR (Goldstein-Ramakrishnan-Shaft 1998 + Zukowski-Heman-Nes-Boncz 2006 MonetDB — Lucene/Elasticsearch/Snowflake/ClickHouse foundation), T1.15-T1.19 Elias gamma+delta+omega + Golomb-Rice + Exp-Golomb + Fibonacci + unary universal codes (Elias 1975 + Golomb 1966 + Rice-Plaunt 1971 + Apostolico-Fraenkel 1987 + Salomon textbook — used by FLAC/JPEG-LS/H.264/H.265/H.266/AV1 syntax-element coding, 210 LOC for all 7), T1.20 Gorilla XOR-delta float compression (Pelkonen-Franklin-Teller-Cavallaro-Huang-Meza-Veeraraghavan 2015 VLDB — the de-facto standard adopted by InfluxDB IOx, VictoriaMetrics, Prometheus 2.x, M3DB for f64 time-series, 200 LOC), plus 8 info-theoretic estimators T1.21-T1.28 Miller-Madow 1955 + EntropyFromCounts + KT-1981 + NSB-2002 + Hausser-Strimmer-2009 James-Stein shrinkage + LZ-76 complexity + Tcomplex Titchener-1998 + Cilibrasi-Vitanyi-2005 NCD (closes 041-C2's bias-correction gap and adds the compressor-as-similarity-measure that NCD requires); Tier 2 = 17 primitives / ~4,040 LOC representation-heavy or larger — LZSS+LZ4+Snappy+LZRW1, Adaptive Huffman Vitter-1987, Tunstall 1967, Subbotin-1999 carryless rangecoder (LZMA's), FSE Collet-2013 = zstd's specific tANS variant, FPC Burtscher-Ratanaworabhan-2009 + Pcodec mwlon/pcodec-2024 quantile-segmented float compression (~50% denser than Gorilla, the post-2020 algorithmic activity that the prompt explicitly names), DEFLATE RFC-1951, GroupVarInt Dean-2009-WSDM-keynote + StreamVByte Lemire-Kurz-Rupp-2017 + VByte/SQLite-varint, SimD-FastPFor/TurboPFor-v3 Lemire-Boytsov-2015, Roaring bitmaps Chambi-Lemire-Kaser-Godin-2016, bzip2 pipeline glue; Tier 3 = 19 primitives / ~16,500+ LOC out-of-scope-for-reality but documented for cites — Brotli + Zstd + LZMA + LZX + PPM/PPMd/PPMII + CM/PAQ/ZPAQ Mahoney + BSC + dictzip + ZFP Lindstrom-2014 + SZ/SZ3 Di-Cappello-2016/2018/2022 + TFloat + Akumuli + BinaryPacking4/8 + Simple-9/16 + JPEG-XL ANS-VarLenLZ77 + AV1/AV2 daala_ec + Crayford-Drysdale + JBIG2 MQ-coder + Sequitur + RePair (recommend documenting cites only — Brotli alone has 120 KB of literal English/HTML/JS substrings as static dict, Zstd ref-impl is 50,000 LOC C, beyond reality's <1k-LOC-per-file comfort zone — if callers need these, bind to ref-C-libs via separate lib package not reality/). Web research confirms no new entropy coder has entered canonical-must-ship cohort since rANS in 2013-2014 — the post-2020 activity is Pcodec-2024 (T2.10) + TurboPFor-v3-2024 (T2.15) + dictzip-zstd long-range mode 2.0 ref-impl 1.5.6 (T3.8) for genomic/log streams; foundational textbook list Salomon-Motta-2010 + Sayood-2017 + Mahoney-2012 stable. Cross-package consumers in reality: signal/ for T1.20 Gorilla on f64 sample compression and T1.13/T1.14 PFOR for FFT bin indices; graph/ for T1.13+T1.15 Elias on adjacency-list compression (WebGraph encoding Boldi-Vigna-2004); prob/ for T1.21-T1.25 estimator chain (recommend reality move estimators to prob/ rather than compression/ since Bayesian-PMF callers are the natural consumer); zero current internal consumers of compression/ means every Tier 1 addition is strictly additive zero-breaking-change risk. Recommended commit ladder M1-M12 ~3,570 LOC: P0 = M1 huffman.go + M2 lz77.go + M3 9 golden files closes 041's documentation lie (must ship before claiming Huffman/LZ77 in CLAUDE.md again); P1 = M4 varint family + M5 gorilla + M6 bitcoders + M7 bitio infrastructure ~920 LOC; P2 = M8 arithmetic+range coder + M9 rANS/FSE ~900 LOC; P3 = M10 BWT+MTF + M11 PFOR ~440 LOC; P4 = M12 estimators ~415 LOC; P5 = Tier 2 expansion ~2,000 LOC; P6 = Tier 3 documented-cites-only out-of-scope. Coverage delta if M1-M12 land: Tier 1 primitives 0/20 → 20/20 (100%), Tier 1 estimators 0/8 → 6/8 (75%, deferring NSB+Tcomplex), Tier 2 1/17 (LZSS as M2 sub-deliverable), package surface 3 → 30 named primitives = ~10x growth, golden-file coverage 1 file × 10 vectors → ~38 files × 25 vectors = ~950 vectors closing 041's P2 gap. Non-overlap with 041: that report covered fix-existing (numerical contracts on the 9 implemented fns + 4 S2 numerical defects + the P0 documentation lie + 95% golden-file gap), this report covers add-missing (the implementation work 041 explicitly deferred to topic 042 + a complete ~80-primitive canonical surface map indexed against Brotli/Zstd/JPEG-XL/AV2 2024-2026 specs and the Burtscher/Lindstrom/Lemire 2009-2025 float-and-integer-compression literature). Report at agents/042-compression-missing.md, 300 lines.
043 | 2026-05-07 | compression-sota | reality/compression scores 0/14 on the architecture/engineering axes the modern compression cohort (zstd 1.5.7, Brotli 1.1+CDT-2025, LZMA2/xz, bzip2, FPzip/ZFP/SZ3, Pcodec-0.4, Blosc2+OpenZL-2025, Roaring 1.3, Parquet v2.10/ORC 2.0, JPEG XL ISO 18181-1:2024, AV2/AVM Mar-2026, BtrBlocks-VLDB-2024) has converged on — (1) first-class filter pipeline, (2) self-describing frame header, (3) streaming Encoder/Decoder, (4) caller-supplied buffer + Bound(srcLen), (5) errors-not-panics, (6) offline dictionary training, (7) decoupled long-range matcher, (8) entropy table split from stream, (9) deterministic per-block parallelism, (10) BCJ-style arch-aware preprocess filters, (11) explicit lossy-tolerance contract (ABS/REL/PSNR/PW/NW), (12) Roaring-style hybrid integer-set container, (13) Blosc2-style codec-as-data dispatch, (14) golden-bytes format-stability commitment; eight require zero algorithm research. Single highest-leverage architectural commit is (1)+(2)+(8)+(11) fused: Filter interface + self-describing Frame{filters,codec,tolerance,payload} + Tolerance{Mode,Bound} value type, ~150 LOC, sets the engineering contract for every codec 042 wants to add — Pcodec is three Filters, ZFP three Filters, SZ3 three Filters, bzip2 four Filters, BCJ-x86/ARM64 one Filter each. Per-library engineering tricks reality-portable: zstd's offline-dictionary-via-frame-header (~30 LOC when LZ77 lands, RFC 8878 format-stability exemplar); LZMA's BCJ filters (the Filter interface generalization itself is reality's biggest lift); bzip2's pipeline-as-list-of-Filter (the cleanest small example, RLE refactor as first step); ZFP/SZ3's Tolerance type (settles 20-year API debate, ScalarQuantize immediately gets bound semantics); Pcodec's quantile-binning (~200 LOC, composes with prob/ quantile machinery); Blosc2's codec-as-data dispatch (50 LOC of frame header + dispatch now saves 5000 LOC refactor when 042 Tier-1 lands); Roaring's hybrid container with density-driven selector (pattern generalizes to linalg sparse storage); Parquet/BtrBlocks per-column codec metadata + cascading recursive pipelines (80 LOC tree-encoding once Filter exists); JPEG XL's per-context multi-distribution single-rANS-stream (150 LOC over vanilla rANS makes it shipping-grade). Skip-as-out-of-scope: Brotli's 120 KiB English n-gram static dictionary, multi-threaded encode (consumer's job not library's), daala_ec non-binary range coder (CPUs are cache-line-parallel not loop-step-parallel; cite for context). The deferred decision: ship Filter+Frame+Tolerance types on day 1, ship runtime codec dispatch when second codec lands — smallest change that lets v1 be both useful and forward-compatible. Report at agents/043-compression-sota.md, 389 lines.
044 | 2026-05-07 | compression-api | API-ergonomics audit of compression/'s 9 free-function user-facing surface (entropy.go 177 + coding.go 104 + quantize.go 99 LOC, zero exported types, zero error sentinels, zero constants) finds two incompatible API shapes wired into one namespace: entropy family (Shannon/Joint/Conditional/MI/KL/CrossEntropy) is float-in/float-out with silent-zero-on-degenerate, coding family ships THREE different buffer-ownership conventions across three pairs — RunLength{Encode,Decode} callee-allocs append-grown []byte, Delta{Encode,Decode} callee-allocs make([]int64,n), Scalar{Quantize,Dequantize} caller-allocs with silent truncation on undersized out via `if i >= len(out) { break }` (quantize.go:51,62,92), so CLAUDE.md's "no allocations in hot paths, functions accept output buffers" promise is honored only by 1 of 3 pairs. No *Bound(srcLen) companion (zstd/Brotli/LZ4/Snappy all ship one); RunLengthEncode pre-allocates len(data) capacity but docstring promises 2x worst case so random-byte input triggers ~10 reallocs at 1 KiB; DeltaEncode always returns fresh slice with no DeltaEncodeInPlace or out []int64 form so time-series consumers (Oracle/RubberDuck per CLAUDE.md) double-allocate. Error handling is pervasive silent-failure: 11 functions × 11 silent-on-bad-input behaviors, zero (T, error) returns, RunLengthDecode returns nil indistinguishably for empty-input (legit) and corrupted/truncated (catastrophic), KLDivergence(p,q) on length-mismatch returns 0 == KL(P,P), ScalarDequantize silently partial-writes with void return — flat violation of 043's axis-5 (errors-not-panics) and Go idiom. ZERO streaming surface — no io.Reader/io.Writer adapter, no Encoder.Reset(w), no frame boundary, no Decoder.Read(p) — every call consumes/produces complete buffer; worse off than audio (009: 2 streaming citizens DegradationTracker+Fingerprint, ~15 zero-alloc per-frame forms) since compression has zero stateful types of any kind, even though RLE state is ~16 bytes (currentValue,currentRunLen) and Delta state is 8 bytes (previousValue) — 25 LOC streaming RLE + 50 LOC RLEDecoder is trivially shippable. ZERO composition surface — no Filter interface, no Pipeline type, the canonical "RLE then Huffman" idiom unrepresentable; once 042's 30 codecs land every caller doing transform-then-entropy-code allocates twice with no way to declare pipeline as single value. ZERO framing — no magic bytes, no version, no checksum, no codec ID, append(rle1, rle2...) silently merges frames into a valid-looking but semantically-wrong third frame; primitives genuinely don't need framing (compress/flate doesn't, zlib adds 6 bytes, gzip adds 18+ bytes on top of zlib) but a compression/frame sub-package with Frame{Magic,Version,Codec,Payload,CRC32} is the canonical wrap and is also where codec composition lives via []Codec filter chain (BWT→MTF→RLE→Huffman bzip2 pipeline). Naming-convention split: RunLength{Encode,Decode} + Delta{Encode,Decode} use one verb pair, Scalar{Quantize,Dequantize} uses a different one, entropy family has no verb (nouns/measures); recommend documenting in package doc "lossless codecs use Encode/Decode; lossy codecs use the operation name (Quantize/Dequantize, Predict/Reconstruct, Transform/InverseTransform)" to rationalize without churn — gives 042's tier-1 (Huffman/LZ77/varint/arithmetic/rANS) a naming pattern. RunLength vs RLE: signal/'s FFT-not-FastFourierTransform precedent argues RLE is canonical-acronym so RLEEncode (9 chars) over RunLengthEncode (14 chars) follows repo style. Cross-package: signal/ uses caller-allocated out consistently (PowerSpectrum/Convolve/HannWindow), no streaming citizens but at least consistent (compression's three conventions are strictly worse than signal's one); audio/'s *Into companion pattern (SubtractSpectrum/SubtractSpectrumInto) is the template compression should adopt; testutil/ shows 041's 1-file × 10-cases = ~5% of "min 20/fn" floor is also a discoverability defect (new contributor sees one file for nine functions, concludes abandoned or this-is-the-convention — both wrong). Recommended ladder C1-C12 ~555 LOC strictly additive: C1 *Bound() companions ~15 LOC, C2 *Into() forms returning (n,err) ~80 LOC, C3 four error sentinels (ErrInvalidRLEStream/ErrTruncated/ErrShapeMismatch/ErrOutTooSmall) ~10 LOC, C4 RunLengthDecode validates count>=1 ~5, C5 MutualInformation/ConditionalEntropy reject ragged via ErrShapeMismatch ~12, C6 KL/CrossEntropy return error on length-mismatch ~8 (medium-risk signature change), C7 document buffer-ownership and verb conventions ~25, C8 Quantizer struct ~30, C9 RLEEncoder/Decoder io adapters ~80, C10 Delta same ~60, C11 compression/frame sub-package ~150, C12 Filter+Pipeline per 043 ~80; C1+C2+C3 = minimum cohesive bundle (fixes buffer-ownership inconsistency + no-Bound gap + silent-error gap in one wave), C9+C10 ship together for RLE/Delta parity, C11+C12 ship before 042's tier-1 codec wave to avoid retrofitting. Non-overlap with siblings: 041 (numerical correctness of implemented fns), 042 (missing primitives — Huffman/LZ77/varint/Gorilla/...), 043 (architectural axes — frame format, dictionary, parallelism, BCJ, Roaring, tolerance contracts), 044 = strictly call-site ergonomics. Report at agents/044-compression-api.md, 364 lines.
045 | 2026-05-07 | compression-perf | Runtime-cost lens orthogonal to 041/042/043/044: package has zero benchmarks (no `b.N` anywhere), zero `math/bits`/`encoding/binary`/`unsafe` imports, zero SIMD assembly, zero allocation discipline beyond ScalarQuantize (the only `*Into`-shaped fn). Five concrete perf items in shipped code, ten forward-looking for when 042 lands. P2 (highest impact, 1-line fix) — RunLengthEncode pre-allocates `make([]byte, 0, len(data))` but docstring/comment correctly say worst case is 2× len, so random/incompressible 1 MiB input triggers ~10-12 `runtime.growslice` reallocations and ~3 MiB of memmove copy-traffic; cap → `2*len(data)` gives ~4× speedup, eliminates 12 alloc → 1, no risk. P3 — RunLengthDecode pre-caps via line 47-52's total-walk pass (good) but the inner `for j := 0; j < count; j++ { decoded = append(decoded, val) }` is per-byte function-call through growslice fast path then 1-byte memmove; replace with exponential-doubling-copy memset (`copy(out[off+w:off+count], out[off:off+w])` doubling `w` per step, lowers to optimised `runtime/memmove_amd64.s`) gives ~10-12× on a 256-byte run, ~5-8× average — the same Snappy decoder trick. P4 — entropy family's `for _, p := range probs { if p > 0 { h -= p * math.Log2(p) } }` runs `math.Log2` (~25-40ns/call, Frexp+poly+Ldexp) at ~10µs/call on a 256-PMF and the `if p > 0` branch prevents AVX vectorisation entirely; ladder is current 1× → branchless via `math.Max(p, 1e-300)` 1.4× → `bits.LeadingZeros64`+poly-tail 3× → 256-entry LUT for byte-PMF case 12× (the right answer for Pistachio at 60fps with `logTable[i] = i*math.Log2(i/N)` materialized via `sync.Once`, ~50 LOC, the standard `compress/flate huffmanBitWriter.generate` trick). P6 — ScalarQuantize's `int(math.Round((v-min)/step))` is ~12ns/elem when `int((v-min)/step + 0.5)` is ~3ns (post-clamp values non-negative so trunc=floor), and the two-branch clamp can become Go 1.21 builtin `max(0, min(q, levels-1))` which inlines to `CMOVL` branchless (~1.3× pure-Go ceiling, ~3-4× hand-AVX2). P9 — ConditionalEntropy/MutualInformation allocate marginal scratch slices per call (~2 GiB/sec garbage on Pistachio MI loop with 1024 alphabet), needs output-buffer parameter or sync.Pool. Bit-IO completely absent — no BitWriter/BitReader/WriteBits/ReadBits, no `math/bits` import anywhere; this is the foundational primitive every entropy coder requires (Huffman, canonical-Huffman decode tables, arithmetic, rANS, Golomb-Rice/Elias/Exp-Golomb/Fibonacci, bit-packing/SIMDComp, ULEB128/Protobuf-varint), recommended 64-bit word + cursor + `bits.TrailingZeros64`/`LeadingZeros64` (~150 LOC, lowers to BSR/TZCNT/LZCNT on AMD64 and CLZ on ARM64) is the single most important missing primitive for compression perf — without it 042's Tier-1 codecs cannot ship at production speed. Table-driven decoder forward-looking (when 042 Huffman lands): zlib's two-level layout (9-bit primary 512×2B=1KiB fits in L1d × 16 cache lines + tail tables sized to longest code) is right default, canonical-Huffman over byte alphabets uses `[1024]uint32` packing `(sym uint16, codeLen uint8, _ uint8)` = 4KiB/64 cache-lines fitting entirely in L1d, table must be 64-byte cache-line-aligned (Go has no native align directive but package-level `var huffTable [1024]uint32` lands page-aligned in `.data` section per `compress/flate` precedent). SIMD-amenability table: DeltaEncode auto-vectorises in Go 1.21+ (~3-4× already attained), DeltaDecode does NOT (data dependency on cumulative sum) and ceiling is ~5× via 8-wide prefix-sum (Lemire-Boytsov 2015 SPE) requiring inline AVX2 asm out-of-scope for zero-dep repo, RLE encode early-exit `count<255` blocks vec, RLE decode per-byte append blocks vec, ShannonEntropy `Log2` blocks vec, ScalarQuantize partial via clamp branches. Streaming-vs-batch perf gap pre-empt for 044-C9/C10's RLEEncoder.Write streaming forms: naive Write-per-pair through underlying io.Writer is 100× slower than batch (~80ns/Write call overhead), MUST internal-buffer 4 KiB then chunk-flush — bufio.Writer discipline owned by encoder not caller. The diagnostic vacuum: **every speedup figure here is an estimate from disassembly intent and stdlib precedent, not a measurement** — without `compression_bench_test.go` (~150 LOC, 8 benchmarks across Encode/Decode/Entropy/Quantize with `b.ReportAllocs`) regression detection is impossible and Pareto choices like LUT-vs-Log2 cutoff (12× faster on byte alphabets, slower than Log2 at 65536 symbols when LUT blows L1d) are blind. Total perf headroom on shipped surface: ~3-5× RLE encode, ~5-10× RLE decode (long runs), ~6-15× entropy on small-alphabet PMFs, ~zero on Delta scalar (ceiling unmet). Recommended commit ladder P1-P12 ~705 LOC: P1 bench file (prerequisite, ~150), P2 RLE cap fix (1 line, ~4× free), P3 RLE decode memset (~10 LOC, ~5-10×), P4 branchless entropy (~25 LOC, ~1.3×), P5 entropy LUT (~40 LOC, ~12×), P6 ScalarQuantize fixes (~10 LOC, ~1.3×), P7 *Into forms (~80 via 044-C2), P8 BitWriter/BitReader infrastructure (~150 LOC, prerequisite for 042), P9 entropy scratch buffers (~30), P10 (in 042) Huffman two-level table (~100), P11 (in 042) prefix-sum DeltaDecode (~50), P12 streaming RLEEncoder buffered Write (~80 via 044-C9); P1+P2+P3 minimum cohesive bundle (~30 min work, ~5× wins on named hot consumers Pistachio+Oracle), P8 gates 042's tier-1 codec work. Non-overlap: 041 (numerical correctness), 042 (missing primitives), 043 (architectural axes), 044 (call-site ergonomics), 045 = strictly runtime cost. Report at agents/045-compression-perf.md, 354 lines.
046 | 2026-05-07 | constants-numerics | constants/ shipped values are NIST CODATA 2018 + SI 2019 exact (h,e,k_B,N_A,c,g_n,atm); CODATA 2022 (Mohr/Newell/Taylor/Tiesinga, RMP 97 025002, preprint arXiv 2409.03787, NIST allascii.txt fetched 2026-05-07) moved 2 of 3 non-exact shipped constants — VacuumPermittivity 8.8541878128e-12 -> **8.8541878188e-12** F/m (~6e-10 rel, ~7 ulp), VacuumPermeability 1.25663706212e-6 -> **1.25663706127e-6** H/m (~7e-10 rel, ~7 ulp), GravitationalConst 6.67430e-11 unchanged at 5-digit precision (u_r still ~2.2e-5); not shipped but moved: fine-structure alpha 7.2973525693e-3 -> 7.2973525643e-3 (1/alpha 137.035999084 -> 137.035999177), electron mass 9.1093837015e-31 -> 9.1093837139e-31, proton mass 1.67262192369e-27 -> 1.67262192595e-27, atomic mass constant 1.66053906660e-27 -> 1.66053906892e-27, Rydberg 10973731.568160 -> 10973731.568157; the 2019 SI redefinition four (h,e,k_B,N_A) plus c by definition cannot move in CODATA 2022 or any future cycle, **but** the docstring 'exact' is misleading — only c=299792458 and integer-defined constants are exactly representable in float64; h=6.62607015e-34, k_B=1.380649e-23, N_A=6.02214076e23, e=1.602176634e-19 are exact-by-decimal-definition but float64-nearest (the literal IS the value, cross-language goldens must validate against the literal not against arbitrary-precision derived); StefanBoltzmann shipped as literal 5.670374419e-8 differs from float64-derived 2*pi^5*k_B^4/(15*h^3*c^2)=5.6703744191844e-8 by 1.84e-18 abs / 3.3e-10 rel — derivation-vs-literal inconsistency where docstring says 'derived' but code is hardcoded; PlanckReduced and GasConstant correctly use Go const arithmetic (compile-time = runtime float64 reproducible). Round-trip exactness (verified empirically): cm/m, ft/m, lb/kg, yd/m, NM/m **all bit-exact** for tested x in {1,5,100,1e6}; in/m **fails for x=12** (12 -> 0.30479999999999996 -> 11.999999999999998, ulp drift) but exact for {1,100,1000}; unit-coherence stack is bit-exact (5280*MetersPerFoot == MetersPerMile, 3*MetersPerFoot == MetersPerYard, MetersPerFoot/12 == MetersPerInch, KgPerPound/16 == KgPerOunce — all four equal at the literal-bit level, well done; existing tests at constants_test.go:241-260 use 1e-10 tolerance but actual property is tolerance=0); PascalsPerPSI=6894.757293168361 differs by 1 ulp (9e-13 abs) from float64-derived lbf/inch^2=6894.7572931683617; Fahrenheit-Kelvin has unfixable ~3e-13 K rounding floor because 5/9 unrepresentable ((32+459.67)*5/9 = 273.14999999999997726 not 273.15). Defining-constant gaps: ΔνCs=9192631770 Hz and K_cd=683 lm/W (the other two of the seven SI-2019 defining constants, both integer-exact) NOT shipped; alpha not shipped despite downstream em/ using coulombConst=1/(4*pi*ε₀); zero astronomical constants (AU=149597870700 m exact IAU2012, ly=9460730472580800 m exact, parsec, IAU 2015 nominal GM_sun=1.32712440041e20 — IAU 2015 ships GM products not separate G*M because GM measured to 1e-9 while G to 2.2e-5); zero gallon/ton/eV/BTU/cal/slug/knot units (US-vs-Imperial gallon trap unmentioned — 3.785 vs 4.546 L, 20% off; US vs Imp fl oz also off ~4% in opposite direction); zero Julian year (31557600 s exact), zero sidereal/tropical/Gregorian year, zero machine-readable uncertainty metadata, zero CODATA vintage marker. Time: SecondsPerDay=86400 correct for TAI/TT/UT1 mean solar but UTC days have 86400±1 on 27 historical leap seconds (none since 2017-01-01 through 2026-05; CGPM 2022 resolved to retire leap seconds by 2035, possible negative leap by 2029 as Earth rotation has been speeding up); docstring 'mean solar day' technically correct but UTC consumer will silently drift. Math constants: all 10 (Pi/E/Phi/Sqrt2/Sqrt3/Ln2/Ln10/Log2E/Log10E/EulerGamma) bit-exact-equal to math.X stdlib (where it exists); 7 are aliases via 'const X = math.X', 3 are correctly-rounded literals (Phi=1.618033988749895, Sqrt3=1.7320508075688772, EulerGamma=0.5772156649015329 — all verified correctly-rounded float64 nearest); missing TwoPi, HalfPi, Sqrt2Pi (used in every Gaussian PDF in prob/), InvSqrt2Pi, SqrtPi, Catalan, Apery ζ(3). Tolerance-contract violation: golden file has tolerance:0 for ε₀, μ₀ which becomes immediately false on CODATA 2022 update (the literal IS the test value — cross-language regen mandatory). 17 action items C-2022-1 through C-TOL-1 covering CODATA-2022 update (10 LOC + golden regen), CODATAVintage marker (30 LOC), missing defining-constants ΔνCs/K_cd (6 LOC), StefanBoltzmann derived-vs-literal reconciliation (10 LOC), Uncertainty companion struct (40 LOC), bit-exact unit-coherence test tightening (5 LOC), gal/ton/eV/AU/ly fill-in (30 LOC), IAU 2015 nominal astronomical set with GM products (25 LOC), TwoPi/HalfPi/Sqrt2Pi math additions (15 LOC), Pin Phi/Sqrt3/EulerGamma to correctly-rounded literals (5 LOC); highest-value bundle = C-2022-1+C-EXACT-2+C-MISSING-1+C-ASTRO-1+C-MATH-1 ≈ 85 LOC additive that brings package current to 2026-05 published standards. Non-overlap: this report is solely numerical correctness vs published constants standards; golden-file architecture / API ergonomics / missing primitive surface are sibling slots. Report at agents/046-constants-numerics.md, 222 lines.

multiplying loses 4-5 orders precision], GMEarthNominal=3.986004e14, GMJupiterNominal=1.2668653e17, H0Planck2018=67.4 km/s/Mpc + H0SH0ES2022=73.04 [vintage-tracked, 5sigma Hubble tension], CMBTemperature=2.7255 K [Fixsen 2009 ApJ 707 916], CriticalDensity=9.47e-27 kg/m^3, SecondsPerJulianYear=31557600.0 [defines ly], SecondsPerGregorianYear=31556952.0 integer exact, SecondsPerTropicalYear=31556925.2, SecondsPerSiderealYear=31558149.5, SecondsPerSiderealDay=86164.0905, JulianDateJ2000=2451545.0);
