volume gallon-trap [noted by 046]: LitersPerGallonUS=3.785411784 [=231 in^3 exact] vs LitersPerGallonImperial=4.54609 [UK W&M Act 1985 exact, ~20% larger], LitersPerFluidOunceUS=0.0295735295625 vs LitersPerFluidOunceImperial=0.0284130625 [~4% smaller, opposite-direction trap], LitersPerCubicInch=0.016387064, LitersPerCubicFoot=28.316846592, LitersPerBarrelOil=158.987294928 [42 US gal]; mass: KgPerShortTon=907.18474 [US 2000 lb] + KgPerLongTon=1016.0469088 [UK 2240 lb] + KgPerTonne=1000.0 + KgPerStone=6.35029318 + KgPerGrain=6.479891e-5 [pharmacy/ammo lb/7000] + KgPerCarat=2e-4; pressure: PascalsPerMillibar=100.0 + PascalsPerMmHg=133.322387415 vs PascalsPerTorr=101325/760 [differ by rounding] + PascalsPerInHg=3386.389 + PascalsPerKsi=6894757.293168361; speed: MetersPerSecondPerKnot=0.5144444444444444 [NM/h=1852/3600 exact recurring] + MetersPerSecondPerMph=0.44704 + MetersPerSecondPerKmh=1/3.6 recurring); 1.9 SI prefix table 30 LOC (Quetta=1e30 + Ronna=1e27 + Yotta...Yocto + Ronto=1e-27 + Quecto=1e-30 — CGPM 2022 added quetta/ronna/quecto/ronto, first new SI prefixes since 1991, Reality being 2026 package should ship them; plus IEC 60027-2 binary Kibi...Yobi). Tier 2 ~80 LOC: 2.1 Planck units 20 LOC (4 foundational PlanckLength/Mass/Time/Temperature + 9 derived PlanckEnergy=1.956082e9 J ~1.22e19 GeV, PlanckCharge=1.875546e-18 C, PlanckMomentum=6.524786, PlanckForce=1.210256e44, PlanckPower=3.628254e52, PlanckDensity=5.155500e96, PlanckPressure=4.633090e113, PlanckArea=2.612270e-70, PlanckVolume=4.222111e-105, all const-arithmetic derivable analogous to existing PlanckReduced); 2.2 Stoney 6 LOC (older than Planck — Stoney 1881 vs Planck 1899 — normalises G/c/e/4pi*eps0; StoneyLength=1.380681e-36 [smaller than Planck length by sqrt(alpha)], StoneyMass=1.859210e-9, StoneyTime=4.605375e-45, StoneyEnergy=1.671099e8); 2.3 geometrized GR 6 LOC (G=c=1; GeometrizedMassToMeters=G/c^2~7.4256e-28 m/kg [M_sun -> 1.477 km = R_Schwarzschild/2], GeometrizedTimeToMeters=c, GeometrizedEnergyToMeters=G/c^4~8.262e-45); 2.4 Heaviside-Lorentz/CGS 10 LOC (StatCoulombsPerCoulomb=2.99792458e9 [Gaussian 10*c numerically], GaussPerTesla=1e4 EMU integer exact, OerstedPerAmpPerMeter=4pi/1000, MaxwellPerWeber=1e8 integer exact); 2.5 Tau=2pi+PiCubed/4th/5th+Sqrt5 6 LOC; 2.6 SM couplings + boson masses 12 LOC vintage-tracked (WeakCouplingFermi G_F=1.1663787e-5 GeV^-2, WeakMixingAngleSinSq sin^2(theta_W)=0.23121 CODATA 2022, StrongCouplingMz alpha_s(M_Z)=0.1180 PDG 2024, HiggsVacuumExpectation v=246.219651 GeV, WBosonMass=80.3692 GeV/c^2 [CDF 2022/PDG average], ZBosonMass=91.1880, HiggsBosonMass=125.20, TopQuarkMass=172.69); 2.7 time/epoch 10 LOC (JulianDateUnixEpoch=2440587.5, MJDOffset=2400000.5, B1950Epoch=2433282.4235, GregorianYearDays=365.2425, JulianYearDays=365.25, TropicalYearDays=365.24219, SiderealYearDays=365.25636, AnomalisticYearDays=365.259636 [perihelion return], DraconicYearDays=346.620075883 [eclipse year]). Tier 3 ~30 LOC: 3.1 LoschmidtConstant=2.686780111e25+MolarVolumeIdealGas=22.41396954e-3 IUPAC STP since 1982 + legacy 22.413962e-3 + SackurTetrodeConstant=-1.16487052149 + MolarMassCarbon12=12.0000000e-3 + MolarPlanck=N_A*h~3.990312712e-10 (10 LOC); 3.2 PaperFoldingConstant=0.8507361882 + RamanujanSoldner mu=1.4513692348833810502839 + Plastic rho=1.3247179572447460260 [silver-ratio cousin x^3=x+1] (6 LOC); 3.3 Conway=1.303577269034296 [topic-prompt requested, look-and-say limit ratio] + Chaitin's Omega documented as deliberate-omission-uncomputable (4 LOC); 3.4 specialty units MetersPerAngstrom=1e-10+MetersPerFermi=1e-15+MetersPerMicron+MetersPerSurveyFoot=1200/3937 [US survey foot deprecated 2023 but in legacy GIS, differs from international foot by 2 ppm], RadiansPerArcMinute+ArcSecond+RadiansPerMilArmy=2pi/6400 NATO+RadiansPerMilUSSR=2pi/6000 Warsaw Pact+RadiansPerMilDanish=2pi/6300 (12 LOC). Grand total Tier 1+2+3 = ~373 LOC strictly additive, fits existing math.go/physics.go/units.go plus 2 new files (atomic.go for 1.4, astro.go for 1.7). Highest-value PR bundle = Tiers 1.1-1.9+2.1 ~= ~290 LOC, lifts package from ~14% -> ~75% of scipy.constants surface plus foundational Planck/Hartree blocks; critical single-line wins FineStructure+BohrRadius+RydbergConstant+BohrMagneton+ElectronMass+ProtonMass+MetersPerAU+MetersPerLightYear+PlanckLength+PlanckMass+JoulesPerEv+LitersPerGallonUS. Unit-system gaps table: Reality squarely in scipy.constants-style camp (a) constants-only not (b) Boost.Units/Frink/Wolfram Quantity[] type-tagged framework — the design pivot belongs in a separate units/ package proposal, out of scope for constants/. Cross-system conversion factors as scalar constants remain in scope and are covered in 1.4/2.1/2.2/2.3/2.4/2.5/2.6. Non-overlap: 046 covered numerical correctness of *shipped* values (CODATA 2022 deltas to eps0/mu0, ULP audits, round-trip exactness, derivation-vs-literal inconsistencies); this report is strictly missing-surface enumeration. Report at agents/047-constants-missing.md, 399 lines.
048 | 2026-05-07 | constants-sota | SOTA library/standard comparison disjoint from 046 (numerics) / 047 (missing list): catalog-format and engineering-choice axes across NIST CODATA allascii.txt + scipy.constants physical_constants dict + Boost.Units type-templated codata namespace + mpmath lazy-precision + sympy.physics.units symbolic Quantity + siunitx LaTeX paren-form + ucum unit-string grammar + F# units-of-measure type-erased + nalgebra-physics Rust const-fn + Frink/GNU units text catalogs + Wolfram Quantity[]. Reality places worst-tier in the metadata-hygiene matrix: alone among 11 SOTA references in storing vintage/uncertainty/unit/source as free-form prose comments rather than structured data. Five concrete zero-dep borrows ranked: (1) scipy-style parallel `Meta map[string]ConstantMeta` keyed by name with Value/StdUnc/Unit/Vintage/Source/Notation fields beside hot-path consts ~80 LOC zero risk additive, (2) Boost-style `const CODATAVintage = "2022"` block + SIRedefinition/ISO80000/IAU/YardPound vintage consts ~6 LOC, (3) ucum canonical unit strings (m.s-1 not m/s) in comments and Meta.Unit ~0 LOC edits, (4) siunitx CODATA compact-paren Notation field 6.67430(15)e-11 matches NIST allascii display ~30 LOC, (5) mpmath-style expected_high_precision Decimal-string in goldens for cross-language Python validation ~30 LOC. Highest-value bundle 1+2+3 ~95 LOC lifts Reality from worst-tier-prose to par-with-scipy.constants on doc discipline. Boost.Units type-encoded dimensional analysis correctly declined — Go type system cannot express it cheaply, belongs in hypothetical units/ package not constants/. F# units-of-measure is closest Go-feasible cousin but still requires type-level natural-number arithmetic Go lacks; named-type wrappers (Meters/Newtons) handle identity but not algebra. Recent-SI engineering implications noted: CGPM 2022 retire-leap-seconds-by-2035 affects future time/ package design (mirror IAU SOFA TimeScale enum); CGPM 2022 quetta/ronna/quecto/ronto must ship in 047's Tier-1.9 prefix table; IAU 2018 B5 seven-time-scale taxonomy noted for future astro/. Engineering-choices ranking: Boost.Units > F# UoM > nalgebra-physics > Reality > scipy.constants on encoding elegance; doc-discipline ranking: NIST allascii > scipy.constants > sympy > Boost.Units > Reality. What Reality is doing right (don't change): const float64 hot-path matches all perf-targeted SOTA; const-arithmetic for derived-exact-from-exact (PlanckReduced) matches Boost; math.Pi aliasing matches scipy numpy.pi reuse; free-text source citations are best-in-class for 200-LOC zero-dep package. The metadata gap is *additional* structure not a *replacement*. Report at agents/048-constants-sota.md, 352 lines.
049 | 2026-05-07 | constants-api | Strictly disjoint from 046 (numerics) / 047 (missing surface) / 048 (metadata-encoding peers) -- only call-site ergonomics: naming, direction, grouping, unit-doc, intermediate-factor exposure, stdlib re-export, conversion-helper shape. Three concrete API bugs surfaced: (1) constants.Pi is dead code -- 2 occurrences in 1 file (pkg/canonical/canonical.go:42-43 the name-resolver) vs 169 occurrences of math.Pi in 50 files, the entire 7-alias layer (Pi/E/Sqrt2/Ln2/Ln10/Log2E/Log10E) carries near-zero traffic and the "single source of truth" doc claim at math.go:5-7 is false in practice, the project does not use its own constants; (2) color/difference.go:130-136 reimplements private deg2rad/rad2deg with 7 call sites in CIEDE2000 instead of using shipped DegreesToRadians/RadiansToDegrees -- bare constant-multiply at call site lacks the named-function self-documentation that the local code preferred; (3) the temperature surface is half-built -- CelsiusToKelvin is an additive offset (not multiplicative factor) sharing exact same name-shape as MetersPerFoot creating a type-system trap (a naive consumer multiplies and gets garbage), Fahrenheit conversion needs two constants (FahrenheitToKelvinOffset=459.67 + FahrenheitToKelvinScale=5/9) and there is NO KelvinFromFahrenheit function hiding the algebra -- the only conversion that algebraically benefits from a function form. Naming-direction audit: package documents <Target>Per<Source> convention at units.go:6-9 but ships 3 schemata simultaneously -- 13 constants follow it (MetersPerFoot, KgPerPound, PascalsPerAtm, SecondsPerHour); 2 constants use <Source>To<Target> alternative (RadiansToDegrees, DegreesToRadians) which is same semantic as Per but different preposition; 3 constants use <Source>To<Target>Offset/Scale for affine maps (CelsiusToKelvin, FahrenheitToKelvinOffset, FahrenheitToKelvinScale). scipy.constants/Frink/GNU-units use opposite direction (source-named, value-in-SI: scipy.constants.foot=0.3048 means "1 foot is 0.3048m") -- both forms have same call-site shape, Reality choice is defensible; FootInMeters is a distinct third option (genitive "in") that nobody uses because "in" loses directional cue "Per" carries. Grouping: 31 names in 1 flat package (math.go 10 + physics.go 13 + units.go 18); scipy ships 6 thematic sub-namespaces -- flat works for 31 but creates discoverability friction at 047 Tier-1 ~180-220 surface; recommend either godoc-rendered section-header doc-comments or sub-package split (constants/math, constants/physics, ...) only when surface justifies. Units-in-doc audit: physics.go has trailing "// unit" comment on all 13 constants (m/s, J*s, J/K, mol^-1, F/m, H/m, W m^-2 K^-4, etc.) -- best-practice matches NIST allascii; math.go has no "// dimensionless" annotation on Phi/Sqrt2/Sqrt3/Ln2/Ln10/Log2E/Log10E/EulerGamma; units.go has no annotation on the conversion factors themselves (MetersPerFoot=0.3048 is in m/ft units of dimensional ratio). Notation inconsistency physics.go: m/s slash form (lines 16,77) vs m^-1 / mol^-1 / m^-2 K^-4 caret-negative form (lines 35,65,71) -- recommend standardize on NIST allascii (no slashes for compound). Intermediate factors NOT exposed: c^2=8.987551787e16 m^2/s^2 (E=mc^2), 1/c^2 (relativistic energy), 4*pi=12.566370614, 2*pi=6.283185307, pi^2, 1/(4*pi*eps0) [em/em.go:21 computes privately as coulombConst -- extract per 047 Tier-1.6 CoulombConstant], mu_0/(4*pi), Z_0=sqrt(mu_0/eps_0)=376.73 ohm vacuum impedance -- package exposes definitional layer (c, h, k_B, eps_0) and one derived-exact layer (PlanckReduced, GasConstant) but not call-site-shorthand layer; principle inconsistent -- GasConstant=Avogadro*Boltzmann exposed but c^2 not despite both being single-multiply, recommend explicit policy "expose if appears in scipy/NIST tables under own name OR has named symbol in literature". Stdlib re-export verdict: 7 of 10 math.go consts are aliases via "const X = math.X" (Pi/E/Sqrt2/Ln2/Ln10/Log2E/Log10E), 3 are correctly-rounded literals stdlib lacks (Phi/Sqrt3/EulerGamma); aliases are dead code per usage data -- recommend either drop the 7 aliases (-7 LOC, update 1 importer pkg/canonical) or enforce 50-file migration to constants.Pi (mechanical, only justified if 047 Tier-1.1 ships TwoPi/Sqrt2Pi/InvSqrt2Pi which require constants/ imports anyway). HIGHEST-LEVERAGE single change: API-11A add constants/convert.go ~80 LOC with ~25 pure functions in <Target>From<Source>(value) shape -- KelvinFromCelsius(c)=c+CelsiusToKelvin, KelvinFromFahrenheit(f)=(f+offset)*scale [hides only non-trivial algebra in package], CelsiusFromFahrenheit(f)=(f-32)*5/9, RadiansFromDegrees/DegreesFromRadians, MetersFromFeet/Inches/Miles/Yards/NauticalMiles + reverses, KilogramsFromPounds/Ounces + reverses, PascalsFromAtm/Bar/PSI + reverses, SecondsFromMinutes/Hours/Days; <T>From<S> shape canonical Go reads left-to-right matching assignment (k := KelvinFromCelsius(c)); composes with golden-file infrastructure ~10 vectors per function; eliminates 7 inline recomputes in color/difference.go; no new types, no allocation, fits CLAUDE.md "numbers in, numbers out". Convert(val, fromUnit, toUnit) runtime form (scipy convert_temperature shape) considered and rejected -- string-keyed runtime errors, 200 LOC vs 80, error-handling burden, belongs in hypothetical units/ runtime not constants/. Type system: Boost.Units / F# UoM dimensional types correctly out-of-scope (Go cannot express type-level natural-number arithmetic for dimensional algebra; only feasible mid-point is named-type wrappers "type Meters float64" ~200 LOC ceremony for identity-only checking, separate design proposal). Other call-site issues: AtmPressure (physics.go) and PascalsPerAtm (units.go) are same value 101325.0 under two names, constants_test.go:265-268 even pins them equal -- discoverability foot-gun, deprecate one; StandardGravity belongs in units.go alongside g_n=9.80665 ISO-80000-3 defined unit not in physics.go; package has zero godoc Example functions especially missing for Fahrenheit case where inline arithmetic is non-obvious. Highest-value bundle: API-11A (80 LOC convert.go) + API-1B (alias DegreesPerRadian=RadiansToDegrees etc for direction symmetry, 6 LOC, non-breaking) + API-10A (drop 7 stdlib-alias dead code, -7 LOC) + API-13 (deduplicate AtmPressure/PascalsPerAtm, ~5 LOC) = ~95 LOC additive plus 1 deletion, fixes 3 concrete bugs while preserving numbers-in-numbers-out CLAUDE.md rule. Disjoint-check appendix in report verifies zero topical overlap with 046/047/048. Report at agents/049-constants-api.md, 396 lines.
050 | 2026-05-07 | constants-perf | constants/ is at the internal perf optimum — 31 user-facing names are 100% const, 0% var, 0 funcs, 0 init, 0 allocations possible by construction; 5 derived constants (PlanckReduced, GasConstant, FahrenheitToKelvinScale, RadiansToDegrees, DegreesToRadians) use Go const-arithmetic which the compiler folds at full-precision big.Float and rounds once to float64 at assignment, then inlines into call sites identically to a literal (cross-build deterministic by Go spec, more reproducible than runtime computation in Python which rounds twice); on amd64 each named const becomes an immediate-operand to the consuming MULSD/ADDSD with zero memory load, no .rodata symbol, no addressable global; aliases via "const X = math.X" (Pi/E/Sqrt2/Ln2/Ln10/Log2E/Log10E) are perf-neutral dead code (049 owns API verdict, perf says no action); the only meaningful perf lever is *external* — three patterns recompute derivable values per-call across the codebase: 2*math.Pi (40+ sites in audio/signal/acoustics), 1/math.Sqrt(2*math.Pi) (every Gaussian PDF in prob/, ~30-40 ns recomputed per call: one sqrt+one fdiv vs one fmul of precomputed const), and 1/(4*pi*eps0) which em/em.go:21 already locally hoists as private const coulombConst (right pattern — local const folds same as package const, wins are single-source-of-truth not cycles); info/mdl/codelength.go:46 also locally hoists const logTwoPi=1.8378770664093454836 proving the recurring-expression problem is real and self-acknowledged in the codebase; ship 047 Tier-1.1 + 1.6 derived constants (TwoPi=6.283185307179586, HalfPi, Sqrt2Pi=2.5066282746310002, InvSqrt2Pi=0.3989422804014327, LnSqrt2Pi, CoulombConstant=8.9875517873681764e9, VacuumImpedance=376.730313412, SpeedOfLightSquared=8.987551787e16) ~10 LOC strictly additive saves 10-50 ns per affected hot-path call (Gaussian PDF in prob/, Coulomb force in em/, sinusoid generation in audio/signal/acoustics) — at Pistachio's 60 FPS frame budget this is microseconds per frame per call site; conversion-function audit on 049's proposed convert.go finds Go mid-stack inliner readily inlines single-expression KelvinFromCelsius-style helpers (cost ~5 nodes vs default 80-budget), zero allocations, zero escape, identical codegen to inline arithmetic — the ergonomics proposal is performance-neutral and should add Benchmark*/AllocsPerOp==0 assertions to pin the inlining contract against future regression (someone adding error returns or logging would silently break inlining); preventive CI rule rejecting var at file scope in constants/ ~10 LOC protects against accidental const→var conversion which would break inlining everywhere; expression-rewrite catalogue: math.Sin(2*math.Pi*f*t) → math.Sin(constants.TwoPi*f*t) saves 1 fmul at compile time per call across audio/signal/acoustics; mechanical rename ~50 LOC once TwoPi ships; no benchmark file exists for constants/ today and none is warranted because there is no executable code to benchmark — benchmarks become justified only if convert.go lands; total perf-side action items P-HOIST-1+P-VAR-1+P-CONV-1+P-CODEGEN-1+P-HOIST-2 = ~125 LOC strictly additive, of which ~10 LOC of new named constants is the only one producing measurable runtime wins; disjoint-check appendix verifies zero topical overlap with 046 (numerics) / 047 (missing surface) / 048 (SOTA peers) / 049 (API ergonomics). Report at agents/050-constants-perf.md, ~310 lines.

052 | 2026-05-07 | control-missing | control/ ships 8 primitives (PID + 4 filters + scalar TF Evaluate/Poles/IsStable) vs python-control 0.10's ~250 callable functions / Modelica.Blocks.Continuous / Slycot / Drake reference surface — entire subdiscipline missing across 8 axes; Tier-1 (table-stakes / ~2750 LOC across 11 bundles) covers Bode+Nyquist+DCGain+rolloff+GainMargin+PhaseMargin+DelayMargin (~250 LOC, no new dep — the bare minimum for any TF library), Tustin+ForwardEuler+BackwardEuler+DiscreteTransferFunction (~180), StateSpace{A,B,C,D}+tf2ss+ss2tf+Controllable+Observable (~230, no controllability/observability matrices today, no canonical realization), linalg prereqs Schur(~300)+expm Padé-13(~150)+SolveSylvester(~120)+SolveContinuousLyapunov-Bartels-Stewart(~150)+SolveDiscreteLyapunov(~120)+SolveContinuousARE-Laub(~180) — expm is the single most load-bearing missing primitive across the entire codebase (gates c2d, Kalman propagation, ZOH, local linearization), then ZOH+c2d selectors+Padé-delay (~140), LQRContinuous(~180)+LQRDiscrete(~180)+LQ-output-regulator(~80), Kalman family — Discrete(~100)+Continuous(~120)+Square-root Bierman-Thornton UDU' the numerically-stable one(~200)+EKF(~120)+UKF(~200) — note the standard form Kalman ships in linalg-style codebases is *unstable* in finite precision over many updates as P loses positive-definiteness so square-root variant is the one to ship by default, StepResponse+ImpulseResponse+RootLocus(~180), PlacePoles-Ackermann-SISO+Luenberger-observer+LQG-LTR(~230), Lead+Lag+LeadLag+Notch compensator design(~80), PID auto-tuning Z-N open-loop+Z-N closed-loop+Cohen-Coon+AMIGO+Skogestad-SIMC+Astrom-Hagglund-relay-feedback(~300); Tier-2 ~5050 LOC across 7 bundles covers PRBS+ARX+OE+ARMAX+Box-Jenkins+ETFE+persistent-excitation-check (~220 sysID basics), optim/QP-OSQP-ADMM(~400)+MPCQP{Q,R,N}-condensed (~150) — note Reality's optim/ has L-BFGS/SA/GA but ZERO QP solver so MPC and CBF safety filters are blocked at the optim/ level, SlidingMode-1st-order+boundary-layer+super-twisting-Levant(~150)+Lyapunov-checker(~80)+CLF-Sontag-universal-formula(~50)+CBF-QP-safety-filter(~80, requires QP), IMC-Skogestad(~80)+H∞-DGKF-two-Riccati-Glover-Doyle-1989(~500)+H∞-LoopShape-S/KS-S/T-mixed-sensitivity(~400)+Glover-McFarlane-normalized-coprime-factor(~250), μ-synthesis-D-K-iteration(~600)+structured-singular-value-upper-bound(~300)+LMI-mini-solver-S-procedure-Lyapunov-LMI(~500), MRAC-MIT-rule(~80)+RLS-with-forgetting(~60)+STR-indirect(~150)+STR-direct-MV-Astrom-Wittenmark(~120)+GainScheduling-piecewise-linear-interpolation(~80), N4SID-Van-Overschee-De-Moor(~400)+MOESP(~400)+CVA-canonical-variate-analysis(~400)+ERA-Juang-Pappa-Hankel-SVD(~150)+ETFE-Welch(~80) subspace ID, IDA-PBC-Ortega-van-der-Schaft-Maschke-Escobar-2001-port-Hamiltonian(~250)+Backstepping-strict-feedback(~200)+Feedback-linearization-input-output-relative-degree(~150)+Passivity-Based-Control-Euler-Lagrange(~150)+ISS-gain-margin+small-gain-theorem(~100), Balanced-realization-Moore-1981(~150)+Balanced-truncation(~80)+Hankel-norm-reduction-Glover-1984(~250) model reduction; Tier-3 ~3500 LOC pseudo-spectral-abscissa, distance-to-instability, exact-μ-branch-bound, fractional-PI-lambda-Podlubny, Smith-predictor, repetitive-control, ILC-iterative-learning-control, differential-flatness-flat-output-trajectory-generation, trajectory-optimization-direct-collocation-multiple-shooting, reachability-Hamilton-Jacobi-PDE-Mitchell-level-set, zonotope-interval-arithmetic-reach-sets, fixed-point-PID; reality's PID is missing 7 standard PID features (back-calculation Kt, conditional integration, derivative-on-measurement, derivative-filter Astrom-Murray-§10.5-essential, setpoint weighting b/c, 2-of-DOF, anti-bumpless-transfer); the named topic items map: LQR=L6, LQG=L6+L7, Kalman=L7, MPC=L13, H∞=L15, μ=L16, IDA-PBC=L19, sliding-mode=L14, gain-scheduling=L17. Highest-value first PR (L1+L2+L3+L8+L10) = ~920 LOC no new linalg dep transforms control/ from "PID + scalar TF eval" to "I can design a classical SISO controller end-to-end" (closes Ogata Ch. 5-7 / Astrom-Murray Ch. 9-10 ~60% of any undergrad control textbook). Web research surfaced python-control 0.10.x is the canonical reference (LQR/LQG, Bode/Nyquist/margins, c2d, Riccati via Slycot/SLICOT-Fortran-77; algorithmic API stable since ~0.8/2018; recent work flatsys differential flatness + phaseplot ergonomics — Reality is ~7 years behind on core surface), Slycot wraps Fortran SLICOT for ARE/Lyapunov/balanced-reduction at scale, harold is closest pure-Python design analog Reality should consider, CasADi 3.6 + do-mpc 4.6 are the modern NMPC stack and Reality already has autodiff/ for the symbolic/AD layer, Modelica.Blocks.Continuous is the block-naming reference for L1-L10 surface, Drake/Tedrake/MIT-2025 has SOS-Lyapunov + contact-implicit MPC for Tier-3 territory. Non-overlap: 051 owned numerical correctness (PID kick/derivative filter/Durand-Kerner robustness/complementary-filter algebraic-identity bug); 053 owns library/research-trend comparison narrative; 054 owns naming/error-handling/ergonomics; 055 owns allocation/inlining/SIMD. Report at agents/052-control-missing.md, 340 lines.

051 | 2026-05-07 | control-numerics | control/ is shallower than canon: PID has clamping anti-windup (correct, one of four schemes; missing back-calculation Kt, conditional integration), uses derivative-on-error (NOT derivative-on-measurement) so any setpoint step produces Kd*Δsetpoint/dt spike (10*1500 for typical Pistachio cut — silent bug, untested because TestPID_DerivativeDampsOvershoot uses constant setpoint=1.0), no derivative filter (pure differentiator amplifies Nyquist noise by Kd/dt, Astrom-Murray §10.5 calls filter "essential"), no setpoint weighting (b,c params), discretization is consistent backward Euler (re-read of pid.go:84-87 confirms uses current err — initially read as forward Euler, withdrew that finding, but the choice is undocumented in package doc); ComplementaryFilter at filter.go:84 has algebraic-identity bug — α*(accel+gyro*dt)+(1-α)*accel ≡ accel + α*gyro*dt regardless of α, the α weighting cancels itself, function does not maintain prev-filtered-angle state so there is NO recursion, gyro contribution is NOT integrated across steps, Pistachio IMU sensor fusion broken if it depends on this, current tests at control_test.go:269-295 + control_edge_test.go:106-122 test the broken formula and pass; TransferFunction.Poles uses Durand-Kerner with quadratic convergence for simple roots but degrades to linear for multiple roots (e.g. (s+1)^3 where iter-tolerance 1e-12 yields actual root error ~ε^(1/3)≈1e-5) and explodes for clustered roots (denom = ∏(roots[i]-roots[j]) near-zero, delta=val/denom blows up, can flip stability classification); only tested through degree 3 with tolerance 1e-6 (control_test.go:462), zero golden-file vectors for high-order, repeated-root, or clustered-root polynomials; Cauchy bound r=max|a_i| is loose vs canonical 1+max|a_i/a_n|; durand-kerner reimplements realCos/realSin via 12-term Taylor with naive [0,2π] reduction by repeated subtraction (loses precision for large x; cmplx already imports math making the avoidance unjustified — net LOC reduction available); package missing entire frequency-domain toolkit topic-prompt requested: no Bode (caller can iterate Evaluate over log-spaced ω but no DCGain shortcut, no asymptotic ω→∞ rolloff helper, no phase unwrapping), no GainMargin/PhaseMargin/BandwidthCrossover, no Nyquist; no continuous-to-discrete bridge (no ZOH, no Tustin) so package cannot discretize a designed continuous controller — DiscreteTransferFunction type does not exist; no state-space (A,B,C,D), no LQR (Riccati Schur or doubling), no Kalman filter (standard form is unstable in finite precision, square-root Bierman-Thornton UDU' needed), no step/impulse response. Test coverage: single golden file pid_step_response.json covers PID-step-on-pure-integrator-plant only; LowPassFilter/HighPassFilter/ComplementaryFilter/RateLimiter/TransferFunction.Evaluate/Poles/IsStable have ZERO golden vectors breaking CLAUDE.md cross-language validation rule; no IEEE 754 mandatory edge tests (NaN/Inf coefficients, signed-zero in lowpass, subnormal dt in rate-limiter — though NaN propagation through anti-windup is verified-correct: NaN comparisons return false so neither clamp branch triggers and pTerm+iTerm+dTerm=NaN passes through). Filter API takes raw α not cutoff Hz unlike signal/ package — inconsistent surface; HighPass doc-comment on alpha-direction mildly misleading. Two active correctness bugs (C-PID-KICK-1 derivative-on-measurement 10 LOC, C-FILT-COMP-1 fix complementary algebraic identity 15 LOC) + two precision improvements (C-PID-DFILT-1 derivative filter 20 LOC, C-TF-ROOTS-1 Aberth-Ehrlich or companion-matrix QR 80 LOC) + two missing primitives (C-TF-BODE-1 Bode+margins 130 LOC, C-TF-DISC-1 Tustin/ZOH 120 LOC) + per-function golden vectors (C-TEST-GOLDEN-1 ~250 LOC JSON). Highest-leverage bundle = ~455 LOC fixing 2 silent bugs and adding cross-language coverage. Non-overlap: this report is solely numerical correctness; LQR/Kalman/state-space missing primitives flagged but ceded to 052 control-missing; library-comparison ceded to 053 control-sota; naming/Output() introspection ergonomics ceded to 054 control-api; per-call make() allocation in Poles flagged but ceded to 055 control-perf. Report at agents/051-control-numerics.md, 370 lines.

053 | 2026-05-07 | control-sota | architecture/interface comparison vs python-control 0.10.x + Slycot 0.6 + harold 1.0 + do-mpc 4.6 + CasADi 3.6 + Modelica.Blocks.Continuous 3.6 + Drake 1.30 + MATLAB Control System Toolbox R2025b. Disjoint from 051 (numerical correctness) and 052 (missing primitives) — this report asks "how do peer libraries type, compose, and dispatch control objects, and which patterns should reality borrow zero-dep?" Eight SOTA convergence axes that reality has not picked: (1) LTI umbrella interface — python-control / MATLAB / Modelica all have an abstract base; reality has only concrete *TransferFunction. (2) dt field as continuous/discrete discriminator — python-control 0.9+ unified two type hierarchies into one with dt=None/0/T; reality has NO discrete representation at all so 052-L2's parallel DiscreteTransferFunction would bifurcate every Bode/Nyquist function. (3) Block-diagram operator algebra — Series/Parallel/Feedback as named functions (Go can't overload * +) ports python-control's __mul__/__add__ semantics; reality is missing this entirely so loop-shaping T = G*K/(1+G*K) is not expressible. (4) Symbolic + AD compile target (CasADi MX/SX, Drake LeafSystem<AutoDiffXd>) — reality stays in pure-numeric camp aligned with harold/python-control. (5) FrequencyResponseData as first-class peer — MATLAB frd / python-control FRD is the "I have measurements not a model" entry-point; reality forces every consumer to first fit a TF before frequency analysis. (6) Modelica block-naming taxonomy — 35-block Modelica.Blocks.Continuous catalog (FirstOrder/SecondOrder/Integrator/Derivative/LimPID/Notch/Lead/Lag) is the canonical SISO vocabulary and is what Z-N/Cohen-Coon/AMIGO auto-tuning expect; reality's one-off names (LowPassFilter taking raw alpha) are inconsistent. (7) Pure-functional state pattern (Drake Context-as-explicit-state) — PIDState{IntegralSum, PrevError, PrevMeasured} value type + PIDStep(state, in)→(state, out) is the testable functional shape; reality's stateful struct mutates hidden internal fields, inconsistent with chaos/'s RK4Step pattern. (8) DynamicalSystem callback contract bridging to autodiff/ — CasADi Function-as-value / Drake AutoDiffXd-templated; reality's autodiff/ exists but control/ doesn't consume it, so future EKF/MPC/feedback-linearization have no shared interface. Per-library headline data type + engineering trick + reality-portable borrow: python-control = LTI ABC + dt-field unification + operator-overload composition (port as Series/Parallel/Feedback named funcs ~80 LOC); Slycot = stateless Fortran calls + LAPACK SB02MD/SB02OD naming-as-taxonomy (port as per-algorithm naming when multiple methods ship — LQRContinuousSchur vs LQRContinuousNewton); harold = pure-Python NumPy-only + aggressive host-LA reuse (sizing data point: 10k LOC achieves 80% of python-control surface, reality equivalent ~10-20k LOC over linalg/); do-mpc = symbolic Model + declare-then-compile MPC + orthogonal collocation (port the model/setup/step separation pattern, not the symbolic substrate); CasADi = MX/SX symbolic-functions + jacobian()-as-factory + sparsity-tracking (port function-as-value with derivative requestability via autodiff/, ~40 LOC interface); Modelica.Blocks = equation-based semantics + 35-block SISO catalog + Modelica.Blocks.Continuous naming (port the naming taxonomy not the equation-solving substrate); Drake = LeafSystem<T> templated scalar + Context-as-explicit-state + DiagramBuilder.Connect (port Context pattern, defer templated-scalar AD to Go-2.x generics era); MATLAB CST = tf/ss/zpk/frd four-type design + 30-field metadata + Variable s/z/q/p discriminator (port frd peer type ~40 LOC + 4 metadata fields InputName/OutputName/StateName/Notes ~15 LOC, decline 30-field bloat). Three highest-leverage architectural borrows all zero-dep all on existing surface: A1 LTI interface + dt float64 discriminator (~45 LOC, makes 052-L2 land cleanly without bifurcating type hierarchy), A2 Series/Parallel/Feedback/Negate constructors (~80 LOC, missing block-algebra surface for loop-shaping), A4 FrequencyResponseData{Response,Omega,Dt} type (~50 LOC, opens measured-data workflow). Bundle A1+A2+A4 = ~175 LOC, no new sibling-package deps, makes reality's control package architecturally peer to harold's first release. Second wave A3 ZPK accessor + A5 metadata + A6 C2D-with-method-enum + A8 Modelica naming taxonomy = ~235 LOC. Third wave A7 PIDState pure-functional companion + A9 DynamicalSystem AD-bridging interface = ~100 LOC, pre-empts 052-L7/L13/L14. Engineering elegance ranking CasADi > Drake > python-control > Modelica > harold > Slycot > MATLAB CST > reality. Document discipline ranking MATLAB CST > python-control > Modelica > Drake > CasADi > Slycot > harold > reality. Skip-as-out-of-scope: symbolic algebra layer (CasADi MX/SX would be 30k LOC), Slycot LAPACK work-array protocol (wrong for Go), MATLAB 30-field metadata blob (pick 4 stop), separate zpk type (just an accessor on TF), built-in plotting (downstream concern), JIT-compile-to-C pipeline (incompatible with zero-dep), multi-language code-gen (golden-file is reality's cross-language story). Non-overlap: 051 (numerical correctness — kick/filter/Durand-Kerner/IEEE-754), 052 (missing-primitive map — LQR/Kalman/MPC/H∞/sliding-mode 90 items 20-tier ladder), 053 = strictly architecture/interface, 054 (naming ergonomics ceded for final names), 055 (alloc/perf ceded for polynomial-multiply in Series/Parallel and make([]complex128) in FrequencyResponse). Report at agents/053-control-sota.md, 328 lines.

054 | 2026-05-07 | control-api | control/ user-facing surface (12 names: PIDController/NewPID/Update/Reset, TransferFunction.Evaluate/Poles/IsStable, LowPass/HighPass/Complementary/RateLimiter filters) has 8 ergonomic gaps before any new algorithm lands: (1) state discipline split — PIDController is stateful struct with Update/Reset, three filter funcs are caller-state with no Update/Reset/Init; no shared StatefulFilter interface so Pistachio IMU loop ends up with three idioms for one rhythm; (2) (setpoint, measured, dt) arg order is LabVIEW convention not Arduino/ROS/python-control (process-first or error-first) — keep it but document; missing UpdateError(err, dt) overload for ROS/python-control parity; (3) NewPID(kp,ki,kd,minOut,maxOut) 5-positional constructor will not survive next feature — adding derivativeOnMeasurement bool, integralLeak, setpointWeighting beta-gamma, derivative filter tau, anti-windup mode enum, back-calc Kt, bumpless transfer cycles to 12+ positional args; functional options (NewPID(kp,ki,kd, WithLimits(min,max), WithDerivativeOnMeasurement(), WithAntiWindup(BackCalc), WithDerivativeFilter(tau))) is the only forward-compat shape — matches grpc/k8s/http.Server idiom; (4) IsStable() bool swallows Durand-Kerner non-convergence (1000-iter cap with no error signal at transfer.go:177-180) — silent failure mode; need Stability enum (Unknown/Stable/Marginal/Unstable) plus (poles, error) on Poles(); marginal (Re(p)=0) is currently miscassified as Unstable by >= 0 check at transfer.go:247 because API cannot even express marginal case; (5) Poles() []complex128 has no Zeros/DCGain/RolloffSlope companions, no PoleZeroData struct — 052-Tier-1 needs both for Bode-margin computation; (6) four filter funcs span four naming schemes (LowPassFilter/ComplementaryFilter/RateLimiter conflate frequency-domain filtering with sensor fusion with slew-rate limit) — Modelica taxonomy (FirstOrderLowPass/SecondOrderLowPass/ComplementaryFusion/SlewRateLimit/Integrator/Derivative/LimPID/Notch) is canonical fix that aligns with Z-N/Cohen-Coon/AMIGO auto-tuning vocabulary; (7) TransferFunction.Numerator/Denominator are exported []float64 with no constructor New(num, den) (*TransferFunction, error) so caller can mutate slice between Evaluate and Poles silently desynchronizing future cached pole vector and 053-Tier-1 dt field cannot land without breaking struct literals; (8) three error contracts in 490 LOC — panic for programmer error (Evaluate empty, NewPID min>max), silent clamp for out-of-range (alpha clip in 3 filters, dt-no-op in RateLimiter+Complementary), silent-best-effort for iterative non-convergence (Poles); should consolidate to documented panic/clamp/error matrix matching chaos/linalg/prob policy. Future shapes for 052/055: C2D(sys, dt, method DiscretizationMethod) with enum (Tustin/TustinPrewarp/ZOH/FOH/ForwardEuler/BackwardEuler/Impulse) — ONE function not separate Tustin/ZOH/BackwardEuler funcs forcing user to know all four — matches python-control c2d signature decisively; Bode(sys LTI, omegas []float64, outMag, outPhase []float64) with caller-supplied output buffers per CLAUDE.md no-allocs rule, NOT (mag, phase []float64) parallel-slice tuple return; Margins(sys LTI) StabilityMargins struct (5 floats + bool, struct not 5-tuple); StateSpace{A, B, C, D *linalg.Matrix, dt float64} with NewStateSpace constructor validating shapes A:n×n B:n×m C:p×n D:p×m; TF2SS/SS2TF symmetric naming. python-control parity table: c2d(sys, dt) → C2D(sys, dt, Tustin); bode(sys, w) → Bode(sys, w, outMag, outPhase); margin(sys) → Margins(sys); pole(sys) → sys.Poles(); zero(sys) → sys.Zeros(); pid(kp,ki,kd) → NewPID(kp,ki,kd, WithLimits(...)). Recommendations ordered impact/cost: critical-path PR (155 LOC non-breaking) = error-policy doc + Stability enum + Poles (vals,err) + Zeros/DCGain/RolloffSlope + UpdateError + LTI interface + StatefulFilter; breaking-changes PR = functional-options NewPID + private TF.num/den fields with New constructor. Non-overlap: 051 (numerical correctness — kick/filter/Durand-Kerner/IEEE-754), 052 (missing primitives — LQR/Kalman/MPC/Bode/Nyquist algorithms themselves), 053 (architectural patterns — LTI/dt/Series/Parallel/Feedback/FRD/Modelica naming as patterns); 054 owns Go signature shapes of those patterns. Report at agents/054-control-api.md, 397 lines.

055 | 2026-05-07 | control-perf | control/ hot path is genuinely zero-alloc today (PID.Update, LowPass/HighPass/Complementary/RateLimiter, TransferFunction.Evaluate all verified by inspection — no make, no slice growth, no interface boxing, no closure capture); PID.Update at ~5 ns/call is 0.03% of one core for 100 controllers at 60 FPS, Evaluate at ~10 ns for deg-3 TF means 1000-sample Bode sweep ≈ 30 µs. BUT five "Zero heap allocations" doc claims at filter.go:11/35/71/99 + transfer.go:33 are unverified — repo has zero benchmarks anywhere (find C:/limitless/foundation/reality -name "*bench*.go" returns empty) and zero AllocsPerRun-guard tests; the no-alloc contract is part of CLAUDE.md §3 promise but unenforced. Two real allocation sites: TransferFunction.Poles allocates make([]float64, len(d)) at transfer.go:90 + make([]complex128, degree) at transfer.go:139 = 24·deg+8 bytes/call, fine for one-shot stability check but breaks under 052-Tier-1 RootLocus(num, den, 200 gains) = 200 allocations and Margins-via-pole-search; needs PolesInto(buf, scratch) buffer-passing variant ~25 LOC matching linalg/decompose.go:266 CholeskyDecompose(A, n, L) shape. Forward-looking 052 cost-class forecast across 14 primitives: expm Padé-13 scaling-and-squaring O(n³·log s) ≈ 50n³ flops needs 7 scratch n×n matrices (A2/A4/A6/U/V/P/Q in flight); Hessenberg 10n³/3; Schur Francis-QR ≈ 25n³; Lyapunov Bartels-Stewart 29n³; CARE Hamiltonian-Schur 200n³; DARE symplectic-pencil 300n³ — Pistachio frame budget 16.7 ms means n=4 expm ≈ 3 µs (0.02%), n=8 Schur ≈ 12 µs, n=8 CARE ≈ 110 µs all fine if not done per-frame, but n=32 CARE ≈ 7 ms = 42% of frame catastrophic. Required online/offline partition: ONLINE per-frame = PID.Update, filters, Kalman.Predict/Update (n≤32, p≤8), LQR-precomputed-K · x, matvec, Evaluate, RateLimiter; OFFLINE design-time = expm/Schur/Lyapunov/Sylvester/CARE/DARE/LQR-design/tf2ss/ss2tf/Poles/Zeros/Bode/Margins/RootLocus/place/c2d. Currently asymmetry between Evaluate (online-safe) and Poles (offline-only ~3 µs deg-3, ~30 µs deg-10) is unflagged in package doc — IsStable looks like a cheap predicate by name but is actually a Durand-Kerner pole-find, a Pistachio author calling it per-frame for deg-10 plant busts 60 FPS by 10× silently. Required buffer-protocol design before primitive PRs land or each reinvents scratch convention: ExpmWorkspace(n) int + Expm(A, n, t, out, scratch) int signature; same shape for Schur(A, n, T, Q, scratch), Lyapunov, Sylvester, CARE(A, B, Q, R, P, scratch), DARE, plus PolesInto and Bode(sys, omegas, outMag, outPhase) — 8 functions needing WorkspaceSize+scratch protocol. Cost amortization: cache SchurForm{T, Q, n} for fixed-A gain-scheduled LQR mirrors linalg.LUDecompose+LUSolve pattern, kills 25n³ recompute. Bode ω-grid is data-parallel-ready (no cross-ω deps, embarrassingly parallel — defer to future parallel/ package, keep control/ zero-dep) at ~40 µs per 1000 samples with dB conversion (cmplx.Abs ~30 cyc + math.Log10 ~40 cyc + Horner ~30 ns), Horner already optimal among linear schemes (Estrin only matters n>50 which classical TFs don't reach). complex(coeffs[i], 0) at transfer.go:55-58/154-168 compiles to MOVSD/XORPS 1-cycle pair, free; precomputing []complex128 in TF constructor gives 5% but blocked on 054 private-fields refactor. Internal Taylor realCos/realSin at transfer.go:191-225 not a perf concern (~25 cycles, called once per pole at degree-spaced angles needing 0-1 reduction iters) but math/cmplx already imports math so just calling math.Cos/Sin saves 35 LOC AND gets math's O(1) Cody-Waite reduction — already 051's territory. Recommended bundle 1+2+3 = 95 LOC non-breaking: C-PERF-BENCH-1 (30 LOC control_alloc_test.go with AllocsPerRun on PID.Update/Evaluate/4 filters + 3 Benchmark*) locks no-alloc contract before 052 lands; C-PERF-PARTITION-1 (40 LOC doc) tags all functions Online/Offline; C-PERF-POLES-1 (25 LOC) PolesInto(buf, scratch) + Poles() wrapper required before RootLocus. Items 4+5 (ExpmWorkspace protocol + SchurForm cache) ~40 LOC are the 052 buffer-protocol design — must be fixed before primitives land. Item 7 (cache complex coeffs in TF) ~10 LOC blocked on 054 New(num, den) constructor PR. Pistachio 60-FPS suitability table: PID.Update green (5 ns), all filters green (3-5 ns), RateLimiter green (4 ns), Evaluate-deg3 green (10 ns), Poles-deg3 RED ~3 µs offline-only, IsStable RED ~3 µs offline-only, Poles-deg10 RED ~30 µs offline-only. Non-overlap: 051 owns Durand-Kerner→companion-matrix-QR (which is also a perf cleanup — 055 confirms but defers ownership), 052 owns the 90-item LQR/Kalman/MPC ladder (055 owns runtime cost class + buffer protocol design only), 053 owns Series/Parallel/Feedback composition (055 ceded composition-cost analysis: Series TF polymult is O(deg(N1)·deg(N2)) and allocates new []float64), 054 owns API ergonomics (055 confirms 054's Bode(sys, omegas, outMag, outPhase) buffer-passing recommendation is the alloc-free shape and adds ExpmWorkspace-style protocol for the algorithmic wave). Report at agents/055-control-perf.md, 399 lines.

056 | 2026-05-07 | crypto-numerics | crypto/ is a 884-LOC number-theory + non-crypto-hash + deterministic-PRNG kit whose package name promises crypto but delivers zero cryptographic primitives (no SHA-2/3, no HMAC, no AES, no curve arithmetic, no constant-time anything, no CSPRNG); the number-theoretic core is mathematically correct on the happy path (ModPow verified bit-exact against math/big at modulus 2^64-59 via overflow-safe Russian-peasant mulmod, deterministic Miller-Rabin witness sets {2,3,5,7} for n<3.215×10^9 and {2,3,5,7,11,13,17} for n<3.317×10^24 are correctly applied, MT19937-64 tempering masks 0x5555555555555555/0x71D67FFFEDA60000/0xFFF7EEE000000000 match canonical reference, FNV-1a 32/64 constants match draft-eastlake-fnv-17, MurmurHash3_32 vectors match Appleby smhasher, splitmix64 constants match Vigna 2017, PCG-XSH-RR and Xoshiro256** shapes match O'Neill 2014 / Blackman-Vigna 2021); but ships TWO LATENT SILENT-WRONG bugs both at uint64↔int64 boundary — (1) ModInverse(a, mod) casts both args to int64 unconditionally (modular.go:62), so any a or mod ≥ 2^63 silently misbehaves with no error or panic because ExtendedGCD then operates on a negative input and returns a wrong-but-plausible Bézout coefficient (constructed reproducer: ModInverse(3, 1<<63) returns garbage instead of (0,false)); (2) ChineseRemainder(residues, moduli) computes M = product of moduli without overflow check (modular.go:113-115), wraps modulo 2^64 silently, and every subsequent mulmod/addmod uses the wrapped modulus producing wrong-but-plausible uint64 result with err==nil. Plus six lower-severity findings: millerRabinWitness has theoretical r=0 underflow (currently unreachable but fragile); MillerRabin k=1 golden vector for 561 has misleading description (561 IS caught by witness 2; canonical "k=1 is unsafe" demo is n=2047); mulmod docstring claims "Karatsuba-like 128-bit decomposition" but is actually Russian-peasant doubling (bits.Mul64+bits.Div64 would be 5× faster and simpler in 4 LOC); ConsistentHash uses float64 arithmetic that loses precision for numBuckets > 2^31 (Lamping-Veach footnote); IsPrime witness-set citations missing despite CLAUDE.md §4 mandate (Pomerance-Selfridge-Wagstaff 1980 / Jaeschke 1993 / Sorenson-Webster 2017); MillerRabin(n, 1) using only witness 2 produces false-negative on strong-base-2 pseudoprime n=2047 which the golden file does not test. Constant-time hygiene: ZERO — every conditional branches on data (ModPow on each exp bit, mulmod on each b bit, addmod on a≥m-b comparison, GCD on iteration count, IsPrime early-returns first composite witness); fine for stated non-crypto scope, catastrophic if anyone uses it for secrets. Side-channel hygiene: not even attempted; no concept of "secret data" in this package. Random number generation: three deterministic PRNGs explicitly labelled deterministic ("the same seed always produces the same sequence" — rng.go:31, 99, 144), none claiming crypto-grade — honest. SHA-2/3, HMAC, AES, RSA, ECDSA, Ed25519, X25519, P-256, secp256k1, Curve25519, HKDF, PBKDF2, Argon2, scrypt, AKS, BPSW, Solovay-Strassen, ECPP, Lucas-Lehmer, Pocklington — all absent. FIPS test vectors checked: zero, because no FIPS primitives exist. Golden-file coverage 2/18+ functions (miller_rabin.json 10 cases, mersenne_twister.json 10 cases) vs CLAUDE.md §1 mandate 20+ per function = ~5% of mandate. Recommended bundle: 5-PR fix-set ~37 LOC non-breaking — CRY-DOC-1 prepend "NOT cryptographically secure" warning to package doc (10 LOC), CRY-NUM-1-FIX guard ModInverse args ≥ 2^63 returning (0,false) (12 LOC), CRY-NUM-2-FIX guard ChineseRemainder M overflow via bits.Mul64 (8 LOC), CRY-NUM-3-FIX defensive r==0 guard in millerRabinWitness (3 LOC), CRY-NUM-4-FIX add witness-set citations (4 LOC); deferred bundle CRY-GOLDEN-1 ~600 LOC test data + CRY-MULMOD-OPT 4-LOC bits.Mul64 perf + CRY-FUZZ-1 ~50 LOC fuzz harness. Non-overlap: missing primitives (SHA-2/3, HMAC, AES, curves, constant-time scalar mul) is 057+; API shape ceded; perf ceded except mulmod. Report at agents/056-crypto-numerics.md, 396 lines.

058 | 2026-05-07 | crypto-sota | architecture/hygiene comparison vs libsodium / RustCrypto / Crypto++ / dalek / arkworks / BoringSSL / NSS / Bouncy Castle / Plonky3-Halo2-Risc0-Sp1 / Pasta / Mersenne-Plonky3-circle-FFT — disjoint from 056 (numerical correctness) and 057 (primitive enumeration); 058 asks "how do peer libraries type, encode, and dispatch crypto values, and which patterns transfer zero-dep into Go?" Distilled six transferable principles: P1 typed algebraic carriers (Scalar/Point/Field/Commitment/Signature as distinct types not []byte aliases — borrow from dalek+RustCrypto+arkworks, ~150 LOC up front pre-empts the entire wrong-key-type / wrong-curve CVE class), P2 canonical-encoding discipline at every byte boundary (borrow from dalek+libsodium — every FromBytes strict, every Bytes() unique-canonical, non-canonical inputs typed-error rejected; ~5 LOC per type with negative-case golden vectors), P3 constant-time-by-construction (borrow from RustCrypto subtle::Choice + dalek table lookups; reality re-export crypto/subtle as crypto/ct, document per-function CT-vs-variable-time posture, branch-free conditional-select for table lookups — non-negotiable before any secret-handling primitive ships), P4 opinion at the function name not at enum/string parameter (borrow libsodium+RustCrypto Ed25519Sign-as-name discipline; REJECT Bouncy-Castle factory pattern Cipher.getInstance("AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding") which has 20yr CVE history of typo-as-vulnerability), P5 small ZK-friendly fields are CHEAPEST CRYPTO-MATH WIN AVAILABLE (borrow arkworks PrimeField interface + Plonky3 Goldilocks 2^64-2^32+1 ~10 LOC reduction via bits.Mul64+conditional-subs fits in single uint64 supports FFT size 2^32, plus Mersenne-31 ~80 LOC; reality should ship Goldilocks Field64+Mersenne31 Field32+Poseidon2+Transcript as FIRST non-trivial crypto primitive ~600 LOC before signatures even start, no big-int no curves no CT no CSPRNG, deterministic-everywhere, cross-validates against Plonky3 reference impl — moves crypto/ from "non-cryptographic kit" to "ZK-grade math primitive library" in one month), P6 Transcript object for any Fiat-Shamir-style protocol (borrow from Plonky3/Halo2; ~80 LOC duplex-sponge over Keccak or Poseidon, AbsorbField/AbsorbBytes/SqueezeField/SqueezeBytes API — pre-empts entire Frozen-Heart 2023 / weak-Fiat-Shamir CVE class). Per-library headline + misuse-prevention + reality borrow: libsodium = NaCl-derived 25 functions opinion-stacked + length-constants-as-discoverability + crypto_box_PUBLICKEYBYTES naming + sodium_memzero/mlock secret-buffer-hygiene API + XChaCha20Poly1305 192-bit nonce default to remove reuse footgun (borrow length-constants + opinion-at-function-name, do NOT borrow protocol layer); RustCrypto = ~80-crate workspace with digest/cipher/signature/aead/kdf/mac base traits + zero-cost generic monomorphization + const-fn primitives + typestate (Hasher consumed by .finalize() type-system forbids double-finalize) + zeroize::Zeroize derive-macro + subtle::Choice CtOption branch-free conditional types + elliptic-curve trait family with associated Scalar/AffinePoint/ProjectivePoint/FieldBytes types preventing scalar-from-curve-A times point-from-curve-B (borrow per-curve typed families P256Point/P256Scalar/Ed25519Point/Ed25519Scalar as distinct Go types, [N]byte fixed-size arrays for serialization, do NOT borrow trait substrate use Go generics narrowly); Crypto++ = Wei-Dai 1995 200kLOC ~150 algorithms C++ template metaprogramming compile-time wiring CBC_Mode<AES>::Encryption + BufferedTransformation pipeline source>>filter>>filter>>sink + SecByteBlock-zeroizes-on-destruction + OAEP<SHA256>::Decoder explicitly named (borrow generic HMAC[H Hash] Go-1.18-generics narrow parameterization + naming discipline algorithm-mode-hash uniform across all primitives EcdsaP256Sha256, do NOT borrow template substrate or pipeline operator overloading); dalek = Lovecruft+de-Valence Curve25519/Ed25519/Ristretto255 pure-Rust no-std MOST-CAREFULLY-CONSTANT-TIME EC library + Ristretto255 prime-order quotient eliminating Edwards25519 cofactor-8 confusion + canonical-encoding discipline (one encoding per group element, byte-equality implies group equality, decode rejects 7 non-canonical encodings) + Scalar::from_bytes_mod_order vs from_canonical_bytes two-named-constructors named after security posture + no Default impl preventing accidental zero-scalar/identity-point + 4-bit windowed scalar mul with constant-time table lookup (THIS IS THE ARCHETYPE; borrow canonical-encoding discipline + distinct types per group view EdwardsPoint/MontgomeryPoint/Ristretto255Point + two-named-constructors FromBytes-canonical-strict vs FromBytesUnchecked-document-footgun + constant-time table-lookup pattern); arkworks = Mishra et al 2020 Rust ZK/crypto generic over F:PrimeField G:AffineCurve P:Pairing + concrete BLS12-381 BN254 MNT4/6 Pasta Pallas/Vesta Mersenne/BabyBear/Goldilocks + PrimeField trait derives FFT support via TWO_ADIC_ROOT_OF_UNITY constant + AffineCurve vs ProjectiveCurve type-distinct preventing serialize-projective-with-garbage-Z + SerializationError typed enum (borrow PrimeField interface as canonical Fp shape Add/Sub/Mul/Square/Inverse/Negate/Pow/Sqrt/IsZero/IsOne/FromBytes/ToBytes/TwoAdicRootOfUnity/CharBitLen ~40 LOC + Affine-vs-Projective separation per curve + small-prime field family Goldilocks/Mersenne31/BabyBear, do NOT borrow r1cs-std circuit DSL out-of-scope); BoringSSL/NSS = Google-Mozilla protocol libraries with crypto math at the bottom (BoringSSL crypto/fipsmodule, NSS lib/freebl) hand-tuned per-arch assembly + portable C fallback + opinion-stacked higher-level APIs (BoringSSL low-level BIGNUM/EC_POINT/EVP internal SSL_* exposed; NSS PKCS#11 token abstraction handles never raw bytes) + FIPS 140-2/3 self-tests on startup (borrow math/protocol separation discipline + build-tagged amd64-assembly forward-compat shape Go does naturally + optional crypto.Verify runtime self-test, do NOT borrow BIGNUM/EC_POINT C-API shapes alien to Go); Bouncy Castle = CAUTIONARY TALE 500-algorithm Java/C# JCA factory Cipher.getInstance("AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding") string-keyed has 20yr CVE history of typo-as-vulnerability + provider/SPI plugin allowing malicious-provider injection + OID-based dispatch with multiple wrong-algorithm-under-same-OID bugs (DO NOT borrow factory pattern period, DO NOT borrow provider/SPI architecture security smell, DO borrow OID-as-godoc-citation when ECDSA-P256 ships cite "OID 1.2.840.10045.4.3.2 ecdsa-with-SHA256" for X.509/CMS/JWS wiring + breadth as benchmark reality's 057 ladder ~30% of BC surface); ZK-frame primitives Plonky3/Halo2/Risc0/Sp1 = small-prime field (Goldilocks 2^64-2^32+1, Mersenne-31 2^31-1, BabyBear 2^31-2^27+1, KoalaBear 2^31-2^24+1) → polynomial ring → polynomial commitment FRI/IPA/KZG → constraint system Plonkish/R1CS → frontend DSL → zkVM (Risc0/Sp1 RISC-V, Plonky3 field+IOP only) + algebraic hashes Poseidon2 2023-halved-rounds Rescue-Prime Rescue Anemoi Griffin Monolith Skyscraper + lookup arguments Plookup Caulk Halo2 + soundness-bug-class from incorrect-Fiat-Shamir (Plonky2 Frozen-Heart 2023 missing public-input commitment in transcript) → fix is Transcript-discipline labeled-absorb-then-squeeze (borrow Goldilocks+Mersenne31 small fields + Poseidon2 ZK-hash ~400 LOC Goldilocks instantiation + Transcript ~80 LOC duplex sponge, do NOT borrow Plonkish constraint system zkmark scope); Pasta Pallas/Vesta = Daira-Hopwood Zcash-Halo cycle-of-curves base-field-of-each = scalar-field-of-other for cheap recursive SNARK verification short-Weierstrass a=0 b=5 prime-order ~256-bit + deliberately differently-named to make cross-curve mistakes syntactically obvious anti-pattern Curve0/Curve1 best-practice purpose-named (borrow naming convention only, defer Pasta itself until consumer demand); Mersenne-Plonky3-circle-FFT 2024 = Fp=2^31-1 Mersenne-31 has no large 2-adic subgroup so use circle FFT over Pythagorean curve x^2+y^2=1 in FpxFp whose subgroup of order 2^31 hides math behind RadixDecoupledPoly type with eval_at_point/low_degree_extension methods (borrow Mersenne-31 itself ~80 LOC, do NOT ship circle FFT yet research-frontier, DO borrow lesson small-fields-can-FFT-over-non-obvious-algebraic-objects → factor butterfly so signal/FFT and crypto NTT share ~200 LOC). Architecture-level v1 recommendations: (R1) sub-package the crypto/ tree per 057 forecast as crypto/ct + crypto/field + crypto/curve + crypto/hash + crypto/sig + crypto/kdf + crypto/poly + crypto/zk adopting RustCrypto per-primitive split; (R2) ship Goldilocks Field64 + Mersenne-31 Field32 + Poseidon2 + Transcript FIRST ~600 LOC no-bigint no-curves no-CT no-CSPRNG deterministic golden-cross-validateable against Plonky3 — CHEAPEST REAL CRYPTOGRAPHIC PRIMITIVE SURFACE in 1 month moves crypto/ from "non-cryptographic kit" to "ZK-grade math primitive library", signatures (057-Sequence-A) follow months 2-3; (R3) typed values everywhere not []byte not *big.Int, 30% LOC overhead pays for itself in first CVE not shipped; (R4) canonical-encoding from day 1 free up-front expensive retrofit; (R5) document constant-time policy per-function CLAUDE.md §5 "precision documented not assumed" extends to "side-channel posture documented not assumed". Engineering-elegance ranking 058 editorial: dalek > arkworks > RustCrypto > libsodium > Plonky3 > Crypto++ > BoringSSL > NSS > Bouncy Castle. Non-overlap: 056 owned correctness of present functions + package-name-vs-contents; 057 owned primitive-by-primitive Tier-1/2/3 ladder LOC estimates; 058 NEVER duplicates LOC estimates only covers HOW primitives should be structured (typed values, canonical encoding, CT discipline, transcript) not WHICH primitives ship; future API agent owns Go-signature-shape choices falling out of P1-P6 patterns; future perf agent owns assembly fallbacks/Pippenger-windows/pairing-batching, 058 flags forward-compat assembly-hook shape only. Report at agents/058-crypto-sota.md, 380 lines.

057 | 2026-05-07 | crypto-missing | crypto/ ships zero of the nine topic-listed primitives (EdDSA, BLS, Schnorr, Pedersen, Shamir, VSS, OT, GC, ECIES) and reaching any of them requires first landing five missing foundation layers totaling ~8000-12000 LOC: (T1-BIGINT) multi-precision integer arithmetic since the package is uint64-only and Curve25519 needs 256-bit, BLS12-381 base field needs 381-bit, RSA-2048 needs 2048-bit — decision required between math/big wrapper (cheap, ~100 LOC, ties cross-language golden contract to Go's specific algorithm) vs from-scratch 64-bit-limb impl (~400 LOC, cross-language-friendly, the path blst/arkworks/dalek all chose); (T1-FIELD) generic prime-field Fp with Add/Sub/Mul/Square/Inverse/Sqrt-via-Tonelli-Shanks/Pow/Frobenius ~300 LOC; (T1-FIELDTOWER) Fp2/Fp4/Fp6/Fp12 extension tower required for any pairing-friendly curve, Karatsuba/Toom-Cook extension multiplication, ~400 LOC; (T1-EC) elliptic curve point group in three models — short-Weierstrass for P-256/P-384/P-521/secp256k1/BN254/BLS12-381-G1, twisted-Edwards for Ed25519/Ed448 with complete addition law, Montgomery for X25519 ladder — projective Jacobian coords, constant-time Montgomery ladder OR fixed-window with conditional-select point tables (NO double-and-add-with-branching), ~500 LOC; (T1-HASH) SHA-256/SHA-512 (FIPS 180-4 round function math: 64/80 round Ch/Maj/Sigma/sigma over 32/64-bit words), SHA-3/Keccak-f[1600] (sponge over 5x5x64-lane state, 24 rounds of theta/rho/pi/chi/iota — pure GF(2) linear maps + nonlinear chi step), HMAC, BLAKE2b/3, HKDF (RFC 5869), ~600 LOC; (T1-CT) constant-time discipline kit since 056 confirmed package has zero CT hygiene, ConstantTimeSelect/ByteEq/Compare/LessOrEq branch-free, ~100 LOC. After foundation: Tier-1 topic-listed primitives — Schnorr ~80 LOC (BIP-340 x-only Taproot the canonical modern target), Ed25519+Ed448 ~250 LOC (RFC 8032, deterministic-by-design k = H(prefix||m), known-ambiguous cofactored-vs-non-cofactored verification needs explicit pick + cross-validation against Go-stdlib/pyca/OpenSSL), ECDSA ~150 LOC (P-256/P-384/secp256k1 with RFC 6979 deterministic-k via HMAC_DRBG over (d,m) — the deterministic-friendly version reality must target instead of random-k), Shamir Secret Sharing ~150 LOC pure Lagrange interpolation in Fp (CHEAPEST topic primitive, no curves, no hash, recommended Tier-1 anchor), Pedersen commitments g^v·h^r ~80 LOC over EC (hiding+binding under DLOG, building block for VSS/range proofs/ring sigs). Tier-2 topic+advanced — Feldman+Pedersen VSS ~200 LOC over Shamir+Pedersen, FROST threshold Schnorr ~400 LOC RFC 9591 (2024) two-round threshold for Schnorr/EdDSA, BLS signatures ~600 LOC needing pairing+hash-to-curve (smallest-on-the-wire 48 bytes, most math-heavy), Optimal-Ate pairing on BLS12-381/BN254 ~800 LOC SINGLE LARGEST CRYPTO-MATH EXPENSE Miller's algorithm + line functions + Frobenius twist for G2⊂E'(Fp2)→E(Fp12) embedding + final exponentiation easy-part(Frobenius)+hard-part(cyclotomic) + subgroup checks, RFC 9380 hash-to-curve ~300 LOC SSWU/Icart/Elligator-2/BLS12-381-isogeny-suite + expand_message_xmd/xof, Pippenger MSM ~250 LOC bucket method, ECIES ~150 LOC (math layer to KDF boundary, AES is consumer concern), ElGamal+Paillier ~300 LOC homomorphic encryption (ElGamal multiplicatively homomorphic, Paillier additively homomorphic via lambda=lcm(p-1,q-1) and Carmichael L, all pure modular arithmetic). Tier-3 advanced/niche — 1-of-2 OT (Naor-Pinkas DDH / Chou-Orlandi CCS'15) ~150 LOC + IKNP/KOS/SoftSpokenOT extension ~300 LOC underpinning MPC, Garbled Circuits (Yao + point-and-permute + free-XOR + half-gate + fixed-key-AES garbling — math layer in-scope, circuit IR engineering) ~500 LOC partial, KZG ~150 LOC + FRI ~400 LOC + IPA/Bulletproofs ~300 LOC polynomial commitments OVERLAP-RISK with future zkmark/ package, NTT ~200 LOC (Falcon/Kyber/Dilithium/Plonk, OVERLAPS signal/ FFT — same algorithm different ring, share once), lattice crypto Module-LWE/SIS over Rq=Zq[X]/(X^n+1) ~800 LOC for minimal Kyber/Dilithium math layer, algebraic hashes Poseidon/Rescue/Anemoi ~400 LOC ZK-circuit-friendly low-degree polynomial S-boxes IDEAL FOR REALITY all pure math, Verkle/vector commitments ~400 LOC mostly engineering once polycommit lands, discrete-log algorithms BSGS+Pollard-rho+Pohlig-Hellman ~200 LOC (uses existing ChineseRemainder), symmetric-MATH primitives Poly1305 ~80 LOC (polynomial mod (2^130-5)) + GHASH ~60 LOC (GF(2^128)=GF(2)[X]/(x^128+x^7+x^2+x+1)) + ChaCha20 quarter-round ARX (marginal) + AES S-box as algebraic object (GF(2^8) inverse + affine), HMAC-DRBG/CTR-DRBG/Hash-DRBG ~200 LOC NIST SP 800-90A — DETERMINISTIC GIVEN SEED so fits reality model perfectly, fills the "fake gap" where reality has deterministic non-crypto PRNGs (MT/PCG/Xoshiro) but no deterministic crypto-grade DRBG, NIST CAVP test vectors trivial. Cross-language golden feasibility per primitive: T1-HASH/ECDSA-RFC6979/EdDSA-RFC8032/Schnorr-BIP340/HMAC-DRBG TRIVIAL (official vectors); T1-EC-Weierstrass/HKDF/Hash-to-curve-RFC9380 EASY; BIGINT/FIELD/Shamir/NTT/Poseidon MEDIUM (need to publish reality-canonical vectors); FIELDTOWER/PAIRING HARD (intermediate values non-canonical, fixture only final outputs). Out-of-scope confirmed: AES round function streaming (byte-level engineering, but GF(2^8) S-box AS ALGEBRAIC OBJECT in-scope), AEAD modes (GCM/CCM/SIV/OCB/ChaCha20-Poly1305 = protocol composition), TLS/Noise/QUIC handshake (state machines), X.509/PKCS serialization, CSPRNG-from-OS-entropy (reality is deterministic), PBKDF2-borderline (HMAC iteration in-scope) Argon2/scrypt out (memory-hard memory model), full PQC ML-KEM/ML-DSA/SLH-DSA/Falcon defer to T3. Recommended ordering: Sequence-A signatures-first ~2000 LOC over 3 months delivers FIVE-of-nine topic primitives without pairings — (1) BIGINT+CT 200 LOC, (2) FIELD+HASH+NIST-CAVP-vectors 700 LOC, (3) EC-Weierstrass+ECDSA+RFC-6979 500 LOC, (4) EC-Edwards+Ed25519+RFC-8032 400 LOC, (5) Schnorr+Pedersen+Shamir 200 LOC. Sequence-B pairings-first ~3000 LOC delivers BLS but defers simpler sigs. Recommend Sequence-A first because covers more topic-listed at lower cost, deferring BLS until ZK/threshold-sig consumer materializes. Non-overlap: 056 owns correctness of what's there; this owns what's missing; API-shape (likely crypto/ becomes meta-package with crypto/field, crypto/curve, crypto/sig, crypto/hash subpackages once 8000 LOC lands) deferred to future agent; perf audit (pairing+MSM dominant) deferred; KZG/FRI/IPA overlap-risk with hypothetical zkmark/ flagged but not duplicated; T1-HASH layer overlap with hypothetical hash/ package flagged. Headline: of the topic-listed nine primitives, ZERO are reachable today; Shamir is the cheapest entry point at ~150 LOC and is the recommended Tier-1 user-facing anchor. Report at agents/057-crypto-missing.md, 388 lines.

059 | 2026-05-07 | crypto-api | crypto/ ships uniformly math-style argument order (data-then-modifier) which is correct for number theory but introduces seam-decision when 057 cryptographic signature schemes land — recommend math-style for non-crypto, crypto-style key-first (Sign(priv,msg) per crypto/ed25519 idiom) for crypto, with secret-before-public invariant + named typed keys not []byte; today five concrete misuse vectors audible from source: (M1) package doc prime.go lines 1-11 says "Package crypto provides number theory and cryptographic primitives" overclaim — none of four hash funcs FNV1a32/FNV1a64/MurmurHash3_32/SituationHashWithStructure are collision-resistant, none of three PRNGs MT19937-64/PCG-XSH-RR/Xoshiro256** are CSPRNGs (PCG broken under Bouillaguet 2020 chosen-state attack 512 outputs, MT19937 famously broken 624 outputs, Xoshiro256** broken under known-output) yet consumer reading godoc reasonably concludes these are crypto-grade; (M2) ModPow modular.go lines 31-37 has data-dependent branch on exp%2==1 leaking exponent bit pattern via timing — Kocher 1996 RSA timing attack — fine today only used internally with public n-1 in IsPrime but exported and future RSA-CRT consumer will leak private exponent must rename to imply non-CT or reserve ModPowConstTime for 057 future; (M3) ExtendedGCD prime.go lines 261-266 data-dependent quotients in loop body — same risk dangerous for ECDSA s=k^-1(z+r·d) where k secret; (M4) FNV1a64(password)-stored-as-hash silent footgun, hash docstrings say non-cryptographic but package name undermines individual disclaimers; (M5) IsPrime allocates witness slice fresh on every call hot-path allocation 4-LOC fix to package var. KEY TYPES not applicable today (no keys exist) but seam binding for 057: four candidate shapes — (A) plain []byte zero misuse-prevention crypto/ed25519-stdlib choice, (B) named slice types ~5 LOC catches pub/priv swap, (C) named fixed arrays [32]byte/[64]byte ~10 LOC catches pub/priv + length errors dalek-style, (D) opaque struct private fields ~30 LOC carries algebraic invariants Curve25519-clamped-scalar Ed25519-prime-order-subgroup-checked + Zero() hygiene RustCrypto/dalek-style — recommend (C) for symmetric/hash, (D) for elliptic curves where key carries algebraic invariants, ~150 LOC for full T1+T2 surface. CT SURFACE zero matches for ConstantTime/ConstEq/subtle/ct in source — #1 actionable gap — three options (3a) re-export crypto/subtle directly REJECTED by CLAUDE.md §2 zero-deps without explicit clause, (3b) reimplement in crypto/ct subpackage ~80 LOC RISKY because Go 1.22+ inlining no asm vetting, (3c) HYBRID re-export crypto/subtle in thin wrapper bool-returning idiomatic-Go ~40 LOC + 1-sentence CLAUDE.md clause — RECOMMEND (3c). RANDOM SOURCE INJECTION moot today but for 057 stdlib precedent splits — Ed25519.Sign(priv,msg) deterministic-by-design RFC8032 k=H(prefix||m) NO io.Reader, ECDSA.SignASN1(rand io.Reader,priv,hash) mandatory, RSA.SignPKCS1v15(rand,priv,hash,hashed) mandatory, ed25519.GenerateKey(rand io.Reader) mandatory — pattern deterministic-by-design no rand otherwise io.Reader first named rand stdlib-verbatim. UNIQUE SYNERGY HIDDEN-GEM Reality already exposes deterministic PRNGs as first-class types something NO SOTA peer does (libsodium/RustCrypto/dalek all just use crypto/rand) — F5 fix add Read(p []byte)(n int,err error) adapter to MersenneTwister/PCG/Xoshiro ~15 LOC each ~45 LOC total enables io.Reader-injection so every randomized signature scheme golden-file-tested with bit-exact reproducibility against deterministic PRNG stream — pre-empts golden-test-randomized-sig problem. DOMAIN SEPARATION zero hash/sig functions take DST today defensible for non-crypto but for 057 every modern primitive REQUIRES mandatory DST: BLS12-381 RFC9380 hash-to-curve mandatory DST, BIP-340 Schnorr challenge=tagged_hash(BIP0340/challenge,...), Pedersen H domain-separated from G via hash_to_curve(commitment_h,...), Ed25519ph/ctx RFC8032 §5.1.6 dom2(F,C) prefix IS the DST — recommend mandatory not optional not defaulted every protocol-confusion CVE in BLS/Schnorr/h2c literature traces to optional-or-defaulted DST. ENCODING MurmurHash3_32 hash.go lines 73-76 reads little-endian (data[0] low byte) matches reference C impl UNDOCUMENTED in docstring — C# port via BitConverter on big-endian platform would silently disagree, Float64 methods endianness-neutral via bit shifting IEEE754 bit-exact cross-language, StructuralDescriptor format prose-documented not byte-precise — fix add // Encoding: little-endian byte read 2 LOC + for 057 default big-endian RFC convention except where spec mandates otherwise (Ed25519 little-endian per RFC8032), every multi-byte field documents byte-order with spec citation. NAMING today uniform math-style (data,params) ModPow(base,exp,mod) ModInverse(a,mod) MillerRabin(n,k) MurmurHash3_32(data,seed) IsPrime(n) ChineseRemainder(residues,moduli) — correct for math, crypto-stdlib convention is (key,data) ed25519.Sign(privateKey,message) because keys are long-lived data transient — recommend Option B math-style for non-crypto crypto-style for crypto with secret-before-public invariant Sign(priv,msg) Verify(pub,msg,sig) Encrypt(pub,msg) Decrypt(priv,ct). STDLIB COMPARISON borrow from crypto/ed25519 distinct named types GenerateKey returns (pub,priv,error) public-first deliberate Sign/Verify top-level functions not methods, do NOT borrow stdlib redundancy of Sign-as-function-and-method or stdlib choice of []byte instead of named Signature type; from crypto/cipher Seal/Open verb-pair opinionated naming if AEAD ever ships mirror verbatim, dst as first parameter zero-allocation matches CLAUDE.md §3, nonce/IV separate argument never bundled with key; crypto.Signer interface defer until third signature scheme lands per 058. EIGHT 30-LOC FIXES priority — (F1) fix package doc 4 lines explicit non-cryptographic disclaimer SECURITY WARNING highest leverage; (F2) per-function security comment to NewMersenneTwister/NewPCG/NewXoshiro256 3 lines NOT-CSPRNG; (F3) Encoding line to MurmurHash3_32+StructuralDescriptor 2 lines; (F4) Constant-time:NO line to ModPow/ModInverse/ExtendedGCD 3 lines; (F5) Read(p []byte) adapter on three PRNGs 45 lines highest-leverage capability; (F6) package-var allocation of Miller-Rabin witness slices 4 lines; (F7) CLAUDE.md §2 explicit allowance for crypto/subtle 1 sentence; (F8) crypto/ct subpackage wrapping crypto/subtle ~40 LOC SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT pre-057 misuse-resistance change. Total ~100 LOC mostly comments+adapter+ct subpackage net misuse-risk substantial none conflict with 057/058. Non-overlap 056 numerics owned correctness of present functions; 057 missing owned which primitives to add Tier-1/2/3 ladder LOC; 058 sota owned architectural transferability from libsodium/RustCrypto/dalek/arkworks typed-values+canonical-encoding+CT+transcript; 059 owns CURRENT API shape and seam between math-primitives-today and crypto-primitives-tomorrow; defers memory hygiene (zeroize-on-drop) and consumer-migration and cross-language API parity. Bottom line: package today has small defensible API for what it IS (number-theory + non-crypto-hash + deterministic-PRNG kit) single most-load-bearing risk is package-name overpromising F1+F2 doc fixes resolve in 7 lines, second-most is no-CT-primitive-surface and no-io.Reader-precedent both must be settled BEFORE 057 first cryptographic primitive lands otherwise every subsequent primitive inherits or works around wrong shape, F5 PRNG-as-io.Reader is hidden-gem capability making Reality uniquely-positioned for golden-file-tested randomized signatures property no SOTA peer has 058 implicitly counted but did not name — land F1-F8 before first byte of 057 ships. Report at agents/059-crypto-api.md, 315 lines.060 | 2026-05-07 | crypto-perf | reality/crypto today is within 1.5-2× of canonical C reference for all three PRNGs (MT19937-64 ~3-5 ns, PCG-XSH-RR ~1.5-2 ns, Xoshiro256** ~1.5-2.5 ns) and within 1× for FNV/Murmur, but pays a 10-25× tax on every ModPow/Miller-Rabin/NextPrime/CRT because mulmod is Russian-peasant doubling instead of `bits.Mul64`+`bits.Div64` (4-LOC fix already filed by 056 as CRY-MULMOD-OPT — adds upper-bound here: IsPrime on 2^32 from ~30-100 µs to ~3-10 µs, NextPrime on 2^32 from ~2.5 ms to ~250 µs); zero benchmarks across crypto/, zero AllocsPerRun guards (same hole as 005/010/015/020/025/030/045/050/055), 80 LOC of *_bench_test.go would lock the µs/op contract. Forward-looking µs/op budget for 057's primitives splits three ways: (a) Montgomery REDC ~600 ns/256-bit-sqr inside ModPow loops (no DIVQ ever) wins over Barrett ~750 ns/sqr (no fast-mul-form) wins over schoolbook+DIVQ ~3 µs/sqr — but custom Solinas-style reduction for structured moduli BEATS BOTH at 3 ns Goldilocks 2^64-2^32+1 and 5 ns Mersenne-31, so 058 P5/R2 small-field family ships its own Reduce not generic Mont/Barrett; (b) big-int multiply crossover ladder schoolbook ≤ 1024-bit (~50-800 ns 256-1024-bit), Karatsuba 1024-8192-bit (~750 ns-6 µs), Toom-3 8-32 kbit (~5.5-65 µs), NTT 32+ kbit (~180 µs at 32k climbing) — Sequence-A ECC-first ladder (ECDSA/Ed25519/Schnorr/Pedersen/Shamir all ≤512-bit) covers ALL primitives in schoolbook regime, NO Karatsuba/Toom/NTT needed v1, NTT only matters at BLS12-381 base-field+Fp12-tower pairings (Tier-2) and at Kyber/Dilithium polynomial multiply (Tier-3), and 058 R5 NTT-share-with-signal-FFT play means refactor signal/fft.go butterfly skeleton field-generic so signal complex128 and crypto Fp inject inner ops sharing ~200 LOC; (c) constant-time tax mandatory 2× for Montgomery ladder secret-exp (RSA-decrypt/ECDSA-sign/X25519/Paillier-decrypt/ElGamal-decrypt) and 1.3× for fixed-window-with-branch-free-select secret-scalar, both negotiated at API time as `MulCT`/`MulFixedBase`/`MulVartime` triplet per dalek/RustCrypto split — verification (ECDSA-verify/Ed25519-verify/Schnorr-verify/Pedersen-open/Shamir-reconstruct) MAY use variable-time double-and-add because inputs public. Hash throughput class table: SHA-256 software ~250-400 MB/s without SHA-NI assembly, SHA-NI ~2 GB/s; SHA-3-256 ~50-100 MB/s software (sponge over 5x5x64-lane Keccak-f[1600] expensive); BLAKE2b ~400-700 MB/s portable; BLAKE3 ~700 MB/s portable Go (10 GB/s with AVX-512), beats SHA-2 software without ANY assembly; Poseidon2 over Goldilocks ~1-3 µs/permutation. Recommendation: **BLAKE3 as default reality hash** when 057 T1-HASH lands, SHA-2/3 only when FIPS interop demands. Symmetric-cipher class: AES portable Go ~5-10 ns/byte (100-200 MB/s) vs Go crypto/aes with AESNI ~0.3-0.5 ns/byte (2-3 GB/s) vs OpenSSL+VAES ~6-7 GB/s — reality math layer reimplementing AES would always lose to crypto/aes which already has the AESNI assembly path, correctly out-of-scope per 057 (only GF(2^8) S-box AS ALGEBRAIC OBJECT in scope); ChaCha20 portable ~2 ns/byte (500 MB/s) is 10× faster than AES portable so when reality's Poly1305 ~80 LOC over GF(2^130-5) lands, recommend ChaCha20-Poly1305 over AES-GCM for portable-Go. PRNG-as-io.Reader F5 (per 059) is the unique-capability hidden-gem at ~2 GB/s deterministic byte stream (vs crypto/rand getrandom ~4 MB/s small-read), 4-500× faster, unlocks golden-file-tested randomized signatures with bit-exact reproducibility across Go/Python/C++/C# (Python's random.Random().getrandbits(64) matches MT19937-64 stream byte-for-byte) plus property-based fuzz with deterministic replay — property no SOTA peer library has because libsodium/RustCrypto/dalek all use crypto/rand only. Recommended bundle 4 ship-now items 133 LOC: CRY-PERF-1 mulmod bits.Mul64+Div64 4 LOC (10-25× speedup), CRY-PERF-2 *_bench_test.go 80 LOC twelve benchmarks, CRY-PERF-3 package-var Miller-Rabin witness slices 4 LOC (~50 ns per IsPrime), CRY-PERF-4 PRNG io.Reader adapter 45 LOC; plus 4 forward-looking architecture decisions to settle BEFORE respective 057 layer ships: CRY-PERF-5 Montgomery-default+Barrett-fallback+custom-Solinas posture in BIGINT package doc, CRY-PERF-6 MulCT/MulFixedBase/MulVartime triplet for every secret-handling primitive, CRY-PERF-7 NTT-share-with-signal-FFT butterfly skeleton, CRY-PERF-8 BLAKE3-as-default-hash. Non-overlap: 056 owned correctness (filed mulmod fix); 057 owned primitive ladder LOC; 058 owned architectural patterns (this adds the µs/op cost class for each); 059 owned API/doc fixes (this quantifies F5 ~2 GB/s gain and F6 ~50 ns/IsPrime); 060 owns µs/op ladders + Mont/Barrett/Goldilocks tradeoff + multiply crossover + hash throughput class + AES-vs-ChaCha20 portable posture + PRNG vs SOTA + CT-tax quantification + MulCT/MulFixedBase/MulVartime API triplet + NTT-share-FFT cost claim + BLAKE3-as-default claim; defers per-arch assembly, Pippenger MSM, pairing batching, GPU/SIMD (out-of-scope zero-dep), zeroize-on-drop, CT-leakage test harness.
