061 | 2026-05-07 | em-numerics | em/ is a 213-LOC EM-101 cheat-sheet of 11 scalar formulas (Coulomb, Ohm, Power, R-series/parallel, ½CV²/½LI², τ=RC, f=1/(2π√LC)) all arithmetically correct on happy paths and IEEE-754 propagation-clean — no allocations, no state, no FDTD, no superposition, no current loop, no complex impedance, no eigenvalue analysis, none of the topic-prompt cancellation/CFL/elliptic-integral hazards exist in present code because none of the primitives that would expose them yet exist (those are 062). Two real bugs in current surface: E-1 ResistorsInSeries/Parallel use naive `total += r` and `sumInv += 1/r` accumulating O(N·ε) error vs Kahan-Neumaier O(ε), worst-case at n=10^5 mixed-magnitude resistors error climbs to ~1e-11 still inside golden tolerance 1e-10 but eroded — 10-LOC compensated-summation fix; E-3/E-4 docstring gaps where CoulombForce(0,0,0) returns NaN-not-±Inf (0/0 indeterminate when both q=0 and r=0) and OhmsLaw(0,0) returns NaN-not-±Inf, docstring claims "Returns ±Inf if r==0" silently elides the q=0 NaN case — 12-LOC docstring fix. ResonantFrequencyLC at IEEE-754 picks up ~5 ULP via `math.Sqrt(L*C)` and `1/(2π·sqrt)` chain, well inside 1e-12 golden tolerance, no fix needed; Coulomb's-constant precision bounded by VacuumPermittivity 9-sig-fig CODATA-2018 representation (~1e-9 relative) which post-SI-2019 redefinition is the right honest precision floor since ε₀ is no longer exact. Test coverage CRITICAL: 45 golden vectors across 11 functions = 4.1 avg vs CLAUDE.md §1 mandate 20+ each = 20% of mandate (parallel pattern to 056 crypto-numerics 5%); zero functions cover full IEEE-754 mandatory edges (+Inf/-Inf/NaN/-0.0/subnormals), only two cover ONE edge each (R=0 in OhmsLaw unit test, R=0 in resistors_parallel.json) — bundle EM-GOLDEN-1 ~250 LOC JSON to bring all 11 to 20+ vectors + EM-GOLDEN-2 ~80 LOC for 66 IEEE-754 cases (6 per function). SIX forward-looking numerical-correctness commitments must be settled BEFORE 062-missing primitives ship because first-ship binds golden-file algorithm choice cross-language: F-1 multi-source field superposition uses pairwise summation as default with Kahan-Neumaier variant for high-N (Pistachio particle systems) — naive Σ Eᵢ catastrophically cancels at far point with N=10^6 mixed-sign sources where small-difference-of-large-numbers dominates (matches Numerical Recipes §4.6 / Drake / Bullet posture); F-2 FDTD3DStep/2D/1D MUST validate CFL `cΔt/Δx ≤ 1/√d` on entry and error-return on violation NOT silent-clamp (one over-CFL step blows up to Inf within 10-20 timesteps), golden file mandates CFL=1 marginal-stability vector + CFL=1.01 error vector + CFL=0.99 plane-wave-convergence vector; F-3 magnetic field of current loop uses Carlson symmetric form RF/RJ for complete elliptic integrals K(m)/E(m) — uniformly convergent over m∈[0,1), branch-free, ~30 LOC, matches Boost.Math/scipy.special/mpmath/GSL so Python/C++ ports are trivially golden-equivalent; AGM Brent-1976 acceptable, but reject Cephes/Hastings series-with-branch-at-m=0.5 because algorithm-divergent across branch breaks cross-language golden contract; mandates 30 vectors spanning m∈{0,1e-15,1e-6,0.5,0.999,0.9999} with K-divergence-asymptote ½·ln(16/(1-m)) test at m→1; F-4 complex impedance uses native `complex128` not (real,imag) float64 pairs matching signal/control/ convention, ParallelImpedance(Σ 1/Zᵢ)⁻¹ needs Kahan with separate compensators for real and imag parts ~20 LOC; F-5 N-stage LC ladder modes via companion-matrix QR (when linalg/eigen lands) NOT Durand-Kerner which 051 documented degrades to linear convergence on multiple roots (uniform-line ladder has degenerate L=L=L=L → clustered roots → DK error ~ε^(1/m) ~1e-5); F-6 S-parameter S↔Z conversion `Z=Z₀(I+S)(I-S)⁻¹` MUST use linalg.SolveLU(I-S, Z₀(I+S)) not naive matrix-inverse, error-returns when det(I-S)<1e-14 (physically: unity reflection at port). Total recommended bundle 395 LOC ship-now: EM-NUM-1 Kahan 10 LOC, EM-DOC-1 q=0/V=R=0 docstring fixes 12 LOC, EM-GOLDEN-1 vectors-to-mandate 250 LOC, EM-GOLDEN-2 IEEE-754 mandate 80 LOC, EM-DOC-2 ε₀ precision floor at package doc 5 LOC, EM-DOC-3 forward-looking F-1..F-6 spec block 38 LOC. Bottom line: present surface is small/clean/correct with two ~22-LOC fixes; biggest near-term action is golden-file mandate compliance ~330 LOC; six F-* algorithm-choice questions must be answered in 062 design phase to avoid retrofitting corrected algorithms post-release once Python/C++/C# ports crystallize around naive variants. Non-overlap: 062 owns primitive enumeration LOC ladder, 063 owns peer-library architectural patterns (Meep/Lumerical/COMSOL-RF/scikit-rf), 064 owns Go signature shapes, 065 owns per-call cycle counts and SIMD/parallel posture. (4) Yee staggered grid memory layout decision: SIX SoA flat []float64 (Ex Ey Ez Hx Hy Hz each len Nx*Ny*Nz) NOT one AoS struct{Ex...Hz} — Yee places each component at distinct half-integer offset (Ex at i+½/j/k Ey at i/j+½/k Ez at i/j/k+½ Hx at i/j+½/k+½ etc) packing into one struct interleaves physically-distinct sample points and 8× the memory traffic; matches linalg/matrix.go row-major flat-slice convention and 064 A1 [3]float64 by-value but applied to grid-component scale; lock in em/fdtd/doc.go BEFORE golden files generate since byte-order of dumped traces becomes implicit cross-language contract; (5) inner loop must iterate Z (innermost) for ZYX-stride row-major (idx = i*Ny*Nz + j*Nz + k) — k+1 neighbour at offset +1 stride-1 same cache line, j+1 at +Nz, i+1 at +Ny*Nz — wrong-order pays 4-32× slowdown; standard miss; loop comments mandatory; (6) cache blocking 64×64×64 tile fits L3 (~12 MB working set across 6 fields) closes ~2-5× over un-tiled DRAM-bound case ~40 LOC delta tile-size doesnt affect bit-identical correctness golden-file safe; (7) discrete-curl operator matrix-free for FDTD inline NOT assembled CSR — structured Yee 6-point stencil gives matrix-free 3-10× win over CSR-with-indirection-load + CSR consumes 5× more memory + breaks cache blocking; CSR is right for FEM-EM and MoM where unstructured triangular/tet meshes give irregular sparsity; em/fdtd/ matrix-free, em/fem/+em/mom/ via linalg.SparseCSR which doesnt exist yet; (8) MoM impedance-matrix assembly O(N²) at population O(N³) at solve no SIMD escape — RWG basis on N triangles gives N×N dense complex Z each entry ~5 µs (49-pt Gauss × ~100 ns/eval); cost class N=1000 ~8 sec assembly + 0.7 sec LU, N=10000 ~15 min assembly + 11 min solve; MLFMM crossover at N≈3000-5000 per FEKO/HFSS practice; per 063 MLFMM is boundary of what reality should attempt — recommendation ship dense complex MoM only at first with explicit cite-and-skip for MLFMM pointing to FEKO; pre-allocate Z one-shot exploit complex-symmetric Z[m,n]=Z[n,m] for 2× win; (9) MoM solver lane is dense LU NOT sparse direct — topic-prompt phrase slightly misclassifies; sparse direct is right for FEM-EM/FDFD only; document four-way separation MoM-dense-LU vs MLFMM-GMRES vs FEM-sparse-direct vs FDTD-explicit-time-stepping; (10) linalg/ has zero sparse type today (SparseCSR/CSC/COO grep returns no matches matrix.go is dense flat-slice only) — FDFD/FEM-EM cannot ship without ~1250 LOC of linalg/ work (SparseCSR ~150, SpMV ~80, GMRES ~250, MINRES ~150, ILU(0) ~200, SparseDirectLDL ~400); em is on linalg-sparse critical path — flag in MASTER_PLAN.md so em-FDTD agent doesnt sprint past linalg sparse agent; (11) GMRES/MINRES on complex Helmholtz: ILU(0)-preconditioned ~200 iters × ~3 SpMV equiv × ~22 ms/SpMV at N=10⁵ = ~13 sec per frequency point → ~22 minutes per 100-frequency S-param sweep on portable Go — workable for design verification infeasible for inverse-design optimization loops which need 100-1000× throughput → CUDA territory; document cost class; MINRES 1.5× cheaper for Hermitian Helmholtz pick branch by problem class not user choice; (12) frequency-sweep API mandate slice-in-slice-out batched-over-ω as default for every closed-form (ImpedanceBatch/ReflectionCoefficientBatch/ZToGammaBatch/ChainABCDBatch/FriisTransmissionBatch/MicrostripImpedanceBatch) — costs zero extra LOC vs scalar form (one for-range loop) but enables single allocation by caller, compiler auto-vectorisation ~1.5× over per-element call form, amortised cross-language FFI; per-element scalar reserved for unit-test/golden-vector readability; matches signal/fft.go and linalg/matrix.go caller-out-buffer pattern. Decisions to lock at first ship: SoA-flat-[]float64 layout + ZYX-stride/k-innermost convention + matrix-free curl + dense-MoM-only-cite-and-skip-MLFMM + ILU(0)+GMRES default + batched-frequency-sweep variants + GLUPS-portable-Go-bounded documentation. Cost-of-deferring: Python/C++/C# golden files commit to byte-order of dumped FDTD traces once T3 ships; layout flip forces regen across all four languages. Single most-leveraged today: write em_bench_test.go ~30 LOC + add MASTER_PLAN.md note that em T3 blocks on linalg sparse sprint. Single most-leveraged forward-looking: lock SoA layout + k-innermost convention in em/fdtd/doc.go at first FDTD ship both cost-free at ship time both structurally expensive after Python/C++/C# golden files crystallize. Non-overlap: 061 owns CFL precondition algorithm + Kahan summation + complex arithmetic identity preservation + Yee staggering choice + PML stretched-coords; 062 owns primitive enumeration LOC ladder; 063 owns SOTA library architectural patterns and openEMS operator-extension copy + D8 no-SIMD; 064 owns 11 seam decisions on signature shape (Vec3 vs []float64, complex128 native, multi-return shape, error-vs-panic policy) — 065 owns per-call allocation count + GLUPS class + SoA-vs-AoS layout + cache-tile + curl-storage-decision + sparse-substrate-dependency + frequency-sweep batching only. A-6 pipe geometry unstructured -- PipeFlowFriction(Re,roughness,diameter) + DarcyWeisbach(f,L,D,rho,v) take bare floats; no PipeMaterial enum mapping drawn-copper/commercial-steel/cast-iron/concrete/riveted-steel to Moody-1944 default epsilon; Bell ships ~50 pipe-fitting K-factors keyed by named geometry; same parallel for upcoming Channel{Width,Depth,Slope,ManningN} when 067 T1-OPEN-CHANNEL ships. Cross-cutting SOTA agreements reality should adopt: predicates as named separate kernel-tier concern (every SOTA library names its predicate layer; reality buries sign2D in polygon.go); handles-not-pointers for spatial/mesh structures (cache-friendly + GC-friendly + serializable); adaptor/accessor over fixed point-cloud types (only way to zero-alloc tree construction); closed-form distance catalog *before* GJK iteration; smooth-min for CSG not boolean min (already aligned). Three things reality has that SOTA does not: single canonical impl across 4 languages with bit-pinned golden files (CGAL Python binding can give different rounding for same algorithm), allocation-free hot paths as contract (libigl/OpenMesh allocate freely), source citation as queryable metadata. Total 078-driven additions ~1740 LOC across 6 files (predicates_filter.go, distance.go, mesh.go, kdtree.go, gjk.go, transform.go) on top of 076 predicate work, ~50%0f 077 Tier-1 estimate. blocks ICP/Procrustes/Kabsch/OBB landings, ~150 LOC), (3) matrix-application convention (column-vector / left-application is **already locked** by existing `QuatRotateVec(q, v)` and `QuatMul(a, b)` ordering but undocumented — 5 LOC of doc to prevent 18 months of caller confusion when `Mat3` lands), (4) immutability (the package is **accidentally immutable** because Go arrays are value types and `ConvexHull2D` defensively copies its input at polygon.go:78-80; matches CGAL kernel design; make it deliberate via doc.go, 5 LOC), (5) ray-primitive intersection result shape (077: 0 of 8 present; pre-decide `(RayHit, ok bool)` Go-idiomatic tuple over pbrt-v4 `t==Inf` sentinel — `RayHit{T, Point, Normal, UV}` matches stdlib `map[k]v` lookup, ~30 LOC when tier lands), (6) 2D-vs-3D split (keep separate concrete types per dimension — pbrt-v4 / CGAL `_2`/`_3` pattern; generic `Point[N int]` is impossible in Go because no const-generics for stack-allocated fixed-size arrays without boxing, kills Pistachio 60 FPS), (7) named-array vs struct-of-named-fields (named-array `type Point3 [3]float64` wins over struct `{X, Y, Z float64}` because existing code is index-based throughout — 87 occurrences of `p[0]`/`p[1]`/`p[2]` across four source files — and named-array preserves both indexed access AND iteration loops AND distinct-type-checking); the immediate caller-correctness wins are the `TriangleArea2D(ax,ay,bx,by,cx,cy)` (6 args) → `TriangleArea2D(a, b, c Point2)` (3 args) and `PointInTriangle2D(...8 floats...)` → `PointInTriangle2D(p, a, b, c Point2)` (4 args) collapses — the current 8-scalar signature accepts any 8 floats and silently returns wrong answers if `(ax, ay)` and `(bx, by)` are swapped; total ~430 LOC zero-breaking-change migration via deprecation-wrappers, every recommendation strict subset of pbrt-v4 §3.6 + CGAL Kernel Manual; single highest-leverage commit is R1+R3+R4 (name types + document column convention + lock immutability) at ~120 LOC, prerequisite for the 077 spatial-acceleration tier and 078 Eberly distance tier; report at agents/079-geometry-api.md, 266 lines.

080 | 2026-05-07 | geometry-perf | reality/geometry is a **27-function, 656-LOC, zero-benchmark** package with **26 of 27 functions already alloc-free**; the lone exception ConvexHull2D (polygon.go:65-141) does 3 heap allocs/call (input copy + sort closure box + output stack) and pays a sort.Slice closure-dispatch tax of ~3-5 ns/comparison on top of ~3 ns of cross-product math (500.000000 sort wall time is indirect-call overhead — fix is sort.Sort with typed Less or rewrite to Andrew monotone-chain which sorts by lex(x,y) instead of polar-angle, ~2× faster, ~50 LOC replacing ~70). QuatRotateVec is already at scalar parity with Eigen/pbrt-v4 (~6-9 ns at 18 flops, 12 mul + 6 add/sub) — no scalar perf to recover; the **highest-leverage perf find this audit adds is the absence of any batch API** — Pistachio skinning at 10 chars × 10K vertices × 60 FPS = 6M rot/sec scalar-loops to 36-54 ms/frame today vs ~9-12 ms under SoA QuatRotateVecBatchSoA(q, srcX,srcY,srcZ, dstX,dstY,dstZ []float64) which Go 1.22+ auto-vectorises 4-wide AVX2 (~1.5-2 ns/vertex), 3-4× faster than AoS batch (~3-4 ns/vertex), 4-6× faster than scalar; 077-T1 spatial-tier (k-d tree, BVH, Morton) is unfinished and **the workspace contract has not been pre-decided** — getting it wrong locks alloc-on-query into the public API forever; the right shape is nanoflann-style (tree borrows point cloud, KNN takes caller-owned KNNHeap fixed-size struct, BVH ray traversal uses caller-owned [64]int32 stack — log2(10⁹-prim) is 30 so 64 is generous, fully alloc-free in steady state, vs alloc-per-query which gives 10⁵ allocs/frame × 256 B = 256 MB/s GC pressure → 2-5 ms STW pauses every few seconds, frame-rate-killing); SDF inner-loop for marching cubes at 256³ = 16.7M evals/volume currently passes p+center as 56 B by-value stack args per call → 1.6 GB stack traffic per volume — batch siblings SDFSphereBatch(pts []float64 /*xyz*/, center [3]float64, radius float64, out []float64) hoist center to register and AVX2-load 4 voxels per iteration, 1.5-2× speedup; QuatSlerp is 30-40 ns dominated by 4× transcendentals (acos + 3 sin) — QuatNlerp sibling is 8-10× faster at perceptually-equivalent quality for animation use (Bar-Joseph 2010 quality envelope); BezierCubic3D recomputes Bernstein basis per call — BezierCubic3DTessellate(p0,p1,p2,p3, ts []float64, dst [][3]float64) inlined-in-loop saves ~50 µs/frame at 64 samples × 100 curves; AoS [][3]float64 is correct for existing 27-fn surface but k-d tree split-on-axis-k iterates pts[i][k] for varying i + fixed k → AoS strides 24 B (3 of 8 cache lines wasted) vs SoA stride 8 B (zero waste, 10× cache-line savings on build inner loop) — pre-commit PointAccessor3D adaptor with AoSPoints/SoAPoints implementations + dim-specialised constructors NewKDTree3DAoS/NewKDTree3DSoA. **Total fix-set: 14 ranked items, ~1,070 LOC pure additions + 50/70 LOC ConvexHull2D rewrite**, all backwards-compatible: R1 bench_test.go (250 LOC, gates everything), R2 spatial workspace types (80 LOC, future-regression prevention), R3 QuatRotateVec batch siblings (80 LOC, 2-4×), R4 ConvexHull2D Andrew (2×, removes 076-F3 dead-m), R5 PointAccessor3D adaptor (60 LOC), R6 SDF batch siblings (120 LOC, 1.5-2× MC), R7 QuatNlerp (10 LOC, 8-10×), R8 QuatMul batch (30 LOC, 2×), R9 Bezier/CatmullRom tessellate (80 LOC, 3-4×), R10 polygon batch siblings (60 LOC, 3-4×), R11 ConvexHull2DInto in-place (30 LOC), R12 k-d build benchmark (40 LOC), R13 QuatFromEuler batch (50 LOC), R14 AoS/SoA doc (30 LOC). Single highest-leverage commit is R1+R2+R3 in one PR (~410 LOC): names benchmarks, locks the spatial-tier contract, ships the largest measured speedup. Anti-recs: no hand-rolled asm (4-language port contract), no unsafe.Pointer punning (no Python/C# equivalent), no Point[N int] generics (079-B6), no sync.Pool for batch buffers (caller-owned at frame setup is correct), no auto AoS↔SoA conversion (defeats SoA), no parallelism in default API (caller decides). Report at agents/080-geometry-perf.md, ~400 lines.
081 | 2026-05-07 | graph-numerics | reality/graph is a textbook 9-numeric-function package with full test pass and **zero golden-file vectors** (CLAUDE.md §1 violation — every other package ships JSON); topic-prompt items recharacterised against actual code: Dijkstra ±0 is a non-issue at the `Less` predicate level (probe: `+0<-0` is false, `0+(-0)=+0` so dist values never carry negative-zero), but tie-breaking *is* implicit in heap sift order making `prev[]` non-deterministic across input permutations (~5 LOC to add `(dist,node)` secondary key, locks reproducibility for golden files); Floyd-Warshall O(V³) accumulating error is **not** the real hazard (the relaxation is min not sum, error does not accumulate cell-by-cell — V·ε at V=1000 is 2.2e-13, well below any tolerance), the genuine accumulation hazard is mixed-magnitude weights (~1e15 + ~1.0 silently drops the small one) and that is informational not fixable; Bellman-Ford has a real propagation bug — the post-relaxation pass marks one node per negative cycle with -Inf but does **not** propagate -Inf to downstream nodes, so the doc-comment contract `dist[i] == -Inf means reachable via negative cycle` is met for ~1/N of affected nodes (~15 LOC fix: BFS from each marked node); A* doc says admissible but code requires consistent heuristic (line 111 `inClosed` short-circuit prevents reopening — silent sub-optimal paths under merely-admissible heuristics from differential pivots, fix is doc-only ~5 LOC); MaxFlow integer-overflow hazard from prompt **does not apply** (capacities are `map[[2]int]float64`), real hazard is float-residual termination noise where 10×1e-16 = 1e-15 > 0 augments microscopically forever (~30 LOC EPS-ratio param or integer-only sibling); PageRank has **no convergence test** — loop always runs full `iterations` and damping=0.85 needs ~170 iters for 1e-12 (above the doc-recommended 20-100), silent under-convergence with no signal; EigenvectorCentrality has the standard Perron-Frobenius bipartite-graph oscillation hazard (silently returns un-converged iterate after maxIter, NetworkX returns explicit error, fix is `(ranks, converged bool)` return ~3 LOC); KruskalMST uses `sort.Slice` not `sort.SliceStable` so equal-weight edges produce non-deterministic MST across language ports (Python sorted is stable, Go isn't — golden-file portability hazard waiting to happen, ~1-token fix); Louvain modularity gain at `community.go:248` is **deliberately the igraph-simplification not Blondel-2008** (missing constant scaling factor `(2*ki_in - ki[i]^2/m2)/m2`), `argmax` is preserved but document the choice so a future maintainer doesn't "fix" it back; **every numeric function silently propagates NaN/Inf weights** with zero defensive `math.IsNaN(w) || math.IsInf(w,0)` check anywhere in the package — this is a 6-package repeat of the pattern flagged in 006/011/016/021 and is a candidate for a package-wide `internal/numguard` shared helper instead of 22 per-package re-implementations; Graph Laplacian eigenvalues from prompt are **missing entirely** (out-of-axis for numerics slot — note for missing slot 083?). Total fix-set: 11 ranked items, ~540 LOC pure additions + ~100 LOC doc fixes, all backwards-compatible: R1 stable Dijkstra tie-break (5), R2 golden JSON harness (≥80 vectors across Dijkstra/Bellman/Floyd/PageRank, ~400 incl. ±0/±Inf/NaN), R3 Bellman -Inf propagation (15), R4 PageRank+Eigenvector convergence return (20), R5 Kruskal stable sort (5), R6 input-validation pass (30), R7-R11 lower-priority docs/cleanup. Single highest-leverage commit is R1+R2+R3+R4 (~440 LOC) — locks reproducibility and signals all four cases of silent under-convergence. Anti-recs: no Kahan in Floyd inner loop (V³ op cost doubling for 1% pathological case), no Shewchuk predicates (graphs operate on labels not coordinates), no closed-set re-opening in A* (every realistic 2D/3D heuristic is consistent), no integer-only MaxFlow (probability/finance need fractional). Report at agents/081-graph-numerics.md, 245 lines.

topic-prompt status: Dijkstra/A*/Bellman-Ford/Floyd-Warshall/Kruskal/PageRank/Tarjan-SCC **already present**, **missing** are Johnson, Dinic, push-relabel, MinCut-as-edges, Hopcroft-Karp, Blossom, articulation/bridges, k-cores, modularity reader, Leiden, HITS — and Louvain itself is a partial single-pass impl per 081; cross-walk against NetworkX 3.5 / igraph 0.11 / graph-tool 2.84 / Boost.Graph 1.85 / LAGraph 1.1 yields Tier 1 = 14 routines / ~2,300 LOC closing every topic-prompt gap (T1.1 Johnson is the highest leverage at ~80 LOC since both halves Bellman-Ford+Dijkstra already exist, T1.4 Leiden is the must-ship community-detection successor since NetworkX 3.5 added `nx.community.leiden` upstream in 2025), Tier 2 = 18 routines / ~3,500 LOC (push-relabel, Stoer-Wagner, Gomory-Hu, Blossom, Yen-k-shortest, bidirectional Dijkstra, block-cut tree, 2-edge-CC, Borůvka, weighted-Brandes, Katz, k-truss, label-prop, Newman-spectral, Laplacian/Fiedler, diameter/radius/eccentricity/girth), Tier 3 = 23 routines / ~5,500 LOC (Eppstein, VF2, WL/color-refinement, Infomap, effective-resistance, hitting/cover/mixing/heat-kernel, Node2vec, SBM, configuration model, treewidth/treedepth/hyperbolicity, Bron-Kerbosch, GraphBLAS-via-semiring); five Tier-2/3 routines (Fiedler, EffectiveResistance, NewmanSpectral, HeatKernel, SpectralEmbedding) compose on linalg eigen/matrix-exp primitives slot-coordinated elsewhere in the 400-agent grid; CLAUDE.md §2 zero-deps preserved by every routine; sequencing is PR1=Johnson+Modularity+Kosaraju (<300 LOC, zero new infrastructure), PR2=Articulation+Bridges+k-cores+clustering, PR3=Dinic+MinCut, PR4=Hopcroft-Karp+Hungarian, PR5=Leiden, PR6=HITS+Closeness+Harmonic+random-graph generators. Report at agents/082-graph-missing.md, 250 lines.

083 | 2026-05-07 | graph-sota | reality/graph vs NetworkX-3.5/igraph-0.11/graph-tool-2.84/Gephi-0.10/Boost.Graph-1.85/GraphBLAS-LAGraph-1.1/NetworKit-11.0/SNAP-snap.py-6.0/nauty-bliss-2.8/Pregel-Giraph-GraphX: ten reference points cluster into **four design philosophies** that cannot blend without breaking CLAUDE.md §2 (zero-dep) or §3 (no allocs hot path) — (a) "encyclopedia-ergonomics" (NetworkX dict-of-dict-of-dict adjacency makes API read like math at 5-50× slowdown vs C-backed peers; Gephi adds ForceAtlas2 layout via Barnes-Hut quadtree for interactive 10⁵-node viz), (b) "C-kernel + thin language binding" (igraph integer-vector representation everywhere with caller-owned result buffers, NetworKit OpenMP-parallel-by-default, SNAP big-graph paging — 50-500× faster than NetworkX), (c) "type-system as algorithm correctness proof" (Boost.Graph concept templates + visitor pattern with `discover_vertex`/`tree_edge`/`back_edge` hooks; graph-tool C++ Boost types + Python PropertyMap parallel arrays), (d) "linear algebra is the abstraction" (GraphBLAS/LAGraph: every algorithm = sparse matmul on user-chosen semiring, `(min,+)` = Bellman-Ford, `(or,and)` = reachability, `(any,second)` = BFS frontier — wait for linalg/ sparse + semiring before pre-paying ~400 LOC). **Four ports worth doing** ranked by leverage: **P1** Boost.Graph visitor refactor of BFS/DFS (~80 LOC primitive + ~150 LOC of derived algs that close 082-T1.5/T1.10/T2.8/T2.9 at ~30 LOC each instead of ~80 each, the **highest-leverage refactor in the entire graph-package plan**, net ~300 LOC saved across Tiers 1+2); **P2** igraph dense-int vertex-IDs as the documented convention (already de-facto in 13/17 algorithms, promote to *the* convention with one-shot string-intern adapter, ~50 LOC); **P3** graph-tool lazy-Dijkstra primitive with callback that backs SSSP/weighted-Brandes/Closeness/Harmonic/bidirectional (~50 LOC refactor saving ~150 LOC across 4 derived algs); **P4** NetworKit label-setting/label-correcting naming convention in doc-comments (~10 LOC, pure ergonomics). **Five anti-ports**: NetworkX dict-of-dict storage (wrong Go idiom), Boost.Graph full template-concept machinery (no Go equivalent, interfaces cover the use cases), GraphBLAS-internal-to-graph semiring layer (premature, wait for linalg/), Pregel/GraphX distributed BSP runtime (wrong architectural layer, ship the programming model only), full nauty canonicalization at ~3000 LOC (defer; 1-WL color refinement at ~100 LOC covers realistic non-isomorphism witness use case + ML graph-kernel primitive). **Single-algorithm ports flagged**: Gephi ForceAtlas2 layout (~250 LOC including Barnes-Hut quadtree, blocked on 077-T1 quadtree), SNAP ANF approximate-diameter (~120 LOC, belongs in 082-Tier-2 alongside exact `Diameter`), SNAP R-MAT generator (~60 LOC for 082-T1.14 random-graph fixtures), 1-WL color refinement (~100 LOC for 082-T3.3). Cross-cutting SOTA agreements reality should adopt: dense int vertex IDs (already mostly there), parallel arrays indexed by vertex ID for properties (already de-facto in centrality/pagerank.go), label-correcting/label-setting terminology in shortest-path docs, separate Parallel* API surface rather than parallel-by-default. Three things reality has SOTA does not: single canonical impl across 4 languages with bit-pinned golden files (each SOTA library disagrees on tie-breaks), allocation-free hot paths as contract, source citation as queryable metadata. Net algorithmic-LOC saved by P1+P3 refactors across Tier 1+2 of 082's plan: ~450 LOC. Net LOC added for the four ports themselves: ~190. The visitor refactor (P1) is the **single recommendation with highest leverage-per-LOC in the entire graph-package design space** — one BFS implementation backs ~15 derived algorithms in Boost.Graph; the same factoring cuts canonical-algorithm count by ~3× in `reality/graph/` without losing any algorithm. Report at agents/083-graph-sota.md, 158 lines.
084 | 2026-05-07 | graph-api | graph ships 5 representations, 3 vertex-ID conventions, 4 weight-passing conventions, 2 disjoint algorithm namespaces (string Family A vs int Family B) with no bridge, and 0 documentation of weight semantics / sign constraints / self-loop / multi-edge handling on any of 15 weighted algorithms — 8 back-compat recommendations (~250 LOC additive) name the canonical post-cleanup API, fix 2 latent bugs (Louvain self-loop double-count, Betweenness sigma-inflation on multi-edges), and confirm the immutable architecture against 7 anti-ports.
085 | 2026-05-07 | graph-perf | reality/graph is a 17-function 3398-LOC package with **0 Benchmark functions** (matches the repeated repo-wide hole 005/010/.../080) and the single largest perf hit is `IntAdjacency = map[int][]int`: 3-4 random memory loads per neighbour-walk, ~80-120 ns/edge BFS at V=10k vs ~15-25 ns/edge for flat CSR (offsets+columns+weights parallel slices) — empirical 3-5× factor matches igraph's documented CSR-vs-DOK speedup. **Disagreement with 084**: 084 said graph/ shouldn't ship CSR ahead of linalg/ but graph-local CSR is ~80 LOC of adjacency-traversal storage, NOT the same type as future `linalg.SparseMatrix` (which needs masked-matmul kernels graph/ doesn't need); ship graph-local `graph/csr.go` now with one-direction `CSR.ToSparseMatrix() linalg.SparseMatrix` adapter when linalg/ ships. **Per-call alloc audit of all 17 functions**: 14 of 17 are NOT alloc-clean, top-3 worst are Brandes betweenness (5n+1 slices PER source = 5000 mallocs/call at V=1000, fix is hoist scratch out of source-loop ~80 LOC for ~5× speedup), EigenvectorCentrality (allocates `xNew` every iteration — fix is two-buffer ping-pong already correctly used in PageRank, ~10 LOC for maxIter× allocation reduction), LouvainCommunities (`commWeights := make(map[int]float64)` per node per outer-pass = 100k map allocs at V=10k iter=10, fix is scratch `[]float64` indexed by community label + touched-labels list, ~50 LOC for 3-4× speedup). **Heap micro-opt**: `container/heap` interface dispatch in Dijkstra/A* costs 30-40% wall-clock — `heap.Push(x any)` boxes 16-byte `dijkstraItem` to heap (escape verified), 4 vtable indirect dispatches per op; hand-written concrete-type heap (~60 LOC) recovers it; d-ary (d=4) heap is a further 1.2-1.5× win, defer to second pass. **BFS frontier batching is the GraphBLAS-style refactor that needs ZERO sparse infrastructure**: `frontier`/`next` ping-pong slices is exactly `(any, second)` semiring matvec without the matrix machinery — ~30 LOC `BFSLevels` primitive, allocation-free after first iter, parallel-ready (goroutine-shard the inner `for u := range frontier` later). The 5 BFS sites today (`BFSDownstream`/`BFSReachable`/`ConnectedComponents`/`Brandes`/`MaxFlow`) all use `queue[1:]` head-relink which never frees dequeued head — fix is two-pointer head-index ~5 LOC × 5 sites. **Visitor refactor (per 083-P1) is alloc-NEUTRAL only if visitor is interface-typed not closure-typed**: closures capturing local accumulator state heap-allocate the closure frame on Go's escape analysis, interface-typed visitors with concrete struct receivers inline post-Go-1.20 mid-stack inlining — must document this in `graph/visitor.go` BEFORE 083-P1 lands or every downstream BFS-derived alg inherits closure-alloc shape. **Parallel BFS / Pregel scope decision**: NO — CLAUDE.md golden-file determinism contract makes goroutine-parallel discovery-order-non-deterministic; document frontier-batched skeleton as parallel-ready, defer goroutine variant until application-level concurrency demand surfaces. **GraphBLAS verdict**: 083 said wait for linalg/sparse — agree for Bellman-Ford `(min,+)` matvec which needs masked semirings (~600 LOC), but PageRank/BFS/connected-components-label-prop are GraphBLAS-shape ALREADY and CSR refactor delivers 3-5× without naming the abstraction. **Three more inner-loop wins**: PageRank dangling-set is invariant across iterations (cache once, ~10 LOC for 1.1-1.2× win at V=1M), FloydWarshall flat `[]float64` not `[][]float64` (manual `i*n+j` indexing for 2-3× cache win, modest API churn), MaxFlow `appendUnique` is O(d²) defensive dedup that costs more than it saves on dense graphs (BFS already checks `parent[v] != -1` so duplicates are harmless — drop with regression test, -10 LOC). **Iterative Tarjan SCC**: current recursion can hit Go runtime.morestack 8-12× on deep-chain graphs at V=100k; ~80 LOC iterative Pearce-2005 fix removes stack-grow risk + 1.3× speedup, defer until needed. **Sprint-1 (~325 LOC, zero math change, all golden files unchanged)**: graph_bench_test.go + heap-fix + Brandes-fix + Eigen-fix + BFS-deque + BFSLevels — closes the four worst alloc patterns + locks µs/op floor. **Sprint-2 (~480 LOC)**: graph/csr.go + DijkstraCSR/BFSCSR/PageRankCSR + Louvain-scratch + PageRank-dangling-cache — the 3-5× traversal speedup is the single biggest perf win available to graph/, full stop. **Single most-leveraged commit today**: P-1 bench file (~150 LOC) — every subsequent perf PR needs a hard µs/op floor to prove against, otherwise P-2…P-12 land "improvements" nobody can verify. **Single most-leveraged forward-looking lock**: document frontier-batched BFS as canonical skeleton in `graph/doc.go` BEFORE 083-P1 visitor primitive lands; if visitor lands first with single-node-dequeue semantics, ~15 derived BFS algs (closeness, harmonic, bidirectional, weighted-Brandes per 083-P3) inherit the slower shape and cleanup is N× more expensive. Non-overlap: 081 owns numerics + golden files, 082 owns Tier-1/2/3 missing primitives, 083 owns SOTA-port architectural patterns + visitor recommendation, 084 owns API/contract + representation cleanup — 085 owns ONLY per-call alloc count + heap-impl + frontier shape + parallel-scope-decision + GraphBLAS-readiness without naming. Report at agents/085-graph-perf.md, ~280 lines.

086 | 2026-05-07 | info-numerics | topic asks about entropy/KL/MI/Bhattacharyya/LSE/Kozachenko-Leonenko in `reality/info/` but `info/` actually contains only `lz/` (LZ76 complexity, ~470 LOC) and `mdl/` (NML+BIC+AIC codelengths, ~370 LOC) — checklist primitives live in `infogeo/fdiv.go` (KL/JS/TV/Hellinger/ChiSquared/Renyi, nats) and `compression/entropy.go` (Shannon/Joint/Conditional/MI/KL/CrossEntropy, bits) instead. **`info/lz/` is numerically clean** (pure int LZ76 + symbolisation, two strands of dead-code defensive guards on the `log_A(n)` divide path that are unreachable because `n>=10` floor enforces `log(n)>0`, one silent-NaN-→-bin-0 gotcha in `SymbolizeByQuantile` where filtered NaN positions read as low-rank bin 0 indistinguishable from true low values, mixed-arithmetic 10%-NaN cap should be integer `10*invalid > len(returns)` not float comparison). **`info/mdl/` has ONE real numerics bug: `computeCn2` docstring claims safe to `n=1e6` but central-binomial peak `~2^n/sqrt(n)` overflows float64 at `n>=1024`** — implementation lifts log-sum-exp accumulator back into linear space via `exp(maxLog) * sum` for the recurrence step, so result is `+Inf` for `n>=1024` despite docstring promise; fix is either keep recurrence in log-space (~25 LOC) or amend docstring to `n<=700`. Otherwise `mdl/` is clean: `GaussianCodeLength` no catastrophic-cancellation (mean is hypothesised not estimated), `BICShape`/`AICShape` correctly clamp NaN/Inf NLL → `+Inf`, `UniversalIntegerCodeLength` `log*(n)` iteration converges in ~6 steps and rejects `n<1`, `SelectMDL` correctly REFUSES to argmin over NaN/Inf inputs (rare and admirable). **Cross-cutting issues across `info/`+`infogeo/`+`compression/`**: (1) `compression/entropy.go` does ZERO input validation while `infogeo/fdiv.go` validates strictly via `validatePair` — KL/CrossEntropy on length-mismatched inputs silently return 0 instead of erroring, malformed distributions with negative entries flow through silently. (2) **Two unit conventions** — `compression/` uses bits (`Log2`), `infogeo/` uses nats (`Log`), no documented design rationale. (3) **No canonical `LogSumExp` helper** — only inline at `mdl/nml.go:142-156`, every other site that needs it (Renyi, softmax in autodiff/, importance sampling in copula/) reimplements. (4) **No pinned tests for absolute-continuity `+Inf` boundary** — KL/CrossEntropy/ChiSquared/Renyi(α>1) all promise `+Inf` when `p>0, q=0` but no test pins it under refactors. **Topic-checklist primitives MISSING ENTIRELY from repo**: Bhattacharyya distance (zero hits in source — ~15 LOC trivial via `H² = 1 - BC` from existing Hellinger), Miller-Madow MI bias correction (zero hits — plug-in MI in `compression/` is the only MI estimator and has known `(K-1)/(2N)` bias documented nowhere — ~20 LOC fix), Kozachenko-Leonenko differential entropy (zero hits — substantial gap given package named `info/`, ~200 LOC for kNN estimator with digamma correction), Renyi entropy as opposed to divergence (~5 LOC gap), generic f-divergence with caller-supplied `f` (API gap not numerics). **Renyi divergence has TWO concrete numerics issues**: (a) docstring promises `α→0⁺` (`-log Σ_{i:p_i>0} q_i`) and `α→∞` (`log sup p/q`) limit cases but implementation rejects `α<=0` and infinite α; (b) `math.Pow(p, α) * math.Pow(q, 1-α)` is not log-stable — for `α>>1` and `q<<1` the `q^(1-α) = q^(-large)` overflows; rewrite as `exp(α log p + (1-α) log q)` with LSE accumulation (~5 LOC change, ~20 LOC including tests). **Conditional entropy in `compression/entropy.go:69-80`** uses subtraction form `H(X,Y) - H(X)` which catastrophically cancels when X and Y are nearly deterministic functions of each other (`H(X,Y) ≈ H(X)`); add direct `Σ p(x) Σ p(y|x) log p(y|x)` form (~25 LOC) for that regime, keep subtraction as faster path. **Sprint-1 recommendation (~190 LOC)**: Bhattacharyya + MillerMadow-MI + ConditionalEntropyDirect + LogSumExp helper + Renyi-LSE-rewrite + RenyiEntropy + Renyi α→0/α→∞ limits + `compression.validatePair` + `mdl/nml.go` log-space recurrence (or docstring amend) — closes the headline gaps without changing existing semantics. Sprint-2 (~250 LOC) adds Kozachenko-Leonenko + +Inf-boundary pin tests + bits/nats convention doc. Single most-leveraged commit: `infogeo.Bhattacharyya` is missing, ~15 LOC, derivable from existing Hellinger via `H² = 1 - BC` one-liner — closes the most-cited info-theoretic distance the repo currently lacks. Report at agents/086-info-numerics.md, ~290 lines.

Single highest-leverage commit: **Tier-1 batch alone** closes 15 textbook primitives at ~575 LOC because each is shorter than the existing `Renyi` impl in `infogeo/fdiv.go` and most reuse the existing `validatePair` helper. 088 | 2026-05-07 | info-sota | SOTA library deep-dive across JIDT/IDTxl/dit/NPEET(+LNC)/pyitlib/infomeasure/PyPhi/POT/mpmath, filtered by zero-dep portability to pure-Go `reality`. **Headline ranking by leverage-per-LOC into repo**: (1) `Digamma` special function (~40 LOC, mpmath-reference via `math/big`) is the single highest-leverage commit because it unblocks ALL of KSG-1 MI, Kozachenko-Leonenko entropy, mixed-MI Gao-2017, Frenzel-Pompe conditional MI, transfer entropy — ~1,150 LOC of downstream estimators that share the `digamma(k) - digamma(n_x) - digamma(n_y) + digamma(N)` signature; without it none can be ported, with it all are mechanical. (2) k-d tree with L∞ Chebyshev metric (~400 LOC, JIDT-reference) — KSG-family backbone, may already partially exist in `geometry/`. (3) NPEET is the **highest-portability source** at ~250 LOC of pure-math KSG-1/KSG-2 (delegates kNN to `cKDTree` so the math is uncluttered); replace `cKDTree` with native k-d tree and the entire NPEET bundle ports in ~300 LOC of Go. (4) JIDT-reference circular-shift permutation surrogate driver (~150 LOC) — preserves marginals, is the correct null for TE specifically, plugs into every estimator. (5) infomeasure (Sci. Rep. 2025) contributes the **return-shape refactor** (return `(scalar, []localValues)` per estimator, ~0 net LOC) — the local pointwise array is what enables p-value machinery without re-running the estimator. (6) POT log-stabilised Sinkhorn (~30 LOC over existing `optim/transport/sinkhorn.go`) unlocks `reg→0` regime via three-layer log-space + absorption + lazy-kernel trick. (7) NPEET_LNC local-non-uniformity correction (~50 LOC over KSG-1) halves bias for strongly-dependent variables — turn on by default. (8) Williams-Beer PID with `I_min` + `I_∩^MMI` (~450 LOC, dit-reference, closed-form) — `I_BROJA` deferred until repo has convex optimiser. (9) PyPhi small-Φ IIT-4.0 capped at N≤6 (~700 LOC) — feasible only at small N because PyPhi's memoisation+cut-cache caches are *feasibility requirements not optimisations*; document the cap, ship anyway. (10) dit `Distribution` algebra (`marginal`/`condition`/`coalesce`, ~300 LOC) — not needed for any single measure but the right abstraction for the next 20. **Anti-recommendations** (do NOT port): JIDT GPU CUDA, IDTxl joblib (use goroutines), PyPhi Redis cache, dit `scipy.optimize` BROJA, POT PyTorch/JAX backends, infomeasure pandas ergonomics. **Reference-vector availability** for golden files: dit `tests/distributions/` (~200 discrete distributions), JIDT `demos/data/` (~30 AR/TE datasets), NPEET `test.py` (~10 KSG cases), infomeasure (coupled-Lorenz benchmark + ~50 cases, only library with both discrete and continuous in one suite), PyPhi (~5 canonical small-Φ), POT (~20 closed-form Gaussian Wasserstein-2), mpmath (`mp.dps=50` recomputed on demand → unlimited). dit + JIDT + NPEET + mpmath cover ~95% of needed reference values across 087's Tier-1/2/3. **Cross-cutting tricks worth borrowing**: NPEET's `noise=1e-10` jitter for tied-value de-duplication (production-critical reproducibility one-liner), JIDT's single multivariate k-d tree built once over joint embedding `(X_past, Y_past, Y_future)` and reused across the full TE computation (drops `O(K·N²)` to `O(K·N log N)`, package-defining v1.1 speedup), pyitlib's missing-data jointly-excluded semantics (the difference between using a library and writing your own `H`), infomeasure's unified discrete+continuous estimator API selecting backend by dtype, dit's redundancy-lattice built once and reused across all PID measures, POT's lazy `K @ b` kernel evaluation (O(N²) work, O(N) memory). **Bits-vs-nats convention** across SOTA: dit/pyitlib/infomeasure default bits, JIDT default nats, NPEET nats; matches `reality`'s split (`compression/` bits, `infogeo/` nats) — recommend documenting both surfaces with `Bits`/`Nats` suffix per 086. Report at agents/088-info-sota.md, 398 lines.

089 | 2026-05-07 | info-api | Six API ergonomic divergences across `compression/` (bits, no validation), `infogeo/` (nats, validated), and `prob/distribution.go` (continuous via `Distribution` interface). **Probability vs samples:** today every primitive takes a pmf — no `EntropySamples` exists — but consumers (Sensorhub/Nexus/RubberDuck) hand-roll histograms; recommend explicit estimator family `EntropyPlugin` / `EntropyMLE(samples, opts)` / `EntropyKL(samples, k)` / `EntropyAnalytical(Distribution)` so the function name encodes the estimator and pre-binned probs path stays alongside the sample path. **Discrete vs continuous:** keep separate, encode in suffix (`MLE` / `Vasicek` / `KL` / `Continuous`) — matches existing `KLDivergence` vs `KLDivergenceNumerical` split; reject runtime-type-switch unification (interface{} input is a math-library anti-pattern). **Histogram density:** make explicit, return a `Histogram` type with named bin rules `HistogramScott`/`HistogramFD`/`HistogramSturges`/`HistogramFixed`; the histogram is reusable across Entropy/MI/KL and preserves N for bias correction (which collapsing to probs destroys). **Bits-vs-nats convention is THE silent-bug class:** `compression/` returns bits via `Log2`, `infogeo/` returns nats via `Log`, only `info/mdl/` ships both via `Bits` suffix; cross-package import of `compression.KLDivergence` and `infogeo.KL` differ by `log(2) ≈ 0.693` invisibly. **Mandatory `Bits`/`Nats` suffix on every function**, no global default, ~30 LOC diff, regenerates 6 golden files — single highest-leverage commit because it is mechanical and locks in before new estimators land. **Bias correction pluggability:** none today (plug-in `ShannonEntropy(probs)` cannot apply Miller-Madow because it has no N); recommend `EntropyOpts{Bias: BiasCorrection, BinRule, NBins, Unit}` with enum `BiasNone|BiasMillerMadow|BiasJackknife|BiasNSB|BiasGrassberger` — enum not interface (five canonical correctors are textbook, not user-injectable), ~150 LOC for all five inline. **KL signature `KL(p, q) = KL(p || q)` is uniform** across `compression/` and `infogeo/` and matches SciPy/dit/JIDT; only documentation gap is `infogeo/` does not state asymmetry (compression line 130 does, infogeo line 56 does not) — recommend package-level doc comment in `infogeo/fdiv.go` declaring p=data, q=reference/model convention plus forward/reverse VI naming. **Joint `[][]float64` shape:** keep for bivariate but tighten validation (currently `MutualInformation` accepts ragged matrices via maxCols defensively at line 107-111 — wrong); add `[][]int` counts variants for bias correction (counts preserve N); for ≥3 variables in v2 use flat `Tensor{Data, Dims}` not nested `[][][]float64`. **`Distribution` interface for info functions:** today only `KLDivergenceNumerical` consumes it; expand to `EntropyContinuous` / `KLContinuous` / `JSContinuous` / etc. (~60 LOC, parallel surface to discrete); closed-form pairs (Normal-Normal KL, Beta entropy) as methods on concrete types `(d *NormalDist) KLTo(other *NormalDist)` not free functions — keep `Distribution` interface narrow (PDF/CDF only). **Package home:** promote discrete info surface to new `reality/info/entropy.go`+`info/divergence.go`+`info/histogram.go`+`info/bias.go`+`info/options.go`, leave `compression/` and `infogeo/` re-exporting aliases for back-compat — `info/` is what 087 Tier-1/2/3 needs as a home and what `info/lz` + `info/mdl` already established. **Single highest-leverage NEW infrastructure:** the `EntropyOpts` / `Histogram` / `BinRule` / `BiasCorrection` types in `info/options.go` — these are the *contract* every sample-based estimator from 087 Tier-2/3 will share; define them before any estimator lands so each does not invent its own option struct. Report at agents/089-info-api.md, ~360 lines.



090 | 2026-05-07 | info-perf | Perf audit of `info/`+`infogeo/`+`compression/` (38 funcs). **Allocation table**: 14/38 zero-alloc hot paths (37%); 5 O(n^2) or worse (LZ76, MMD biased/unbiased, Mahalanobis, MedianHeuristic). **Worst existing offenders**: `lz.SymbolizeByQuantile` allocates O(log n) recursive mergesort buffers per call (replace with `slices.SortStableFunc`, -40 LOC); `lz.RollingComplexity` allocates ~3*windows (e.g. 2700 allocs for n=1000/win=100/step=1) — `RollingComplexityInto` with shared scratch is highest-leverage existing-code win at ~30 LOC; `infogeo.MedianHeuristicBandwidth` full-sorts O(N^2) distances for the median (use quickselect, ~50 LOC); `infogeo.Bregman` allocates `gradY` per call inside mirror-descent inner loops (~10 LOC scratch fix); `infogeo.MahalanobisSquared` allocates `d` and reimplements GEMV (route to existing linalg blocked GEMV, ~15 LOC); `compression.MutualInformation` / `ConditionalEntropy` allocate marginals every call (`Into` variants for Pistachio 60 FPS path, ~30 LOC). **Forward-looking** (Tier-2/3 from 087): (1) **shared k-d tree in `geometry/`** with L-infinity Chebyshev metric (~400 LOC) is the single highest-leverage infrastructure commit because it services KSG-1, KSG-2, KL-kNN entropy, MIXED-KSG, Frenzel-Pompe conditional MI, KSG-TE, AIS — five-to-seven estimators share one backend, the JIDT v1.1 package-defining trick. Coordinate with 077 geometry T1-4 — info and computational geometry both need it; build once, share. Worst-case O(N^2) is theoretical; for d<=20, N<=1e6 it behaves O(N log N). Brute-force fallback for d>20 or N<1000. Single arena `make([]node, 2N)`, fixed-size kNN heap reused across queries. (2) **LZ76 suffix-automaton refactor** O(n^3/log n) -> O(n) via Crochemore-Verin 1997 (~250 LOC, 10000x speedup at n=10k cap, lifts `LZ76MaxSymbols` to 1e6); single flat `[]samNode` arena allocation. (3) **Canonical `LogSumExp` helper** (~20 LOC promoted from inline `info/mdl/nml.go:142-156`) plus **online streaming LSE** with `partialSum *= exp(oldMax-newMax)` rescale on new max (~30 LOC, used by Bayesian online updates and importance sampling, currently zero in repo). Two-pass max-then-exp is correct; fused single-pass is numerically worse. Go does not auto-vectorise `math.Exp`; accept ~3 ns/element scalar and document — do NOT add asm files (violates spirit of "only stdlib math" CLAUDE.md rule). (4) **`Histogram{Counts, Edges, NSamples}` type** with `HistogramScott` (O(n)), `HistogramSturges` (O(1)), `HistogramFD` (O(n) via twin quickselect for IQR — NOT full sort which is the perf landmine for n=1e6: 30 ms vs 3 ms), pre-sized struct reused across Sensorhub frames, ~80 LOC. (5) Vasicek univariate diff-entropy is a single sort + strided spacing scan (~5 ns/elem with `slices.Sort`); ship `VasicekInto(sortedSamples, m)` zero-alloc + `Vasicek(samples, m)` allocating wrapper. (6) PID `I_min` lattice computed once per `n` and cached at package init for n<=4 (Bell(4)=166 antichains); ship `ErrPIDDimTooLarge` for n>=5. **Cross-cutting principles**: codify `XxxInto(..., scratch)` suffix convention (already used by `ScalarQuantize`/`ScalarDequantize`); add `BenchmarkXxx_Allocations` CI pattern with `b.ReportAllocs()` failing the build for any function the consumer calls inside `Update()` allocating more than docstring states (the only way the no-alloc rule survives 50+ contributors); FMA `vfmaddNNNsd` should be firing in `KL`/`CrossEntropy`/`MMD` inner loops on Go 1.18+ — verify with `go tool objdump`; **flat `Tensor{Data, Rows, Cols}` shape** beats nested `[][]float64` by 1.5-3x on cold cache (pointer-chase per row), recommend for the v2 >=3-variable surface to share with 089-API `Tensor` proposal. **SIMD verdict**: ignore for v1.0; let downstream fork specific functions into asm if they need it — this is the crypto/sha256 stdlib pattern. **Sprint-1 batch (items 2-9)**: ~260 LOC of scratch-buffer + sort-replacement boilerplate closes every existing alloc gap. **Single highest-leverage**: shared k-d tree (~400 LOC), unblocks ~5 KSG-family estimators. Report at agents/090-info-perf.md, ~340 lines.

091 | 2026-05-07 | infogeo-numerics | **Headline: requested scope (Fisher info matrix conditioning, geodesic ODE drift, exp/log map precision, natural gradient G⁻¹∇L, Christoffel symbols, α-connections) is NOT implemented in `infogeo/`** — package contents are f-divergences (KL, JS, TV, Hellinger, χ², Rényi-α), Bregman divergences (sq Euclidean, gen-KL, Itakura-Saito, Mahalanobis), and MMD². Naming-vs-scope hazard: downstream consumers seeing `infogeo` will assume manifold/Fisher machinery is present. **Single highest-leverage commit (5 LOC):** narrow doc.go:1 to "Package infogeo implements information-theoretic divergences on discrete probability vectors. Despite its name, does NOT yet implement the Riemannian-manifold side." **Latent numerical bug found:** `Renyi(p, q, α)` uses `math.Pow(pi,α)·math.Pow(qi,1-α)` (fdiv.go:221) — for α=10, pi=1e-6, `pi^10=1e-60` underflows; if every term flushes to 0 the function returns +Inf via the `sum<=0` branch (fdiv.go:223) when correct answer is finite `(LSE)/(α-1)`. **Recommendation:** rewrite to log-sum-exp `xs[i] = α·log(pi) + (1-α)·log(qi); Renyi = LSE(xs)/(α-1)` (~15 LOC) — closes underflow, also faster (one exp+one log per term vs two Pow). **Renyi docs/code mismatch**: doc-comment lists α=0 (Hartley), α=∞ (max-divergence) limits but the function rejects them at `alpha <= 0` check (fdiv.go:200) — implement the limits OR tighten doc to "α ∈ (0,1)∪(1,∞), open at both ends." **KL cancellation regime**: `p=(0.5+ε,0.5−ε), q=(0.5−ε,0.5+ε)` gives KL≈4ε² with two summands cancelling from O(ε) to O(ε²); for ε=1e-7 answer is ~4e-14, within OOM of float64 ε. Add a golden vector here so future refactors can't silently regress (1 line). **Bregman generic cancellation**: `D_φ(x,y) = φ(x) − φ(y) − <∇φ(y), x−y>` (bregman.go:44) loses ~16 bits per coord at `||x−y||=1e-8` — three terms equal in magnitude, answer is small surviving quantity; Taylor mid-point Hessian formulation is the textbook fix but out of scope for v1, just doc the sharp edge. **Itakura-Saito**: `math.Log(x/y)` for x/y near 1 loses precision; use `math.Log1p((x-y)/y)` for `|x-y|/y < 1e-8` (~3 LOC); test `TestItakuraSaito_ZeroAtEqual` only exercises x==y exactly, off-by-1ULP not covered. **MahalanobisSquared**: no PD check on M; for κ(M)>1e8 ~half precision lost silently; offer `MahalanobisSquaredCholesky(x, y, L)` variant computing `||L^T·d||²` (purely additive, no cancellation, also faster) ~20 LOC. **MMD2Biased** does NOT clamp at 0 (theoretically squared RKHS norm so ≥0, but for N=10⁶ rounding ~N²·ε=1e-4 can drag negative); Kahan-compensated summation is the textbook fix at exactly this regime (~30 LOC). **MMD inner loop calls k(xi,xj) for both (i,j) and (j,i)**: Gaussian kernel is symmetric — 2× wasted work; compute upper triangle, double, single diagonal pass for k(xi,xi)=1 (~15 LOC, halves accumulator rounding). **MedianHeuristicBandwidth perf cliff**: full O(N²) pairwise distances then **insertion sort** (median.go:191-208) — astronomically wrong for N>100; quickselect for the median or `slices.Sort` (~50 LOC). **Autodiff KL parity test (`autodiff_test.go:39-101`) is the strongest numerical assertion in package** — pins ∇_θ KL(p||softmax(θ)) = q−p closed form against reverse-mode AD at 1e-9 per coord (R-CLOSED-FORM-PINNED-TO-AUTODIFF pattern, saturation 2/3); indirectly validates every KL code path EXCEPT pi=0 / qi=0 boundary branches (test inputs all strictly interior); recommend adding θ=(0,0,0,-50) case where q[3]=exp(-50)/Z is denormal-adjacent (~10 LOC). **Forward-looking when Fisher/geodesics/α land**: exp-family Fisher first (closed form ∂²A/∂θ², no drift); non-exp Fisher singular at boundary → ship with `cond(F)` query, let downstream pick Tikhonov regularisation; geodesic RK4 drifts off manifold (Poincaré disk: ||θ||<1, RK4 steps past 1) — needs **symplectic Verlet on Hamiltonian H=0.5·g^{ij}·p_i·p_j** which doesn't exist in `chaos/` yet (~80 LOC cross-cutting); cheaper alternative is manifold-projection retraction (~20 LOC) for v1. Exp/log map near antipode (||v||→π in multinomial Fisher-Rao parametrisation): catastrophic cancellation between `sqrt(p_i)·cos(||v||)` and `(v_i/2π)·sin(||v||)` — use `cos(π-x) = -cos(x)` reduction when ||v||>π/2 (~5 LOC). Natural-gradient G⁻¹∇L: don't explicitly invert — use **CG with G·v matvec products** (Martens 2010 Hessian-Free, doable as JVPs in autodiff without forming G) plus Levenberg-Marquardt-damped `(G+λI)δ=∇L` reusing `optim/` trust-region machinery (~50-150 LOC). Christoffel symbols on simplex closed form `Γ^k_{ij} = -0.5·δ^k_i·δ^k_j·(1/p_k)` — only issue is boundary p_k→0 (same singularity as nat-grad blowup). α-divergence `D_α = (1/α(1-α))(1 - sum p^α·q^{1-α})` shares the same Pow-underflow hazard as Rényi-α — lift the LSE fix simultaneously. **Dual flatness gives free win**: α=±1 are flat in their respective coordinates, geodesics are STRAIGHT LINES (θ_t = θ_0 + tv, no ODE, no drift) — ship α=±1 geodesics as v1 minimum, covers majority of practical use because exp-family natural-parameter geodesic IS the α=1 geodesic. Cross-package: no tie-in to `prob.Distribution` for FIM-as-method (mirror existing KLDivergence/KLDivergenceNumerical split); no tie-in to `linalg.CholeskySolve` for nat-grad solves; no tie-in to `chaos/` ODE solvers (which lack symplectic). **Sprint-1 batch (recs 1+9+10):** ~70 LOC closes one perf cliff, doubles MMD throughput, removes largest doc-vs-impl gap. Report at agents/091-infogeo-numerics.md, 331 lines.

092 | 2026-05-07 | infogeo-missing | reality/infogeo currently ships f-divergences + Bregman + MMD² (1,373 LOC, 4 files) and is missing the *entire* Riemannian / dual-geometry side of its own name — no Fisher info, no Christoffels, no e/m coords, no e/m projections, no α-connections, no exp/log map, no parallel transport, no curvature, no natural-gradient stepper, no Stein discrepancy, no SVGD, no JKO, no Wasserstein gradient flow, no SPD/sphere/hyperbolic/Stiefel/Grassmann manifold; 091's headline "naming-vs-scope hazard" stands and this audit names the closure path. **Tier 1 (11 must-ship primitives, ~700 LOC, zero blockers if scoped to closed-form simplex+Gaussian):** T1.1 FisherRaoSimplex (25 LOC, diagonal in √p coords), T1.2 FisherRaoGaussian (80 LOC, Mardia-Kent-Bibby block-diagonal Σ⁻¹ + ½D^T(Σ⁻¹⊗Σ⁻¹)D form via existing linalg.Cholesky), T1.3 generic Fisher via score (closed-form on exp-family = ∂²A/∂θ², MC fallback on Distribution interface — closed-form path blocked on prob.Distribution growing LogPDF + ScoreAt ~30 LOC), T1.4 Christoffel symbols closed-form on simplex (-½δ^k_iδ^k_j/p_k per Amari 2016 §2.4) and Gaussian (Calvo-Oller 1991), T1.5 AlphaDivergence (35 LOC, **lift the LSE fix from 091 R3 simultaneously** — same `p^α q^{1-α}` underflow), T1.6 e/m coordinate conversion (multinomial: e=log(p_i/p_n) ↔ m=p_i; Gaussian: e=(Σ⁻¹μ, -½Σ⁻¹) ↔ m=(μ, Σ+μμ^T) — 120 LOC closed-form invertible), T1.7 e-geodesic + m-geodesic (**straight lines in dual-flat coords, NO ODE, NO symplectic — closes the geodesic story for ~80% of practical use** — 60 LOC over T1.6), T1.8 e-projection + m-projection with Csiszár-Amari Pythagorean theorem (150 LOC, generic over a Manifold interface that ships only for exp-family + mixture submanifolds at v1), T1.9 NaturalGradientStepper via Cholesky of Tikhonov-damped F (80 LOC; CG-via-JVP variant deferred to v2), T1.10 JKO scheme on simplex composing existing transport.Sinkhorn + proximal.FBS (60 LOC of glue — closes the v2-deferred line in optim/transport/doc.go:108-110), T1.11 KernelSteinDiscrepancy reusing existing mmd.go kernel infrastructure (70 LOC). **Tier 2 (12 primitives, ~1,200 LOC, lifts the package to Geomstats-shaped contract):** T2.1 `Manifold` / `RiemannianMetric` interface (the Geomstats contract: Project / InnerProduct / Exp / Log / GeodesicDistance / ParallelTransport — ~80 LOC, paired with 094-API), T2.2 closed-form exp/log per manifold with **091 R cos(π-x) reduction at ||v||>π/2** for simplex Fisher-Rao antipode-cancellation, T2.3 SPD manifold under affine-invariant metric Pennec-Fillard-Ayache 2006 (**blocked by linalg.MatrixExp / linalg.MatrixLog ~300 LOC Higham 2008 squaring-and-scaling — the next cross-package ticket**), T2.4 sphere S^{n-1} (the "hello world" manifold, ~80 LOC, validates interface shape), T2.5 hyperbolic Lorentz + Poincaré ball (Ungar 2008 + Nickel-Kiela 2017, ~150 LOC, ship both with closed-form bijection), T2.6 Stiefel + Grassmann via Edelman-Arias-Smith 1998 (~250 LOC, both reuse linalg.QR/SVD already in repo), T2.7 parallel transport (closed-form per manifold + Schild's-ladder generic Lorenzi-Pennec 2014 fallback), T2.8 geodesic ODE shooting (**blocked by chaos.Verlet symplectic integrator ~80 LOC — RK4 drifts off the manifold per 091 §2.2**) + boundary-value Newton on Exp_p(v)-q=0 reusing optim/, T2.9 curvature tensor (closed form on simplex = constant +1/4 sectional curvature in √p coords, closed form on Gaussian Skovgaard 1984 mixed signs, generic numerical blocked by autodiff HVP), T2.10 mirror descent composing existing Bregman infrastructure (40 LOC over Bregman + gradPhiStar — same numerical content as natural-gradient when φ is FIM-derived), T2.11 EM in IG-form as alternating e-projection / m-projection per Amari-Kurata 1995 + Csiszár-Tusnády 1984 (80 LOC over T1.8 — re-derives EM from IG primitives, the package's pedagogical demo), T2.12 SVGD particle-VI with Liu-Wang 2016 update (80 LOC, uses T1.11 + autodiff scores). **Tier 3 (13 frontier/specialist primitives):** continuous-time Wasserstein gradient flow (Otto 2001 PDE), sliced-W on Stiefel/Grassmann (lives in transport/ not infogeo/), Hessian-free / K-FAC / CG natural gradient (Martens 2010 + Martens-Grosse 2015), Fréchet mean (Karcher 1977), Fréchet variance, Riemannian / tangent PCA (Fletcher-Lu-Pizer-Joshi 2004 over linalg.PCA + Tier 2 manifolds), α-projection (general α, generalises T1.8), Bures-Wasserstein metric on SPD (Bhatia-Jain-Lim 2019, *different* metric on SPD than T2.3 affine-invariant — ship both, document when), second-order natural gradient (Newton-direction analogue, blocked by Hessian), divergence-induced metric+dual connections via Eguchi 1992 theorem (recovers Fisher metric + α-connections from α-divergence; the theoretical bridge), debiased Sinkhorn divergence Feydy-Séjourné 2019 (already in transport/ deferred list), Riemannian-manifold HMC Girolami-Calderhead 2011 (RMHMC uses local Fisher as HMC mass matrix), α-NMF Cichocki-Cruces-Amari 2011. **Cross-package coordination ledger** (each blocks ≥1 primitive above, in dependency order): chaos.Verlet symplectic integrator (~80 LOC, blocks T2.8 per 091 §2.2), linalg.MatrixExp + linalg.MatrixLog (~300 LOC Higham 2008, blocks T2.3 SPD), autodiff forward-mode + HVP (~600 LOC per autodiff-missing T1 / 012, blocks T1.3 generic + T2.9 generic + T3 second-order), prob.Distribution growing LogPDF + ScoreAt (~30 LOC + per-distribution implementations, blocks T1.3 closed-form path). **Lean v1 ships zero-blocker via Tier 1 only** with autodiff path scoped to MC variant only — 700 LOC of pure additions, no sibling-package changes. **Single highest-leverage commit** (~200 LOC): T1.6+T1.7+T1.8 *as a triple* (e/m coords ↔ geodesics ↔ projections on the simplex) — closed form, no ODE, no blocker, and the moment they land the package's name becomes honest: it ships **information geometry on the multinomial manifold**. Every later Tier 1/Tier 2 expansion is then a structural copy-paste of these three onto another manifold. Sibling-package state confirmed: optim/transport/doc.go:107-114 explicitly defers JKO/W-gradient-flow/sliced-W/unbalanced/Gromov-W (T1.10 wires the first), optim/proximal/ ships FBS/FISTA/ADMM with 8 prox operators (T1.10's Euclidean-prox half), prob/Distribution interface ships PDF/CDF only (T1.3 hook surface), linalg ships Cholesky/LU/QR/eigen/PCA (T1.9 solve target), chaos has no symplectic integrator (T2.8 blocker), geometry's quaternion implements SO(3) exp/log implicitly (cite as prior art), autodiff ships scalar reverse-mode tape only (T1.4 / T2.9 generic blocker). External reference frame anchored on Geomstats v2.7 (Miolane et al. JMLR 2020), Pymanopt v2.x (Townsend et al. JMLR 2016), Amari S. (2016) AMS vol 194 Springer, Nielsen F. (2020) Entropy 22(10):1100, Ay-Jost-Lê-Schwachhöfer (2017) Ergebnisse vol 64, Pennec-Sommer-Fletcher (2020) Elsevier, Otto F. (2001) Comm. PDE 26:101-174.
