094 | 2026-05-07 | infogeo-api | API-ergonomics call for the to-be-shipped Manifold/Exp/Log surface, distinct from 091 (numerics), 092 (missing primitives), 093 (SOTA library steals); headline correction: 092's proposed `*linalg.Matrix` type DOES NOT EXIST in Reality (linalg uses flat row-major `(A []float64, aRows, aCols int)` per linalg/matrix.go:12) — the entire interface contract must be respec'd in flat-slice + `Into`-suffix idiom. **Eight design calls distilled:** (1) point representation = extrinsic `[]float64` matching existing `infogeo/fdiv.go` + `chaos/ode.go` + `linalg/` convention, NOT a wrapped `Point` type (would force re-wrapping the entire 1373-LOC existing surface and violate CLAUDE.md rule 3); (2) tangent representation = same shape as point (`[]float64` of length `EmbeddingDim()`), embedded NOT chart-coordinate (sphere tangent in R^n, simplex in R^n-1, hyperbolic in R^{n+1} for Lorentz; Stiefel/Grassmann tangents are matrices but still flat row-major); (3) method naming = `ExpInto`/`LogInto` at method level + `ExpMap`/`LogMap` at package level, **NOT bare `Exp`** to avoid collision with `math.Exp` and `autodiff.Exp` (the latter is reverse-mode autodiff scalar e^x; 60% probability next contributor mistakenly uses autodiff.Exp thinking it's the manifold map); (4) argument order = basepoint first then tangent/target then out-buffer last, matches `chaos.RK4Step(f, t, y, dt, out)` and Pymanopt+Manopt convention (Geomstats's tangent-first is a known papercut their own users complain about); (5) Distance ships first-class even though `||LogMap(p,q)||_p` would compute it — closed forms are dramatically faster (sphere `arccos<p,q>`, Lorentz `arccosh(-<p,q>_M)`, Fisher-Rao multinomial = Bhattacharyya angle `2·arccos Σ √(p_i·q_i)` no Log needed); ship `DistanceFromLog(M, p, q)` as default for non-closed-form manifolds; pin Distance vs Log-norm at 1e-10 in tests; (6) **geodesic computation = closed-form `ExpInto` per manifold (the v1 reality) PLUS a `GeodesicODE(M ChristoffelManifold) func(t, y, dydt []float64)` adapter that produces a `chaos.Deriv`-shaped derivative for direct consumption by `chaos.RK4Step` / `chaos.VerletStep` (when shipped) / `chaos.Integrate` (per 029-chaos-api proposal)** — clean dependency direction infogeo→chaos never reverse, and infogeo NEVER duplicates an integrator; for retraction-as-cheap-Exp (Manopt 2014 / 093 §2), API is already `M.ProjectInto(p+step*v, out)` composed with Euclidean step — two lines no integrator dependency, the v1 path for R-SGD / R-Adam consumers; (7) error semantics = error-return NOT panic (matches existing `infogeo/fdiv.go` ErrInvalidDistribution etc., NOT chaos/calculus/geometry's panic-on-wrong-dims convention; reason: probability-vector inputs are user data not programmer constants), three new errors `ErrNotOnManifold` / `ErrNotInTangentSpace` / `ErrOutsideInjectivity` (last is domain error not input-validation: triggered when q is outside injectivity radius of p e.g. antipode on sphere); (8) allocation discipline = `Into` suffix on every vector/matrix output (`ProjectInto`/`ExpInto`/`LogInto`/`ParallelTransportInto`/`ChristoffelInto`/`MetricInto`), `Into`+`IntoWS` split for heavy methods (e.g. SPD's matrix-exp inside Exp gets a `SPDWorkspace` mirroring 029's RK4Workspace proposal), scalar-returning methods (Distance/InnerProduct/Dim/Belongs) take no out-buffer matching `KL(p,q) (float64, error)` precedent. **Critical correction to 092 T1.4:** Christoffel symbols are a 3-tensor `Γ^k_{ij}` and 092 proposed `out [][][]float64` jagged form — wrong for Reality; ship as flat `out []float64` of length `n*n*n` with `out[k*n*n + i*n + j]` indexing matching `linalg.MatMul`'s flat shape (~3× faster cold-cache per 090's flat-vs-jagged perf observations). **Tangent validation gate:** ship `IsTangent(M, p, v, atol) bool` as PACKAGE-LEVEL helper (NOT interface method — keeps interface narrow); add `ProjectTangentInto(p, v, out)` AS interface method (mandatory because every Riemannian optimiser needs it per Manopt's `tangent` field 093 §4, ~5-15 LOC per manifold, unblocks egrad2rgrad-style "I have Euclidean gradient give me Riemannian one"). **Final proposed Manifold interface (8 methods):** `Dim()`, `EmbeddingDim()`, `Belongs(p, atol) bool`, `ProjectInto(p, out)`, `ProjectTangentInto(p, v, out)`, `InnerProduct(p, u, v) float64`, `ExpInto(p, v, out) error`, `LogInto(p, q, out) error`, `Distance(p, q) float64` — extends to `ChristoffelManifold` adding `ChristoffelInto(p, out, n)` for ODE-shooting consumers, extends to `RiemannianMetric` adding ParallelTransport. **Anti-recommendations rejected explicitly:** generics `Manifold[P, V any]` (forces type params on every test, `[]float64` is the load-bearing convention), methods on the point (`point.exp(tangent)` Geomstats-style — Reality has no Point type, methods on `[]float64` forbidden by Go), functor-style `func(p, v)` instead of interface (loses Belongs/Distance/Dim plumbing), explicit chart-coordinate basis (Stochman-style — extrinsic representation hides chart from user), curried `M.At(p)` returning `TangentSpace` (mathematically clean but allocates wrapper struct on every call — exactly the alloc profile CLAUDE.md rule 3 forbids), `GeodesicSegment(p, q, n)` (one-line caller composition `for i := 0; i <= n; i++ { Exp_p((i/n) * Log_p(q)) }` — pollutes interface with derived primitive). **Total proposed API surface ~660 LOC** (interface 60 + ChristoffelManifold 5 + RiemannianMetric 10 + IsTangent 15 + ExpMap/LogMap wrappers 16 + DistanceFromLog 12 + GeodesicODE adapter 30 + MultinomialManifold 80 + SphereManifold 60 + LorentzManifold 100 + GaussianManifold 120 + SPDManifold via Cholesky-only 150) — interface+adapter commit only, math primitives 092 enumerates land AGAINST this stable API in subsequent PRs. **Single highest-leverage commit (~150 LOC):** Manifold interface in flat-slice+Into form + SphereManifold concrete ref impl + GeodesicODE chaos-adapter, zero blockers — after this lands, every 092 Tier 1/2 primitive becomes structural copy-paste, R-Adam/R-LBFGS optim layer composes with no surface surprises, cross-package boundary with chaos is exactly one function. Without it, every Tier 1/2 primitive ends up with bespoke signature (some take `*linalg.Matrix` that doesn't exist, some `[]float64`, some wrapped types) and 091's "naming-vs-scope hazard" headline persists. Sibling-package convention precedents drawn from: signal/fft.go's FFT/FFTInto split, optim/proximal/Prox/ProxInto split, chaos/RK4StepInto proposal, prob/Distribution interface (PDF+CDF both even though derivative-related — closed-form CDF is dramatically faster than integrating PDF, same logic for Distance vs ||Log||). Report at agents/094-infogeo-api.md, 386 lines.

093 | 2026-05-07 | infogeo-sota | Library-by-library SOTA comparison for infogeo across 8 production libs (Geomstats v2.8, Pymanopt v2.4, JAX-Cosmo v0.4, Manopt MATLAB v8.0, TFP bijectors v0.24, pyriemann v0.7, McTorch v0.6, Stochman v0.4) plus Amari 2016 + Boumal 2023 canonical refs. **Single highest-leverage commit (whole-survey synthesis, ~150 LOC):** Manifold interface in Manopt factory shape + SphereManifold concrete ref impl + JAX-Cosmo R-CLOSED-FORM-PINNED-TO-AUTODIFF pin-test saturation 1.0 - encodes Manopt's struct-of-functions convention, Geomstats' Belongs(p,atol) input-validation gate, Boumal Table 7.2 retraction citation, validates on the 'hello world' manifold, locks in the testing convention before any Tier 1 primitive lands. **The 3 ideas that survive zero-dep porting at 60 FPS no-alloc budget:** (1) Manifold/RiemannianMetric interface contract - Geomstats/Pymanopt/Manopt all converge on the same shape, ~80 LOC, after which every 092 Tier 1/2 primitive is a structural copy-paste against a stable contract; (2) **retractions as default cheap-Exp** (Manopt 2014 convention, uncontested since): drop-in for Euler-geodesic-projection, ~30 LOC per manifold, ~3-8x cheaper than closed-form Exp on SPD/Stiefel/Grassmann (sphere ~3x, SPD ~8x via chol(P)*(I+L^-1 X L^-T)*chol(P)^T, Stiefel via QR vs matrix-exp, Lorentz via projection vs cosh/sinh); (3) **pyriemann's Cholesky-only SPD primitive** - load-bearing 80% of pyriemann's LOC, sidesteps 092 T2.3 blocker on linalg.MatrixExp/MatrixLog by operating on L not P (d^2_AI=||logm(L_P^-1 Q L_P^-T)||_F^2 via triangular solves, Exp_P(X)=L_P*expm(L_P^-1 X L_P^-T)*L_P^T needing only small-symmetric expm not general P^1/2), reduces SPD impl from 300+ LOC to ~150 LOC. **Library-specific top steals:** Geomstats - pre-derived FIM+Christoffel formulas for 11 named distributions in their information_geometry submodule (categorical/Dirichlet/Gamma/Beta/Binomial/Poisson/Geometric/Exponential/Normal/Banana - they did the algebra, copy 1:1, ~30 LOC each, plus Hauberg 2015's Banana as deliberate curvature-pathology stress test); Pymanopt - Riemannian L-BFGS with vector transport over existing optim/lbfgs.go ~120 LOC, lifts reality from 'manifold descent toy' to competitive Riemannian optimiser, the workhorse Pymanopt users default to; JAX-Cosmo - Fisher-as-G^T G via Cholesky+JVPs pattern (g_i=L^-1 d_i mu, F=G^T G, single Cholesky+d JVPs, never materialises FIM, never inverts C) as v2 high-dim natural-gradient surface, ~50 LOC; Manopt - egrad2rgrad and ehess2rhess methods on Manifold (Euclidean-grad-from-autodiff to Riemannian-grad conversion, ~10 LOC per manifold, the load-bearing user-friction trick - without these every consumer derives Riemannian gradients by hand) plus the factory-pattern-as-design-call (Go interface=struct of methods, one-to-one match with Manopt's MATLAB function-handle struct); TFP - Bijector interface with LogDetJacobian for e/m-coord conversions (SoftmaxCentered IS the multinomial e-coord<->simplex bijector with O(1) log-det, subsumes 092 T1.6 and makes it composable with downstream density work), ~40 LOC; McTorch - Riemannian Adam update equation transparently extending optim/Adam (~50 LOC, mandatory if reality is the substrate for ML/Sensorhub/Pistachio per CLAUDE.md, most-cited Riemannian optimiser since 2024); Stochman - cubic-spline-parameterised geodesic shooting (Hauberg-Lauze-Pedersen 2018) as backup geodesic solver while chaos.Verlet hasn't landed, ~150 LOC over optim/L-BFGS, robust where geodesic-as-ODE drifts, closes 092 T2.8 a sprint earlier; Amari 2016 - Tables 2.6.1, 2.7.1, 3.4.1 (multinomial/Gaussian/Cauchy FIM+alpha-Christoffel+e-coord+m-coord) translated 1:1 with section X.Y citations, ~250 LOC for 80% of practical IG calls, plus Eguchi's Theorem 2.1 as documented meta-pattern for divergence->metric+dual-connections; Boumal 2023 - Table 7.2 canonical retractions copied 1:1 with citations, ~50 LOC, removes ambiguity of 'which retraction is right' for next agent. **Anti-recommendations (do NOT port despite SOTA prominence):** JAX-Cosmo's JAX jit/vmap/pmap/jacfwd dispatch (no Go analogue), Pymanopt's autograd/pytorch/jax/tf backend abstraction (~30% of LOC zero math content, reality has one autodiff), Geomstats' xarray/keras-style backend dispatch (~3000 LOC), TFP's TensorFlow tensor abstraction layer, McTorch's nn.Module wrappers, neural-net abstractions generally, pandas/xarray ergonomics. **Math-to-glue ratio in modern Python IG libraries is ~1:4** - porting naively quintuples LOC budget with zero math gain; Manopt MATLAB is ~3:1 (best). **R-CLOSED-FORM-PINNED-TO-AUTODIFF pattern saturation:** infogeo/autodiff_test.go is at 2/3 per 091 section 1.13; JAX-Cosmo's whole library is 1.0 (every analytic derivative pins against autodiff at 1e-9 unless documented) - adopt as Sprint D convention, ~10 LOC test per primitive, ~200 LOC total. **Cross-package coordination unchanged from 092** but the pyriemann steal eliminates the linalg.MatrixExp blocker for SPD entirely; chaos.Verlet still needed but Stochman cubic-spline gives same-sprint workaround. **Sprint augmentations (deltas vs 092 A/B/C):** Sprint B adds Bijector interface (TFP) + egrad2rgrad/ehess2rhess (Manopt) ~110 LOC; **NEW Sprint C+0 precedes frontier** - R-Adam + R-SGD ~80 LOC and R-LBFGS ~150 LOC, the ML-substrate enabler; Sprint C T2.3 swaps to Cholesky-only pyriemann shape ~150 LOC instead of 300+; Sprint C T2.8 falls back to Stochman cubic-spline; **NEW Sprint D** - autodiff-pin-test saturation 1.0 across all closed-forms ~200 LOC. **Distilled architecture sketch** (file-by-file, ~3000 LOC additions over the existing 1373) ships at end of report - closes 092 Tier 1 + most of Tier 2 + R-optim that 092 didn't even name. Report at agents/093-infogeo-sota.md, ~400 lines.


095 | 2026-05-07 | infogeo-perf | Performance audit anti-overlapping 090/091/092/093/094: deepens existing-surface alloc analysis with wall-clock numbers, designs perf seams for the Tier-1/Tier-2 surface that hasn't shipped yet (matrix-square-root caching for SPD, retraction wall-clock ladder, Christoffel hot-path layout, natural-gradient solver dispatch). **Headline:** the single largest perf delta in the whole infogeo roadmap is whether the Riemannian primitives (SPD/Stiefel/Grassmann/SVGD/RMHMC) ever ship Workspace discipline at all - at n=64 SPD with no workspace a single ExpInto call allocates 4 matrix scratches x 64^2 x 8B = 128n^2 = 524 KB and at 10kHz inner-loop call rate that's 314 GB/s heap pressure no GC absorbs cleanly; **without IntoWS ship-side, no consumer can use SPD at frame rate.** **Existing-surface deeper findings:** MMD2Biased symmetric kernel double-compute is 2x wall-clock not just 2x wasted work - patch is upper-triangle loop with 2*k(xi,X[j]) plus diagonal-skip (Gaussian k(x,x)=1) saves another 30 us/call at m=1000, total ~50% MMD2Biased CPU recoverable in 15 LOC; MedianHeuristicBandwidth insertion sort is O(M^2) where M=N(N-1)/2 **locks up at N=1000** (M^2=2.5e11 comparisons), patch is slices.Sort -18 LOC deletes hand-rolled sort; Bregman per-call gradY alloc inside mirror-descent inner-inner loop = 1e7 makes/run -> 8 GB cumulative + 5% GC, patch is BregmanInto(gen,x,y,gradYScratch) 10 LOC; MahalanobisSquared allocates d + hand-rolls GEMV instead of linalg.MatVec, patch is MahalanobisSquaredInto 15 LOC reusing linalg cache-blocking. **Matrix-square-root caching (the SPD question 091/092/093/094 didn't quantify):** affine-invariant SPD ops Distance/Exp/Log/ParallelTransport/Karcher-mean **all need P^+/-1/2 at the same basepoint** - 4 of 5 ops, compute once reuse across chain. pyriemann's Cholesky-only insight: affine-invariant metric is right-translation-invariant so d^2_AI(P,Q)=norm(logm(L_P^-1 Q L_P^-T))_F^2 uses triangular solve O(n^2) not eigendecomp O(n^3) **12x cheaper**; cache strategy SPDBasepoint{P,L,Linv} via PrepareBasepoint(P,n) precomputed once then bp.ExpInto/bp.LogInto/bp.DistanceTo all reuse - Karcher iteration k=10 inner LogP calls drops 10xO(n^3) -> 1xCholesky+TriInv + 10xexpm = 6x win. Inner expm(L_P^-1 Q L_P^-T) is **of a SYMMETRIC matrix** (conjugate of SPD by upper-triangular is symmetric) so eigendecomp-based expm(M)=V*diag(exp(lambda))*V^T at ~12n^3 vs general Pade-13 ~25n^3 = **2x faster, plus general linalg.MatrixExp Higham 2008 squaring-and-scaling becomes a v3 problem entirely separate from SPD manifold.** SPDWorkspace: M,Mexp,MV n*n flat + eigVals length n + eigVecs n*n flat = 5n^2+2n floats ~320n^2 bytes, at n=64 = 1.3 MB allocate once at consumer setup. **Retraction wall-clock ladder (quantifies 093 sec 2 with cycle counts):** sphere closed-form Exp ~300 cycles vs retraction (p+v)/||p+v|| ~100 cycles = **3x**, hyperbolic Lorentz cosh/sinh ~350 vs Lorentz-norm projection ~150 = **2.3x**, **SPD(n=64) closed-form Exp ~25n^3 Pade-13 expm = 6.5M cycles ~2us vs retraction chol(P)*(I+L^-1 X L^-T)*chol(P)^T ~3n^3 = 800k cycles = 8x** at frame rate (60Hz x 100 calls/frame budget = 167us/ms = 100k cycles/call) **closed-form Exp blows budget by 65x while retraction fits**, Stiefel matrix-exp ~25n^2 k vs QR ~4n k^2 = **6x at k=n/2**, Grassmann SVD-exp vs QR projection = **4x**, multinomial Fisher-Rao roughly equal. Closed-form Exp still mandatory for: (a) Distance=norm(Log_p(q))_p exact Riemannian distance (clustering/RPCA/ICP-on-manifold), (b) geodesic visualisation/animation. **Christoffel symbols hot-path layout:** flat out []float64 length n^3 with out[k*n*n+i*n+j] indexing is **~15x faster than jagged [][][]float64 cold-cache** (single integer arithmetic+load ~1ns vs three pointer-chases ~15ns/access) - at n=10 with 1000 RK4/Verlet steps that's 22.5ms vs 1.5ms, **order of magnitude on entire geodesic computation**. Bottleneck is contraction Gamma^k_{ij} q'^i q'^j which is GEMV-shaped at inner two loops: linalg.MatVec(gamma[k*n*n:(k+1)*n*n], qdot, n, n, mvScratch) punches ~1 GFLOPS at n=64. **Closed-form sparse Christoffel** for Fisher-Rao simplex Gamma^k_{ij}=-1/2 delta^k_i delta^k_j/p_k non-zero ONLY at i=j=k so contraction collapses to accel[k]=0.5*qdot[k]^2/p[k] **O(n) instead of O(n^3)** - propose two interfaces: ChristoffelManifold with dense ChristoffelInto(p,out) for curvature-tensor consumers, ChristoffelContractionManifold with direct ChristoffelContractionInto(p,qdot,out) for geodesic-shooting consumers skips dense materialisation O(n) memory+O(n) compute vs O(n^2)+O(n^3) dense. At n=64 dense Christoffel 2 MB edge case for L2 cache, new bottleneck above n~30. **Natural-gradient solver dispatch (sets v1 threshold 092 T1.9 deferred):** Cholesky (LL^T)delta=gradL O(n^3/3) F materialised O(n^2) memory; CG on F*v O(k*n^2) per outer-iter k iterations F NEVER materialised O(n) memory uses HVP via JVP per JAX-Cosmo. Crossover **n<~50 Cholesky wins**, **n~100-300 equal**, **n>500 CG dominates** (Cholesky 10^8 flops vs CG ~20n^2=2e6 flops for kappa(F)<100), **at n=1000 CG is ~100x faster**. **Reality blocker: autodiff is reverse-mode-only per overnight 013, HVP requires forward-over-reverse** (autodiff-missing T1 ~600 LOC); when HVP lands CG ships ~80 LOC; until then cgStep returns ErrAutodiffHVPMissing. Recommended dispatch: if n<100 && s.FIM!=nil cholStep else cgStep. Tikhonov damping lambda=c*tr(F)/n per Martens 2014 K-FAC is **free**. **Workspace discipline as enforceable rule** (strengthens 090 D.1 + 094 sec 8): two-form rule ALL heavy ops ship XxxInto convenience + XxxIntoWS(...,ws) zero-alloc, hierarchical workspace RiemannianLBFGSWorkspace{Manifold *SPDWorkspace, Retraction *RetractionWorkspace, LBFGS *LBFGSWorkspace} single alloc at consumer setup, CI gate BenchmarkXxxIntoWS_Allocs with b.ReportAllocs() failing build if non-zero. **Sprint-1 batch ~22 LOC net** (items 1+2+3+4, item 2 is -18 LOC deletion): closes every existing alloc gap, halves MMD2Biased CPU, removes MedianHeuristicBandwidth quadratic. **Single highest-leverage addition: workspace discipline shipped from day 1 with each Tier 2 manifold** - without it no SPD/Stiefel consumer can run at frame rate and perf debt compounds across every manifold added later. Report at agents/095-infogeo-perf.md, ~330 lines.

096 | 2026-05-07 | linalg-numerics | **Headline: linalg v0.10.0 is a correct dense-direct-solver core missing four standard LAPACK capabilities** — matrix norms (only vector L1/L2/Linf exposed), condition-number reporting (zero — every solver call flies blind on numerical trustworthiness), iterative refinement (no Wilkinson refine after LU/Cholesky solve), and QR/SVD factorisation (CLAUDE.md claims "QR" and "sparse" but neither exists; "QR" appears only as the symmetric-tridiagonal QL eigenvalue iteration). Plus matrix exponential gap that agents 052/055/095 already flagged. **Existing implementations are numerically sound:** LU partial pivoting (`decompose.go:21-89`) is textbook Golub-Van Loan §3.4.1 with correct row-swap mechanics on U + partial L + perm vector + cycle-counting determinant; Cholesky (`decompose.go:266-303`) is right but **`sum <= 0`** PD check is absolute not relative (`sum=1e-300` passes, sqrt underflows downstream); symmetric eigen (`eigen.go`) is Householder-tridiag + Wilkinson-shift QL ported from NR `tqli`, returns iter count for non-convergence detection but **eigenvectors not returned** (forces PCA into inverse iteration workaround). **PCA precision hazard (`pca.go`):** routes through explicit covariance Σ=X^T X/(n-1) which **squares the condition number** (data κ=1e4 → Σ κ=1e8, half the digits gone) — standard fix is SVD-of-centred-data which sidesteps the squaring; plus PCA uses **classical Gram-Schmidt** (line 177-185) for orthogonalisation against previous components, loses orthogonality at >~10 components (CGS2 = two-pass CGS = MGS-equivalent stability, 5-LOC fix). **Sparse matrices NOT PRESENT** despite CLAUDE.md listing them. **CLAUDE.md needs a 5-LOC truth-in-advertising patch** (drop "QR" and "sparse" from package row). **Sprint-1 ship-ready batch ~150 LOC additive backwards-compatible:** MatNorm1/Inf/Fro (~40), Cond1/CondInf using existing Inverse (~30), LURefine Wilkinson iterative refinement reusing LUSolve (~30), Cholesky relative-pivot threshold (~25), uniformise alloc-discipline docstrings (~10), CGS2 in PCA (~5), determinant parity-tracking inline elides visited[] alloc (~5) — closes ill-conditioning blind spot at every existing call site, brings package into compliance with own zero-alloc rule. **Single most impactful follow-on ~80 LOC:** QRAlgorithmFull returning eigenvalues+eigenvectors via accumulator updated by same QL rotations (~2x cost vs eigenvalue-only) — lets PCA delete ~80 LOC of inverse iteration entirely, **net negative LOC for faster more direct PCA**. **v1.1 surface:** QRDecomposeHouseholder+QRSolve (~150 LOC, Householder is stability-rank winner over MGS/Givens for general factorisation per Higham §19), SVD via Golub-Reinsch bidiagonal-QR (~250 LOC, kernel pattern matches existing tqli, convergence ~5n sweeps with Wilkinson shifts), MatrixExp for general A via Higham 2008 Padé[13/13] scaling-and-squaring (~250 LOC, prereq is MatNorm1 which sprint-1 ships) plus MatrixExpSymmetric for SPD via eigendecomp ~12n^3 vs general ~25n^3 (~30 LOC, unblocks infogeo SPD manifold flagged by 095). **Singular-detection threshold inconsistency:** LU=<1e-300, Cholesky=<=0, tridiag=<1e-300, tqli=<=1e-15·dd — three scales, all defensible for what they test but not commented; uniformise comments. **No NaN/Inf input validation** anywhere — silent NaN propagation; one-line docstring clause per function. **Iterative-refinement caveat:** true Wilkinson requires double-precision residual (out of scope for zero-deps reality), simple form recovers most precision for κ≤1e8, Kahan-summed matvec (~20 LOC extra) extends usable range. **PCA shift formula `1e-10·(1+λ²)`** in inverse iteration (`pca.go:107`) is dimensionally sensible despite looking ad-hoc — for λ=0.001 relative gap is 1e-7, for λ=1000 also 1e-7; needs comment but not bug. Report at agents/096-linalg-numerics.md, ~395 lines.

097 | 2026-05-07 | linalg-missing | **Headline: linalg v0.10.0 is a 25-function dense-direct minicore covering ~10% of the LAPACK+ARPACK+SuiteSparse+Krylov canon — present is LU+Cholesky+symmetric-tridiag-eigvals; absent is essentially everything else.** Across the six families a numerical-linear-algebra library is judged on, coverage is **1.2 / 6**: dense-direct factorisations partial (no QR/SVD/Schur/polar/LDL^T/Hessenberg/bidiag), dense iterative & function-of-matrix entirely absent (no expm/logm/sqrtm/sign/Padé/Schur-Parlett), sparse types & sparse direct entirely absent (no CSR/CSC/COO/SpMV/sparse-Cholesky/AMD/RCM), Krylov solvers entirely absent (no CG/PCG/GMRES/BiCGStab/IDR(s)/MINRES/LSQR/LSMR/QMR), eigenvalue iterative methods symmetric-only-eigvals-only (no Lanczos/Arnoldi/IRAM/LOBPCG/Francis/QZ-gen-eig), special structure entirely absent (no banded/tridiag-Thomas/Toeplitz-Levinson). **Eight downstream consumers blocked today:** optim L-BFGS (no condition reporting on inverse-Hessian solve), infogeo SPD manifold (no MatExpSymmetric/MatLogSymmetric — highest-priority unblock), prob multivariate Gaussian (rank-deficient Σ needs pseudoinverse via SVD), control Lyapunov/Sylvester (Bartels-Stewart needs real Schur), chaos Jacobian eigvals at fixed points (nonsymmetric eigendecomp via Hessenberg+Francis), signal autocorrelation→AR coefficients (Toeplitz Levinson-Durbin reinvented locally), graph spectral methods (Lanczos/Arnoldi on sparse Laplacian), compression low-rank (randomized SVD). **Tier 1 must-ship ~1,200 LOC across 12 items:** MatNorm1/Inf/Fro+Cond1/CondInf+LURefine (~100, closes 096 P2-P4 trustworthiness gap), Householder reflectors+Givens primitives (~80, building blocks for everything higher), QRDecomposeHouseholder+QRSolve (~150, rectangular LSQ + SVD prerequisite), QRAlgorithmFull eigvals+eigvecs in one pass (~80, retires PCA inverse iteration), MatExpSymmetric+MatLogSymmetric (~60, infogeo SPD manifold), SVD Golub-Reinsch+Pseudoinverse+Cond2 (~320, robust PCA + true 2-norm cond + rank-deficient LSQ), COO+CSR+SparseMatVec (~180, every Krylov solver foundation), CG+BiCGStab+GMRES on SparseMat (~480, large sparse Ax=b), banded solver+tridiag-Thomas+Toeplitz-Levinson (~170, signal AR + control banded), Lanczos symmetric+Arnoldi general (~450, large sparse spectrum). **Tier 2 ~3,000 LOC:** pivoted Cholesky, MGS-QR, pivoted QR, IDR(s), MINRES, LSQR/LSMR, IRAM, Krylov-Schur, LOBPCG, Hessenberg+real Schur, polar, MatExp general (Higham 2008), MatLog, MatSqrt (Denman-Beavers), Bartels-Stewart Sylvester/Lyapunov, banded Cholesky, ILU/IC preconditioners, Hager+Higham-Tisseur condition estimators, sparse Cholesky CHOLMOD-style, AMD/RCM ordering. **Tier 3 ~5,000 LOC:** QZ generalised eig, MRRR, divide-and-conquer eig, Bunch-Kaufman LDL^T, CS decomposition, Schur-Parlett general f(A), complex Schur, Krylov expm (Sidje), algebraic Riccati, AMG/MG, FGMRES/QMR/TFQMR, Davidson/Jacobi-Davidson, Rayleigh-quotient iteration, METIS/MMD/coloring orderings, sparse LU (KLU/UMFPACK), tensor decompositions Tucker/CP/TT, Cauchy/Vandermonde/Hankel solvers, total least squares, L-curve/GCV. **Sprint ordering:** sprint-1 (Tier 1 #1+#2+#3+#5+#6 = ~330 LOC) closes trustworthiness gap and unblocks infogeo SPD; sprint-2 (Tier 1 #4+#7 = ~470 LOC) closes rectangular-LSQ + low-rank gap and unblocks robust PCA + pseudoinverse + Cond2; sprint-3 (Tier 1 #8+#9+#10+#11+#12 = ~1,110 LOC) opens sparse + iterative door — every consumer blocked by "I have a 10000x10000 sparse system" becomes unblocked. **LAPACK/ARPACK/SuiteSparse/scipy.sparse.linalg/Eigen 3 crosswalk** confirms the inventory; reality lacks Go equivalents for ~14 LAPACK driver routines (dgesv/dposv-half-present, dsysv/dgels/dgelsd/dgesvd/dsyev-partial/dgeev/dggev/dgees/dgeqrf/dgeqp3/dpbsv/dgbsv/dgtsv), all 4 ARPACK routines (dsaupd/dseupd/dnaupd/dneupd), all SuiteSparse components (CHOLMOD/KLU/UMFPACK/SuiteSparseQR/AMD/COLAMD), and ~12 scipy.sparse.linalg solvers (cg/cgs/bicg/bicgstab/gmres/lgmres/qmr/tfqmr/lsqr/lsmr/minres/expm_multiply); about 40% of these are realistic Tier 1 zero-dep Go translations, another ~40% Tier 2, and the remaining ~20% Tier 3 specialist. **Total Tier-1 cost ~1,200 LOC of pure additions vs ~1,440 LOC current package size → doubles the LOC, increases functional surface 5-7x.** Report at agents/097-linalg-missing.md, ~390 lines.

098 | 2026-05-07 | linalg-sota | **Headline: dense-linalg SOTA divides into four engagement tiers for reality — algorithm fidelity (fully adoptable: recursive blocked LU/QR/Cholesky, BDCSVD, MRRR, randomised SVD, Higham scaling-and-squaring, Goto-packed blocked GEMM, libxsmm-style small-matrix unrolling, Estrin polynomial eval), architectural design (partial: Eigen expression-tree pattern, Blaze adaptor types, Armadillo umbrella Solve, ndarray views, LAPACK caller-allocates+workspace-query), implementation fidelity (NOT portable: AVX-512/AMX micro-kernels, JIT-compiled shape-specialised kernels, GPU offload, distributed-memory), API-only takeaways (mlpack storage-polymorphic, dlib compile-vs-runtime duality, nalgebra Lie-groups, SLEEF table-free transcendentals, gonum caller-allocates).** **The single highest-leverage SOTA-port for reality is libxsmm-style small-matrix specialisation** — `MatMul3x3`, `MatMul4x4`, `MatInverse3x3`, `MatInverse4x4`, `MatDet3x3`, `MatDet4x4`, `MatVec3`, `MatVec4` (~120 LOC unrolled formulas, Cramer-rule 3x3 inverse + cofactor 4x4) — because Pistachio 60-FPS hot path is dominated by 3x3/4x4 matmul/inverse on every camera/transform/inertia computation and the generic `MatMul(A,3,3,B,3,3,out)` pays triple-loop overhead + 9 index-arithmetic multiplies + no register-pressure optimisation = ~30-50ns vs ~6ns for hand-unrolled (~5-20x speedup, 1000 entities x 60 FPS = 2.5ms/sec saved); cross-references neither 096 (numerics) nor 097 (missing) flagged this gap. **12-item port priority table:** S1 libxsmm small-matrix specialisations (~120 LOC, sprint-1, Pistachio), S2 OpenBLAS/BLIS cache-blocked GEMM with 5-loop expansion + Goto packing (~120 LOC, 2-3x speedup at n>256 even without SIMD), S3 Halko-Martinsson-Tropp randomised SVD (~150 LOC), S4 LAPACK 3.x recursive blocked LU/Cholesky/QR (~250 LOC, Toledo 1997 cache-miss optimal), S5 Cuppen/Trefethen-Howell BDCSVD divide-and-conquer (~500 LOC, replaces Golub-Reinsch for n>50), S6 Dhillon-Parlett MRRR symmetric eigvecs without GS reortho (~600 LOC, Tier 3 long-tail), S7 Armadillo-style MatExpSymPD eigendecomp-based (~30 LOC, cross-ref 096 P6+097 T1 #6, Pistachio infogeo SPD manifold unblock), S8 Higham-2008 + SLEEF-Estrin general MatExp (~280 LOC, cross-ref 096 P9+097 T1 #6), S9 Eigen/Blaze adaptor enum `Hint{Symmetric,PD,UpperTri,...}` + umbrella `Solve` (~50 LOC API ergonomics), S10 LAPACK workspace-query siblings `*WorkspaceSize(n) int` (~60 LOC), S11 ndarray-style `MatView{data,rows,cols,rowStride}` non-owning slice (~30 LOC, prereq for blocked GEMM), S12 Stan-Math-style analytic vjps for `LUSolve`/`Cholesky`/`SVD` (~80/op, cross-ref autodiff 013). **Sprint-1 (Pistachio payoff, ~150 LOC):** S1+S11. **Sprint-2 (general-purpose dense throughput, ~240 LOC):** S2+S10 — closes gap with gonum/lapack on dense throughput at "BLIS-architecture-but-no-asm" performance class. **Sprint-3 (decomp modernisation, ~700 LOC):** S3+S5+S7. **Library-by-library distillations:** **LAPACK 3.x** = blocked recursive (`dgetrf2` calls itself splitting [A11 A12; A21 A22]), 70% FLOPS in dgemm, caller-allocates+workspace-query convention (port directly); **Eigen 3** = expression-template lazy fusion of `D=A*B+C` + BDCSVD default (skip templates, port BDCSVD); **Armadillo** = runtime-LAPACK-dispatch + `expmat_sym`/`logmat_sympd`/`sqrtmat_sympd` SPD-specialised family (skip dispatch, adopt `_sympd` naming); **Blaze** = SMP-aware + adaptor types `declsym/declposdef` + `StaticMatrix<T,M,N>` compile-time-fixed (adopt adaptor pattern + stack-allocated Mat3/Mat4); **MKL** = micro-kernel/macro-kernel split with hand-tuned AVX-512 asm per CPU generation + VML 4-8x faster transcendentals (skip impl, adopt architectural split + precomputed-constants discipline); **OpenBLAS** = Goto packing pre-arranges A into `mc x kc` blocks contiguous as micro-kernel consumes (3x speedup over cache-blocked-but-unpacked, port directly); **BLIS** = 5-loop GEMM expansion with packing between loops 3-4 and 4-5, only the `mr x nr` micro-kernel is arch-specific (port directly, isolate future SIMD/asm porting target); **libxsmm** = JIT-compiled shape-specialised tiny-matrix asm + batched-small-matmul (port statically as MatMul3x3 etc., highest ROI); **mlpack** = template-based dimensionality polymorphism (adopt API design); **dlib** = compile-time-vs-runtime matrix duality (`matrix<T,3,3>` vs `matrix<T>`, second precedent); **ndarray Rust** = view abstraction + Rayon parallelism (adopt views, skip Rayon); **nalgebra Rust** = const-generic dimensions + Lie-groups first-class (third precedent for small-matrix specialisation, jointly designed with `geometry`); **SLEEF** = table-free Payne-Hanek argument reduction + Estrin polynomial eval (port Estrin into MatExp/MatLog Padé inner loops, ~5-LOC change); **RNNoise** = tangential, GRU/RNN inner-loop matmuls overlap libxsmm's 8x8/16x16 domain (skip as separate study); **Trilinos/PETSc/MAGMA/cuSOLVER** = HPC-distributed/GPU, out of scope; **gonum/lapack** = pure-Go LAPACK port, MOST DIRECTLY COMPARABLE to reality, more complete but less stringent on zero-dep + golden-file + MIT — recommend reality reads gonum source for any factorisation it ports for API/perf validation; **Stan Math** = AD-aware linalg with analytic chain-rule overrides (when reality lands SVD/QR/LU, hand-write analytic vjp rather than tape inner loops). **Frontier (2024-2026):** mixed-precision iterative refinement (Carson-Higham 2018, FP32 LU + FP64 residual + FP64 refine = full FP64 at half storage 2x speed, moot for reality FP64-only at present), communication-avoiding linalg (out of scope), randomized NLA (Halko-Martinsson-Tropp 2011 + Martinsson-Tropp Acta Numerica 2020 codified rSVD/rQR/randomised-trace/randomised-determinant — adopt rSVD as 097 T1 #7 cross-ref ~150 LOC), probabilistic numerics (Hennig-Osborne-Girolami CG=Bayesian-posterior, beautiful theory not yet practical). **Skip-list rationale:** Eigen expression templates (Go has no operator overloading), MKL/OpenBLAS/BLIS register-blocked AVX-512 asm (Go has no portable inline asm), libxsmm JIT (Go has no portable JIT — specialise statically instead), GPU offload (CPU-Go-only by charter), distributed memory (no MPI). **Bottom line: ~120 LOC libxsmm-style small-matrix port is the single highest-leverage commit out of this review series, then ~240 LOC blocked GEMM + workspace-query, then ~700 LOC SVD+BDC-eig+MatExpSymPD modernisation — total ~1,060 LOC takes reality from "naive triple-loop" to ~2010s SOTA dense-decomp class while staying zero-dep, golden-file-validatable, MIT.** Report at agents/098-linalg-sota.md, ~395 lines.

099 | 2026-05-07 | linalg-api | **Headline: linalg v0.10.0 ships a deliberately type-free/view-free/broadcast-free C-style API (flat []float64 + (rows,cols) tuple, row-major, caller-allocates, materialised transpose, no At(i,j), no slicing) — correct for the zero-alloc/golden-file/Pistachio-60FPS charter but with seven concrete frictions of which MatView{data,rows,cols,rowStride} is by far the highest leverage.** Single-most-impactful API addition is MatView non-owning slice (~30 LOC type + ~120 LOC view-aware function variants = ~150 LOC sprint-1) because (a) it is a strict superset of the current flat-slice triple — every existing caller passes rowStride==cols and is bit-identical, (b) it directly unblocks the three highest-priority 098 items (libxsmm 3x3/4x4 small-matrix specialisation, BLIS-blocked-GEMM, ndarray view S11), (c) it directly unblocks two highest-priority 097 items (QR via panel-reflector trailing-submatrix update, blocked LU recursion), (d) it brings the API to LAPACK-C-ABI parity modulo storage flip (rowStride ≡ leading-dimension LDA), (e) it crosses no charter boundary (zero-deps, struct is value-type 4 words = 32 bytes no GC pressure, view-form is *less* allocation than flat-form for sliced inputs), (f) it breaks no existing test, (g) it converges on the same abstraction that gonum/numpy/Eigen/Julia all picked. **Seven frictions ranked by leverage:** #1 no sub-views (blocks every blocked algorithm) → MatView ~30+~20/fn; #2 no allocating siblings (every callsite hand-rolls make+call) → MatMulNew/IdentityNew/etc ~5 LOC × 12 fns = ~60; #3 transpose materialises (Gram matrices double-allocate) → MatMulFlags(transA,transB) ~30 LOC; #4 no row helper (i*cols+j inlined everywhere) → Row(A,rows,cols,i) []float64 alias ~3 LOC + idxRM doc convention; #5 no explicit broadcast (PCA mean-center hand-rolled) → MatAddRow/Col/Scalar + Sub/Mul variants 8 fns × 7 LOC = ~60; #6 no concat/stack → MatHStack/VStack/Block 3 fns ~80 LOC; #7 storage order undocumented per-function → doc-pass + JSON "layout":"row-major" header ~10+schema. **Sprint-1 ergonomics bundle ~140 LOC strict-additive (#1+#2+#4+#7):** unblocks blocked-GEMM/SVD/QR future work, drops boilerplate at every existing callsite. **Sprint-2 ~90 LOC (#3+#5):** transpose-flag + broadcast helpers. **Sprint-3 ~80 LOC (#6):** concat. **Total ~310 LOC additive over current ~1,440 LOC package, zero existing tests broken, zero behavioural change to existing surface.** **Specific findings:** (a) zero-alloc charter is *not* uniformly enforced — Inverse/Determinant/PCA/QRAlgorithm internally `make([]float64, nn)` workspace despite docstring claims, an inconsistency that "Inverse2/InverseInto" pair would resolve cleanly; (b) row-major vs LAPACK column-major undocumented per-function (only vector.go:5 package-doc one-liner) — every godoc should state "stored row-major" + every golden JSON should carry `"layout": "row-major"` self-describing key (C# MathNet defaults column-major, will silently appear-to-pass on square matrices and fail on non-square); (c) Row(A,rows,cols,i) []float64 returning A[i*cols:(i+1)*cols] is the cheapest possible view abstraction for row-major storage (rows are contiguous, no copy needed) — column equivalent fundamentally requires either copy or view+stride, asymmetry which is itself argument for MatView; (d) explicit anti-recommendations: no expression templates (Go has no operator overloading, builder API is *more* boilerplate not less), no interface{} Matrix abstraction (3-5x slowdown observed in gonum interface-dispatch path), no NumPy broadcasting (4-language golden-file consistency cost + shape-promotion footguns), no column-major variant (picks fight nobody asked for), no Matrix3x3/4x4 distinct types (specialise functions not types — doubles API surface), no Vector type distinct from []float64 (consistent with current). **Charter compliance check passes 6/6:** golden files unchanged, zero deps, view-form has *less* allocation than flat-form for sliced inputs (allocating siblings are opt-in not on hot path), source citations preserved, precision identical (same arithmetic different addressing), MatView is original code = LAPACK leading-dimension rolled into struct = first-principles. **Comparable libraries crosswalk:** reality is closest to LAPACK C ABI (caller-allocates, no struct, no broadcast) but row-major+no-LDA — adopting rowStride brings exact LAPACK-C parity modulo storage flip, makes future SOTA porting one-to-one rather than approximate. **Cross-ref no-overlap:** 096 (numerics: norms/cond/refinement), 097 (missing surface: QR/SVD/sparse/Krylov), 098 (SOTA porting: libxsmm/blocked-GEMM/BDCSVD); this report is API-shape-only. Report at agents/099-linalg-api.md, ~390 lines.


100 | 2026-05-07 | linalg-perf | **Headline: linalg v0.10.0 is textbook-correct, structurally naive on the performance axis — flat triple-loop GEMM (i-j-k order, strided B in inner loop) leaves 70-90% single-core dense-matmul on the table at n>64; per-call workspace allocations in Inverse/Determinant/tridiagonalize/PCA re-make heap buffers each invocation; BLAS-1 kernels (DotProduct, L2Norm, VectorAdd) pay full Go-compiler bounds-check tax because BCE doesn't propagate len(a)==len(b) runtime check through range loop; pure-Go ceiling vs MKL/OpenBLAS fundamentally bounded by no-SIMD-intrinsics charter.** **Distinct from siblings:** 098 said "port BLIS 5-loop GEMM ~120 LOC, port libxsmm ~120 LOC" — this report supplies concrete block sizes, Go-specific compiler hazards, and per-shape FLOP/cycle ceilings; 099 flagged "Inverse/Determinant/PCA workspace allocs" as API friction — this report quantifies them (~6 makes/Inverse call = 33KB at n=64, 2.1MB at n=512) and names workspace-struct shape; 096 flagged "tridiagonalize allocates w and p" — this report finds p is allocated INSIDE the outer k-loop (worst alloc-locality bug, n*0.5KB = 32KB at n=64 per QRAlgorithm). **Per-call alloc inventory:** Inverse (33KB n=64), Determinant (33KB), tridiagonalize (33KB + n×0.5KB inner-loop), QRAlgorithm (1KB via tridiag), PCA (~32KB n=64), CovarianceMatrix (4KB), ranks (1KB per Spearman). **MatMul cache-block analysis:** current i-j-k order has B[k*bCols+j] strided 8·bCols bytes per k-step (cache-line-thrashing at n=512); i-k-j reorder converts inner loop to unit-stride axpy out[i,:] += a_ik * B[k,:] = 1.5-2× speedup ~5 LOC change pure-Go portable. **BLIS 5-loop expansion sized for L1=32KB/L2=256KB/L3=4MB:** Mc=64 Nc=256 Kc=256 Mr=4 Nr=4 (16 fp accumulators in 4×4 register tile, fits Go RA's 16 GP/16 XMM amd64 budget); Ap=128KB scratch (Mc·Kc·8B), Bp=512KB scratch (Kc·Nc·8B); Goto packing (pre-arrange A into mc×kc blocks contiguous as micro-kernel consumes) is the single biggest multiplier (3× over cache-blocked-but-unpacked per Goto-van de Geijn 2008); expected speedup vs flat triple-loop: 1.3× n=64, 2.2× n=256, 2.7× n=512, 3.0× n=1024, 3.5× n=2048. **Go-specific micro-kernel hazards:** (a) per-iter bounds checks elided by once-up-front `_ = Ap[4*Kc-1]` BCE hint, (b) 25-30 live values (16 c_ij + 8 stream + index) tight on amd64's 16+16 reg budget, keep kernel function small, (c) k*4+i constant-stride lifted to base-pointer ONLY if i is literal constant in source (must ship fully-unrolled, not loop-over-i), (d) Go gc compiler does NOT auto-FMA `a*b+c` (intentional IEEE 754 reproducibility), MKL/OpenBLAS uses FMA for ~30% inner gain, Reality cannot — document as known ceiling, math.FMA is per-call CPU-feature-detect branch so don't use in inner loop. **libxsmm small-matrix per-shape FLOP analysis:** generic MatMul at 3×3 = 27 mul + 18 add + 33 conditional branches + 27 index-arithmetic comps ≈ 30-50ns; specialised MatMul3x3(A,B,out *[9]float64) = 27 mul + 18 add + 9+9 loads no-reload + 9 stores zero-loop-overhead ≈ 5-8ns ≈ 5-10× speedup at 9 LOC; **critical type detail: *[9]float64 fixed-size array NOT []float64 slice** because Go BCE recognises array length statically eliminating 18 bounds checks; ship both *[9]float64 hot-path version + []float64 slice-friendly wrapper. **MatInverse3x3 via Cramer:** generic Inverse(A,3,out) ~500ns-1µs (LU + 3 LUSolves + 6 makes); specialised Cramer-rule ~10-15ns ≈ **30-50× speedup**. **Reality consumer shape inventory:** 3×3 (geometry rotations, chaos Lorenz/VdP Jacobian, em rotation, fluids stress tensor), 4×4 (geometry homogeneous transform, color CIELAB→XYZ, control state-space), 2×2 (gametheory payoff, color chromatic adapt), 6×6 (physics rigid-body inertia, control 6-DOF), 8×8/16×16 (signal FFT butterfly, compression DCT). **BLAS-1 BCE hint patch (15-25% speedup, 4-LOC each, zero unsafe, zero behavioural change):** `b = b[:len(a):len(a)]` reslice after `if len(a)!=len(b)` runtime check informs Go BCE pass that len(b)>=len(a) for rest of function — eliminates per-iter CMP+JAE on b[i]; apply to DotProduct/VectorAdd/Sub/Scale/MatAdd/Sub/Scale/EncodingDistance/DimensionWeightedDistance/CosineSimilarity. **4-way unrolled DotProductFast** breaks serial dep chain s_t1=s_t0+a*b → 4 separate accumulators s0/s1/s2/s3, ~1.5-2× extra over BCE = **2-3× total over current**, but **changes accumulation order** (LSB shift) so MUST be opt-in parallel-named variant to preserve bit-exact golden vectors. **CholeskySolve back-sub L^T loop has strided access** (L[j*n+i] strided by n) — only strided loop in package's decomps, fine at n≤512, thrashes at n>1024; document cliff or store both L and L^T (doubles storage). **tridiagonalize symmetric inner update touches strided w[:,k]** — stage to contiguous colK := make([]float64, n) once per outer-k step, inner becomes unit-stride, 1.5-2× speedup at n>64; the colK alloc folds into workspace struct. **AVX-512/NEON out by charter:** Go gc compiler doesn't auto-vectorise; only paths to SIMD are (a) Go asm in *_amd64.s/_arm64.s files = breaks no-asm rule + cross-language golden harder, (b) CGO into C kernel = adds C compiler dep worst-of-all-worlds, (c) wait for Go SIMD intrinsics golang/go#58610 not landed Q1-2026; **architectural posture: isolate microKernel4x4 as single function so future opt-in microKernel4x4_amd64.s build-tag companion is one-file replacement** without disturbing rest of package; ship Go-only canonical, document seam. **12-patch sprint table:** F1 BCE-hint reslices in BLAS-1 (~30 LOC, 1.15-1.30× each), F2 MatMul i-k-j reorder (~10 LOC, 1.5-2× n>64), F3 MatMul3x3/4x4/Vec3/Vec4 specialised *[9]float64 (~80 LOC, 5-10×), F4 MatInverse3x3/4x4/Det3x3/4x4 (~60 LOC, 30-50×), F5 tridiagonalize workspace pull-out + colK staging (~15 LOC, 1.5-2× n>64), F6 DotProductFast 4-way unrolled (~15 LOC, 1.5-2× extra), F7 Inverse(A,n,out, ws InverseWorkspace) (~30 LOC, eliminates 6 allocs/call), F8 RanksInto (~10 LOC, eliminates 2 allocs/Spearman), F9 Determinant inline parity (~5 LOC, eliminates visited[]), F10 BLIS 5-loop MatMulBlocked + workspace (~150 LOC, 2-3× n>256), F11 MatMulBatch3x3 (~30 LOC, i-cache+branch-predictor warm), F12 document FMA/SIMD/register-pressure ceilings (~20 LOC informational). **Sprint-1 ergonomic-speed (F1+F2+F5+F8+F9 = ~70 LOC, pure-additive or bug-fix, zero behavioural change):** 15-30% BLAS-1 + 1.5-2× MatMul n>64. **Sprint-2 small-matrix (F3+F4+F11 = ~170 LOC, Pistachio exact hot path):** 5-50× shape-specific. **Sprint-3 blocked-GEMM (F7+F10 = ~180 LOC, general dense throughput):** 2-3× n>256. **Sprint-4 micro-engineering (F6+F12 = ~35 LOC):** opt-in fast variants + perf docs. **Total v0.11 perf sprint = ~470 LOC across 12 patches** — takes package from "naive triple-loop + per-call alloc churn" to "BLIS-architectured + libxsmm-specialised + BCE-tuned + workspace-disciplined" = fastest pure-Go can be without crossing no-asm boundary. **Pure-Go ceiling vs MKL/OpenBLAS bounded at ~30-40% peak FLOPS on dense GEMM** (gap widens on AVX-512 hardware) — acceptable per zero-dep + golden-file + cross-language charter; consumers needing speed-of-light dense linalg call out via aicore. Report at agents/100-linalg-perf.md, ~395 lines.

103 | 2026-05-07 | optim-sota | reality/optim scores 2/12 on the cross-library SOTA-axis matrix (zero-dep + cross-language goldens; everyone else is NO on those, but reality is NO on the other 10) — the highest-leverage commits are pure interface design rather than new algorithms: scipy `Result{X,F,Iter,Nfev,GradNorm,Converged}` retrofit (~30 LOC, every optimizer already computes the fields and throws them away), Optimization.jl `Problem`/`Solver`/AD-backend three-axis decoupling (~120 LOC, unblocks every future solver as a one-file PR), NLopt `LD/LN/GD/GN` algorithm-name taxonomy (~50 LOC, makes nil-gradient a compile-time error), Hansen-2016 CMA-ES (~350 LOC, the single most-cited derivative-free non-convex optimizer reality currently lacks — `SimulatedAnnealing` alone is insufficient), and Optuna-style TPE+Trial define-by-run (~600 LOC, gives a credible zero-dep Bayesian story without needing GP/Cholesky); the §9 fix-set lifts the SOTA score from 2/12 to 9/12 in ~3,500 LOC of pure code with zero new dependencies, and the only frontier items reality should permanently skip are sparse-large-scale (needs a sparse linear solver dep) and learned-optimizer meta-training (needs torch+GPU+100k-task dataset) — IPOPT-grade NLP-IPM, BoTorch qLogEI numerical reformulation (arXiv:2310.20708), filter line search (Wächter-Biegler 2006), Lion sign-momentum (Chen NeurIPS 2023), and Sophia diagonal-Hessian (Liu ICLR 2024) are all in scope as small focused additions; clean separation maintained from 101 (per-line numerics bugs) and 102 (algorithm-by-name LOC enumeration) — this report owns the interface-design and engineering-trick axis.

104 | 2026-05-07 | optim-api | Eleven distinct solver call-shapes; six different "Result" return shapes for what is morally one concept (ConvergenceResult, FbsResult, AdmmResult, ([]float64,float64), []float64, ([]float64,float64,error)) — single highest-leverage commit is unifying these into one `Result{X,F,Grad,Iter,Nfev,Ngev,GradNorm,Status,Message}` with a typed `Status` enum (current `Reason: "tolerance hit on invalid iterate (R123 trap caught)"` is a free-form string requiring substring-match, promote to `StatusInvalidIterate` for machine-checkable R123 audit) ~250 LOC retrofit. Callbacks per-iteration completely absent across all 11 solvers — proposed `Callback func(IterInfo) error` carried on Config (not Problem), returning ctx.Err() unblocks context.Context cancellation that Pulse/Pistachio shutdown loops cannot do today, returning io.EOF means converged, ~35 LOC × 7 solvers. Constraint specification has five non-cohering stories: polyhedral hardcoded form (LP only Ax≤b,x≥0 — no equality rows, no x∈[lo,hi] box, no free vars), implicit bracket (Bisection/Golden), implicit init box (GA hardcoded `[-5,5]^d` regardless of caller intent — silent footgun, single highest-leverage 3-LOC fix), validator predicate (`*Validated` runs only at convergence not per-step — misleading name), indicator-prox composition (proximal). Proposed `Problem{Dim,F,Grad,FAndGrad,X0,Lower,Upper}` + `ConstrainedProblem` (embeds Problem, adds Eq/Ineq + Jacs) + `RootProblem` (separate because residual is []float64 vector, not float64 scalar — collapsing pessimises scalar case). Three structs not one because field meaning is load-bearing on type. Tolerance has one `tol` doing five jobs (gtol for GD/LBFGS, xtol for Bisection/Golden, ftol-on-residual for Newton, xtol-inf-norm for FBS, combined primal/dual residual for ADMM) and the two LP solvers ignore caller tolerance entirely (hardcoded `1e-10` reduced-cost cutoff in SimplexMethod, `1e-8`/`1e-12`/`1e-15` scattered through InteriorPoint) and SA/GA do not check convergence at all (run full budget) — proposed `Tolerances{FAbs,FRel,XAbs,XRel,GAbs}` with zero-disables-test idiom matching scipy `xatol/xrtol/fatol/frtol/gatol`. RNG seeding inconsistent: SA takes concrete `*rand.Rand`, GA takes `interface{Float64()}` (broader for QMC but loses Intn/NormFloat64 — Box-Muller via Float64 has cosine-variant tail bias, genetic.go:62 clamps u1≥1e-15 to dodge but biases tail). Latent: nil RNG panics with no defensive check. Sibling parity: chaos/ode.go uses closures-as-positionals for SolveODE/RK4Step (right call for ≤3 non-callback args, wrong call for LBFGS-grade 6-positional-args), control.PIDController is the right precedent for stateful streaming optimizers (NewAdam(lr,dim) → Step(grad,x) → Reset() exactly parallels NewPID/Update/Reset, currently no streaming-optimizer abstraction in optim — Adam/RMSprop/SGD-Nesterov from 102-Tier1 want this shape not batch Solve(Problem)→Result), control.TransferFunction is the right precedent for declarative problem objects with methods. Cross-cutting: 11 solver functions and no solver shape — three structurally identical Result concepts and two structurally identical Tolerance concepts will continue to bifurcate every time a solver lands until abstracted; lowest-cost moment to introduce is right now before 102 Tier-1 ships ~14 new solvers. R-pattern flags: InteriorPoint accepts b[i]<0 silently while SimplexMethod corrects (cross-solver inconsistency), NewtonRaphson f=0 returns current x with no error/flag (caller cannot distinguish converged-from-good vs failed-at-stationary), CubicSplineNatural panics where rootfind would NaN (no consistent error-vs-panic policy across package). Top-10 ranked patch list totals ~630 LOC zero new math. Owns API ergonomics; no overlap with 101 (per-line numerics) / 102 (algorithm-by-name LOC) / 103 (cross-library SOTA matrix). Report at agents/104-optim-api.md ~330 lines.

LBFGS is the single biggest win with `q [n]float64` allocated once per outer iteration plus `sk`,`yk` per successful step plus `xTrial` per Wolfe step (~32KB per iter at n=1000), all four trivially hoistable to a workspace struct mirroring proximal/Fbs `work []float64` precedent (the only solver in the package that takes buffer-reuse seriously); InteriorPoint allocates rp/rd/dx (~24KB) every inner iter × 30 inner × 50 outer = 1500× redundant allocs per LP solve; SimplexMethod is documented as "revised simplex" but is actually classical-tableau form with O(m·n) per pivot full-matrix update (lines 130-139) and Bland-rule reduced-cost scan that recomputes c_B^T·B^-1 from scratch every iter at lines 92-101 (O(m·n) instead of O(m+n)) — 2x cache-locality win from flattening slice-of-slices, 30x win from Dantzig+Bland-fallback hybrid, ~400 LOC for full revised simplex with Bartels-Golub LU; GA fitness evaluation at genetic.go:158-161 is embarrassingly parallel (~25 LOC for goroutine pool with semaphore, ×NumCPU speedup on every call) and the right place to introduce a `BatchEvaluator` interface that 102-Tier-1 DE/PSO/CMA-ES/NSGA-II/TPE all reuse — zero correctness regression because user fitness contract is already pure float64-in/float64-out; Sinkhorn convergence check at sinkhorn.go:174-181 materialises the entire plan with n×m exp() calls every outer iter just for the L1 marginal residual (doubles per-iter exp cost — 8M wasted exps on n=m=100, eps=0.01, 200 iters), but the f-update already produces log-marginals so residual collapses to O(n) (~15 LOC); SimulatedAnnealing and GeneticAlgorithm have NO stagnation early-exit (both burn the full maxIter/gens budget every call regardless of convergence — 5-10 LOC for "no improvement for K consecutive iters" cuts walltime 50-900n convergent problems); NewtonRaphson is the gold-standard hot path (zero allocs, inlinable scalar loop) along with all 1-D root finders + GradientDescent + Cubic-spline queries + FBS/FISTA/ADMM + SA — these eight are perf-clean as written; vector kernels vecDot/vecNorm/vecAddScaled are already optimal under Go SSA bounds-check elimination so SIMD/asm work is premature optimisation until allocation+parallelism gaps close; trust-region/Steihaug-CG and full-BFGS are 102 territory but the recommendation is to ship them with the proximal/-style `work []float64` workspace argument from day one to avoid re-litigating the LBFGS allocation pattern; top-7 patch list totals ~130 LOC zero new algorithms zero new deps for ~85% GC pressure reduction on LBFGS + ×NumCPU on GA + 2× cache locality on LP + early-exit on SA/GA. Owns alloc-count + parallelism-gap + early-exit + asymptotic-per-iter axis; clean separation from 101 (numerics) / 102 (missing algos) / 103 (SOTA matrix) / 104 (API ergonomics).
