cross-cutting findings include EscapeVelocity uses M not μ breaking package convention (LOW/MED — hits CODATA 2018 G uncertainty 2.2e-5 instead of μ_⊕ relative ~1e-9), KeplerOrbit rotation-doc says "3-1-3 Euler" without citing Vallado eq. 2-145 (LOW doc), output frame of KeplerOrbit is implicit-from-input not documented (MED — depends on what reference epoch Ω is measured against), Hohmann r1>r2 silently produces negative dv1 violating godoc "always >= 0" contract (MED), test suite has no Kepler-equation residual test so a non-convergent-but-bounded result passes all current tests (TestTrueAnomalyFromMean_ConvergesForHighE only checks not-NaN and in-[0,2π) range — does not verify |E - e·sinE - M| < eps), no parabolic stress test (e=0.999 M=1e-6 worst-case Newton-from-E=M starter), no hyperbolic KeplerOrbit test, no Hohmann reverse-direction test; reproducibility is bit-exact (only *, /, Sqrt, Cos, Sin, Atan2, Cbrt, Mod, Abs, Inf — no Pow, no FMA, deterministic Newton given (M,e,maxIter)); zero allocations confirmed (Pistachio-friendly per CLAUDE.md rule 3); citations present (Bate-Mueller-White / Vallado / Murray-Dermott / Meeus / Hill / Hohmann — CLAUDE.md rule 4 satisfied) but precision godocs do not document failure modes for edge cases (CLAUDE.md rule 5 partial). Report at agents/106-orbital-numerics.md ~265 lines.

107 | 2026-05-07 | orbital-missing | orbital ships 8 textbook two-body functions and is missing virtually the entire credible-astrodynamics surface — Tier 1 (~1,600 LOC, 21 fns, 6 goldens, 14 days): universal-variable Stumpff propagator (250 LOC, single highest-leverage commit, replaces e<1 case-split + closes 106-F1 parabolic singularity), Izzo Lambert solver (350 LOC, second-highest leverage — unlocks every transfer design), full anomaly-conversion symmetry (M↔E↔ν, 80 LOC, 4 fns), hyperbolic Kepler M=e·sinhF-F (70), Barker parabolic (50), RV↔COE state-vector conversions (180, 2 fns, Vallado Algos 9-10), J2 zonal acceleration + secular rates (130, frozen-orbit + sun-sync design), Cowell direct propagator wrapping chaos/ode.go RKF45 with Perturbation interface (120), TT/TAI/UTC/GPS/JD/MJD time-scale lattice (150, leap-second-aware, defers TDB), topocentric az/el/range + ECEF↔geodetic Bowring (120), Hill-Clohessy-Wiltshire relative motion + STM (150, ISS rendezvous / formation flying); Tier 2 (~3,500 LOC, 35 fns, 10 weeks): J3/J4 + triaxial harmonics, atmospheric drag (exponential + NRLMSISE-00 stub), SRP with cylindrical Earth shadow, third-body (Battin small-q form for cancellation, Meeus low-precision lunar/solar ephem ~250 LOC defers JPL DE), CR3BP + Lagrange points + Jacobi constant + L4/L5 Routh stability, Encke perturbation propagator with rectification, Variation-of-Parameters Lagrange/Gauss planetary equations, **SGP4/SDP4 (~1200 LOC, the most-used propagator in the world, validated against AIAA-2006-6753 32-TLE acceptance suite)**, gravity-assist patched-conic flyby, bi-elliptic + plane-change + phasing, IAU 2006/2010 precession-nutation + ERA + ECI↔ECEF chain (~600 LOC, full IAU 2000A nutation 1365+687 terms, requires IERS Bulletin-A EOP parser, foundation of every modern frame transform), Tisserand parameter (25 LOC); Tier 3 (~3,400 LOC, 20+ fns, on-demand): Battin/Gauss Lambert (Izzo dominates but historical/cross-val), Kustaanheimo-Stiefel regularization for r→0 collision singularity, Sundman dt=r^α·ds time-stretching, symplectic N-body (Wisdom-Holman democratic-heliocentric, Yoshida 4/6/8, IAS15), GR PPN correction (Mercury 43arcsec/century, GPS clock), continuous low-thrust (Edelbaum analytic + Sims-Flanagan + indirect Pontryagin for Dawn/Psyche/JUICE-class missions), DSST mean-element propagator (~1200 LOC, USSPACECOM-grade long-term LEO), TDB Fairhead-Bretagnon 1000-term series for pulsar timing/JPL-DE, JPL DE440 Chebyshev reader (~500 LOC arcsec-grade vs Meeus 0.01°), HPOP integration testbench. Cross-cutting structural recommendations: sub-package the propagators (orbital/lambert, /sgp4, /frames, /time, /ephem, /propagator) keeping core orbital lean, define `State{R,V[3]float64;T float64}` canonical type, `Perturbation func(State) [3]float64` composable acceleration interface, Frame uint8 enum + transform registry chained Floyd-Warshall (precedent: color/space.go 8-space graph), **time-scale lattice as named types `type TT float64` etc. — single highest bug-prevention API decision**, perturbation magnitudes table in godoc with calibrated 1e-2/1e-7 m/s² thresholds, add to constants/ J2/J3/J4-Earth + MuSun + MuMoon + WGS84 radii + flattening + solar pressure 4.56e-6 + leap-second count 37. Sprint ordering: v0.11 universal-variable + anomaly-symmetry + hyperbolic + RV-COE + J2 + time scales (~700 LOC), v0.12 Izzo + Cowell + topocentric + Hill-CW + Barker + Tisserand (~700), v0.13 IAU-frames-subset + SRP + 3body + Encke + bi-elliptic + flyby (~1100), v1.0 SGP4 + AIAA goldens (~1200) → "credible operational astrodynamics library" in 14 weeks; out of scope: SPICE port, GMAT-grade mission scripting, manoeuvre NLP (belongs in optim/), GUI, real-time TLE fetching, Skyfield API mimicry. Reference libraries audited: poliastro (canonical Python), Orekit (CNES production Java), GMAT (NASA Apache-2), Brandon Rhodes sgp4 (NORAD bit-exact), AstroPy (frames+time ecosystem), Vallado 4th ed (English-language canonical textbook). Report at agents/107-orbital-missing.md ~285 lines.

108 | 2026-05-07 | orbital-sota | SOTA library comparison head-to-head with poliastro / Orekit / GMAT / STK / pykep / python-sgp4 / Skyfield / Astropy / ML-augmented-prop, scoped to (1) headline algorithm + (2) engineering trick + (3) zero-dep-Go portability without duplicating 106 (per-line numerics) or 107 (Tier-1/2/3 missing-feature backlog) — niche is choosing *which* implementation each missing primitive gets ported from; the three highest-leverage 80/20 ports identified are (a) **Markley 1995 Kepler solver from poliastro** (28-coef public-domain polynomial + one Halley step, replaces Newton in TrueAnomalyFromMean and closes 106-F1 parabolic-divisor singularity + 106-F2 silent-non-convergence in one ~80-LOC commit, license-clean MIT-compatible), (b) **Izzo 2014 Lambert solver from pykep** (Householder iteration on Battin's x-parameter with logarithmic-transformed closed-form initial guess, 2-3 iters to machine precision for any transfer angle / multi-rev, 20-33% faster than Gooding 1990 + dramatically more robust at multi-rev than Battin 1987, ~350 LOC, ESA-ACT 2014 paper has full pseudocode, re-implement to avoid pykep GPL-3 contamination), (c) **Vallado 2006 SGP4 reference C from python-sgp4 lineage** (~1500 LOC public-domain port validated against the August-2010 AIAA reference corpus to 0.1mm over 100yr propagation, NORAD-grade, the de-facto industry standard against which all TLE propagators are validated, MIT-clean reimplementation slots into reality's golden-file model verbatim — would make reality the only zero-dep Go library with NORAD-grade SGP4); secondary high-value ports: Orekit's force-model-composition pattern (slice of `Perturbation func([3]float64,[3]float64,float64) [3]float64` reduced into RKF45 RHS, the cleanest API in Orekit, perfectly fits 107-T1.8 Cowell propagator at ~120 LOC), Astropy's frame-graph transformation registry (Floyd-Warshall over directed-graph of registered frame-pair transforms, ~50 LOC pattern + truncated IAU 2006 nutation top-100 terms ~3KB constants for ~1arcsec precision matching reality's "precision documented" rule), GMAT's PrinceDormand78 / DOP853 13-stage Butcher tableau (sibling of RKF45 in chaos/ode.go for tighter tolerance, ~200 LOC); **explicitly out-of-scope-for-reality** (correctly belong upstream in aicore or as separate sub-packages): pykep's Taylor-AD propagator (depends on full autodiff infrastructure, 6-month project), Orekit DSST mean-element semianalytic (~3000 LOC of deeply analytic algebra), STK EGM2008-360 (2MB harmonic-coefficient table — start with J2/J3/J4/J6 ~150 floats), Skyfield SPK/DAF binary ephemeris parsing (file-format engineering not math, defer to Tier-3 separate package; the *Chebyshev/Clenshaw evaluation* is portable in 50 LOC and could go in calculus/), AstroPy ERFA/SOFA full IAU resolution suite (1365+687 nutation terms is overkill, truncate to 100), STK/Astrogator differential corrector (build atop optim/ as Tier-3 trajectory targeter ~150 LOC — uses already-existing bisection/Newton/L-BFGS), GMAT mission-scripting DSL (product engineering not math), ML-augmented SGP4-residual LSTM/iTransformer propagators (75% along-track-error reduction in LEO/GEO with 0.018s inference per Curzi 2024 + Astrodynamics 2026 — but tensor/BLAS runtime is incompatible with reality's zero-dep mandate, belongs in aicore which imports reality and uses reality SGP4 as the baseline that ML correction sits atop); license matrix: poliastro (MIT — direct algo copy OK), Orekit (Apache-2 — pattern copy OK, content reimplement), GMAT (Apache-2 — algo copy OK), STK (closed-source, algos textbook), pykep (GPL-3 — must reimplement from Izzo 2014 paper, NOT copy source), python-sgp4 (MIT — but reimplement from Vallado 2006 reference C which is public domain), Skyfield/jplephem (MIT), AstroPy (BSD-3 — license-compatible), ERFA (BSD-3 — license-compatible); cross-library portability matrix: HIGH portability for Markley + Izzo + Vallado-SGP4 + Orekit-force-pattern + DOP853, MEDIUM for Astropy frame-graph + truncated-IAU-2006 + Chebyshev-eval, LOW/Tier-3-deferred for SPK file format + EGM2008-360 + DSST + Astrogator targeter, OUT-OF-SCOPE for ML-residual learners + Astropy units layer + GMAT mission DSL + STK GUI; bottom-line ordered roadmap consistent with 107 sprint plan: v0.11 Markley + anomaly symmetry + RV-COE + J2 + time scales (~700 LOC matches 107's v0.11 estimate), v0.12 add Izzo Lambert + Cowell + topocentric + Hill-CW (~700 LOC), v0.13 truncated-IAU frames + SRP + third-body + Encke (~1100 LOC), v1.0 add Vallado SGP4 + 32-TLE AIAA acceptance corpus (~1200 LOC) → reality/orbital reaches the same algorithmic core as poliastro / Orekit / pykep at MIT-clean zero-dep, well past STK on closed-source liability and within striking distance of NORAD-grade satellite tracking. Owns the *which-implementation* axis cleanly orthogonal to 106 (numerics axis) and 107 (what-features axis). Report at agents/108-orbital-sota.md ~290 lines.

109 | 2026-05-07 | orbital-api | API ergonomics audit of orbital's 8-function surface, scoped to state representation (RV vs COE), frame tagging, time-scale tagging, mu/body parameter convention, and sibling-package alignment — explicitly avoids 106 (numerics) / 107 (missing-features) / 108 (SOTA porting) angles. Headline finding: package is untagged-float64-everywhere which is internally consistent with reality but blocks 107's Tier-1 RV↔COE round-trip and is one transposition away from a silent foot-gun (KeplerOrbit's argument order is `(a,e,i,omega,capOmega,nu)` — i.e. ω before Ω — but every standard textbook ordering is a,e,i,Ω,ω,ν, meaning a Python user copy-pasting an `(a,e,i,raan,argp,nu)` tuple from poliastro into `orbital.KeplerOrbit(*coe)` gets a wrong rotation answer with no compile error — recommendation F, the highest-priority API break in this audit). Top three priority API touches: (F) fix Ω/ω order — silent wrong-result vs textbook, (D1) `EscapeVelocity(mu, r)` not `(M, r)` — currently the only function in package using G+kg instead of μ which violates the explicit package contract and costs ~7 decimal digits of precision (μ_⊕ relative ~1e-12 vs G relative 2.2e-5), (A2) add `RVtoCOE(r, v, mu)` — every Tier-1 propagator (107-T1.1 universal-variable Kepler, 107-T1.5 Lambert) round-trips on this and its absence makes their outputs caller-unconvertible. State-representation analysis: three options ranked — Option 0 keep scalar tuples (matches reality convention but loses type safety on COE-vs-RV mix-up), **Option 1b named types `type COE [6]float64` + `type RV [6]float64`** (cheapest type-safe, matches geometry/'s [3]float64/[4]float64 idiom, JSON round-trips trivially, compiler blocks COE-RV confusion — recommended for v0.11), Option 2 explicit `OrbitState` struct with frame/time/mu fields (most ergonomic, departs from numbers-in-numbers-out, defer to v0.11+ once 107-T1.1 universal-variable propagator locks the canonical field set so we don't break twice). KeplerOrbit forward-only smell: name buries that it's a coordinate transform (sounds like a propagator), returns position only when same trig already cached for velocity, no inverse — recommend rename to `COEtoRV(a,e,i,Ω,ω,ν,mu) → (r,v [3]float64)` with deprecated `KeplerOrbit` alias. Frame tagging: minimum-viable `type Frame uint8` enum with constants `ICRF, GCRF, EME2000, ITRF, TEME, Perifocal, Heliocentric, FrameUnspecified` — uint8 not string for cross-language golden-file bit-identity, defer until 107-T2 frame-transform functions ship (premature without `TransformFrame(r [3]float64, from, to Frame, jdTT float64) [3]float64` to consume them). Time-scale tagging: same defer logic — `type TimeScale uint8 {TT, TAI, UTC, UT1, TDB, GPS}` waits until propagator lands; leap-second table is dependency-creep risk mitigated by static-table-with-runtime-`RegisterLeapSecond`-extension hook (poliastro/Astropy precedent). Mu/body provenance: package doc says "μ used throughout because known to higher precision than G·M" but `EscapeVelocity(M,r)` violates this — fix is one-line `EscapeVelocity(mu,r)=Sqrt(2mu/r)` with kept-deprecated `EscapeVelocityFromMass(M,r)`; argument-order inconsistency `OrbitalVelocity(mu,r,a)` puts mu first while `OrbitalPeriod(a,mu)`/`HohmannTransfer(r1,r2,mu)` put mu last — pick state-first (idiomatic for reality), update `OrbitalVelocity` → `(r,a,mu)`; `Body` struct shortcut `{Mu,Radius,J2,Name}` for Earth/Moon/Sun/Mars goes in **constants/celestial** package, NOT orbital/, keeping orbital body-agnostic and matching poliastro's `bodies.py` separation. Sibling-package alignment: physics/ uses scalars only (don't copy for vectorial outputs), geometry/ uses [3]float64/[4]float64 fixed arrays zero-alloc (match this for r/v), chaos/ uses []float64 slices for arbitrary-dim ODE state (match for propagator integrand), linalg/ exposes named Vec3 type — recommend `[3]float64` (not `linalg.Vec3` to keep orbital → linalg dependency clean) for new functions returning position/velocity, document in package doc "position/velocity vectors are [3]float64 in the function's documented frame". Out-of-scope what NOT to do: don't ship Frame enum without transform functions, don't ship TimeScale enum without propagator, don't migrate to OrbitState struct until 107-T1.1 universal-variable propagator + RVtoCOE lock the canonical field set — premature abstraction here will bake choices that 107's Tier-1 work will need to re-break. Decision matrix: 10 recommendations sized low-cost-1-line to ~80-LOC, severities {1 HIGH-API-break F, 1 HIGH-API-break D1, 1 HIGH-add A2, 4 MED, 3 LOW}. Report at agents/109-orbital-api.md ~245 lines.

110 | 2026-05-07 | orbital-perf | Existing 8 closed-form orbital functions are zero-alloc by inspection (verified Grep: zero `make`/`append`/closures across the package, only stack-resident float64 + Cos/Sin/Sqrt/Cbrt/Atan2/Mod/Abs) — perf risk is entirely forward-looking in 107's backlog; three structural perf decisions dominate the next 4 sprints. (1) **Promote symplectic integrators (Velocity-Verlet ~30 LOC, Yoshida-4 ~50 LOC, Yoshida-6 ~80, Wisdom-Holman ~150) from 107-T3.4 to v0.12 default propagator** — RK4-Cowell global energy error grows linearly with t (10⁻⁸ over 7-day GPS, 10⁻⁶ over 1-yr OD arc, unbounded over 100-yr stability) while Yoshida-4 has bounded energy error oscillating about 10⁻¹² independent of integration length set by float64 roundoff floor only; perf gap at fixed accuracy is ~30× achievable timestep for high-e orbits, 3-4 orders of magnitude for million-orbit secular runs; ~80 LOC marginal cost is smaller than re-litigating the algorithm choice in v1.0 + symplectic is *both* more accurate *and* lower-allocation than Cowell-RK4 (Verlet needs only `aOld [3]float64`, Yoshida-4 is pure-function composition with zero workspace). (2) **Batched SGP4 over thousands of TLEs at one timestamp is the dominant industrial workload** (Starlink collision screening 5k TLEs/s, LeoLabs CSpOC, LSST observation planning) — scalar `(*TLE).Propagate(jd) (r,v [3]float64)` API kills throughput at 32k TLEs × 100 Hz = 3.2M calls/s ≥ 3.2 s wallclock per real-second; correct API is three-phase `InitSGP4(tle) *SGP4State` (~30 LOC of Brouwer-Lyddane secular rates + drag-deriv cache + deep-space-resonance flags amortized once per TLE) + `Step(state, jd)` (~80 LOC hot path, no preprocessing) + `BatchPropagate(states, jd, rs, vs [][3]float64)` (zero-alloc, row-major caller-owned writes) + `BatchPropagateParallel(states, jd, rs, vs, nWorkers)` worker-pool over runtime.NumCPU() with disjoint row writes (no synchronization on result array, ~25 LOC, same shape as 105-flagged optim/genetic.go GA fitness pattern); target 32k TLEs <50 ms single-thread <5 ms NumCPU=16 matching python-sgp4 published numbers. (3) **Caching layers for slowly-varying functions**: Stumpff C(z)/S(z) Chebyshev table (universal-variable Newton calls Stumpff 25× per propagation step → 150 ms/s on 1000-particle-60FPS visualizer; degree-12 Chebyshev fits |z|≤50 to 10⁻¹⁴ relative, drops eval from 100 ns to 15 ns, **6× speedup with no precision loss**, closed-form trig/hyperbolic for |z|>50 exotic-flyby tail); composite ECI↔ECEF rotation matrix cached with 1-second window (full IAU 2006 nutation 1365+687 terms = 10 µs/call but Earth nutation flat to 10 decimals over 1-second timestep → cache pre-multiplied 3×3 matrix, per-call cost drops to 9 muls + 6 adds = 5 ns, **1000× speedup**, configurable window for OD vs. visualization); one-element leap-second cache for UTC↔TAI (60k binary searches/s → 60k `if jd < lastBoundaryJD` integer compares); per-orbit `KeplerOrbitState` trig cache (current KeplerOrbit recomputes 6 trig of (i,ω,Ω)-constants on every nu evaluation; cache once → recompute only Cos(nu)/Sin(nu), 3× speedup in particle visualizer 18ms→6ms per frame at 10k particles 60 FPS). Cross-cutting perf-correctness gates that 107 PRs must pass before merge: (a) Workspace structs from day one (`type CowellWorkspace struct{k1,k2,k3,k4,tmp [6]float64}` then `(ws *Workspace).Step(state, perturb, dt) State`, same precedent as agent-105-flagged optim/proximal.go `work []float64` pattern — current chaos/ode.go RK4Step at lines 38-42 allocates k1,k2,k3,k4,tmp inside per-step function = 300k allocs/s on 1000-particle-60FPS, gc-thrashes audio thread); (b) `[3]float64` everywhere for r,v not `[]float64` not `linalg.Vec3` (stack-allocated, value-semantics, prevents heap escape, sibling-package idiom — geometry uses [3]float64); (c) Perturbation `Sum(p1,p2,p3)` composition without closures — `CompiledPerturbation` struct with explicit gravity/drag/SRP/3body field-pointers + branchless flag dispatch (~50 LOC, same shape as signal/filter.go Biquad cascade with explicit coefficient fields); (d) ban `interface{}` from hot paths — keep concrete `Cowell`,`Verlet`,`Yoshida4` types with method dispatch only at outer Step(state,dt) boundary not inside integration loop; (e) MJD-first internal representation (JD ~2.46e6 days from epoch loses precision to ~10⁻¹⁰ days = 10 µs in float64; MJD = JD - 2400000.5 stays in 5-digit range, only convert at I/O boundary, same precision-preserving trick as time.Time storing nanosecond offsets from recent epoch); (f) **benchmark-suite scaffold from PR #1** — currently zero `Benchmark` functions in orbital/ (verified `Grep -r Benchmark orbital/` empty; same gap as 005-acoustics-perf and 010-audio-perf); first 107 PR ships BenchmarkKeplerOrbit + BenchmarkTrueAnomalyFromMean + reference Kepler-propagation bench against whichever propagator lands first; (g) `testing.AllocsPerRun(100, fn) == 0` enforcement on every new propagator/lambert function — same pattern as signal/fft.go advertises "zero allocations in hot paths" in package-doc, makes CLAUDE.md rule-3 queryable from CI; (h) `go test -race` mandatory on SGP4 batch parallel paths. Sprint-aligned perf checklist: v0.11 (universal-variable + anomaly + RV-COE + J2 + time scales) — Stumpff Chebyshev table + KeplerOrbitState trig cache + leap-second one-element cache + [3]float64 r/v + AllocsPerRun=0 gate + bench scaffold; v0.12 (Lambert + Cowell + topocentric + Hill-CW) — **Verlet + Yoshida-4 added alongside Cowell-RK4 not deferred** + Cowell Workspace struct from first commit + Perturbation Sum without closures + RK4-vs-Yoshida-4 1000-orbit energy-drift bench documenting 6-orders-of-magnitude gap; v0.13 (IAU frames + SRP + 3body + Encke) — composite ECI↔ECEF matrix cache with configurable window + truncated nutation top-100 terms (108-SOTA recommendation) cuts cold-cache cost 10 µs→1 µs without affecting warm-cache; v1.0 (SGP4 + AIAA goldens) — three-phase Init/Step/BatchPropagate API + NumCPU goroutine pool for N>256 + 32k-TLE bench target. Out-of-scope deliberately: AVX/SSE assembly fast path (separate internal/asm package, ~500 LOC, not in 107's plan), GPU offload (CUDA/SIMD violates zero-dep), quad-precision propagation (math/bigfloat sub-package), JIT-compiled perturbation models (JAX/Diffrax-style, violates zero-dep), NumPy-vectorized API (Go batched-slice IS the equivalent), mmap'd JPL DE ephemeris reader (defer to 107-T3.9 platform-specific). KeplerOrbit micro-opts cosmetic for one-shot calls but high-leverage in particle-visualizer regime — promote to per-orbit cached struct; TrueAnomalyFromMean Newton inner loop has Sin+Cos per iter where Go stdlib lacks sincos primitive (signal/fft.go:66-67 has same pattern flagged in 010-audio-perf F-23) — internal/mathx.SinCos helper would halve trig cost across orbital + signal + cqt simultaneously. Single highest-leverage perf commit: **Yoshida-4 symplectic integrator (~50 LOC)** — promotes propagator from "drifts visibly over 100 orbits" to "bounded energy error over 10⁹ orbits", same multi-orders-of-magnitude leap FFT brought to convolution. Owns perf axis cleanly orthogonal to 106 (numerics) / 107 (what-features) / 108 (which-impl) / 109 (type-signatures). Report at agents/110-orbital-perf.md ~280 lines.

111 | 2026-05-07 | physics-numerics | physics package is a 28-function freshman-physics surface; the audit headline topics (stress tensor, principal-stress eigenvalue, Van der Waals, Redlich-Kwong, special relativity gamma, Lagrangian/Hamiltonian) are all in the missing-features column — package has no tensor type, no real-gas EoS, no Lorentz primitives, no symplectic integrator. 12 numerical findings on shipped code: F-5 HIGH-DOC the Pendulum "damping" term -damping*sin(theta) is non-physical and silently violates energy monotonicity at the extrema (sin(theta)!=0 while thetadot=0 means it injects energy at turning points and removes it through equilibrium), should accept omega as fourth arg matching SpringForce(k,x,c,v); F-4 MED ElasticCollision NaNs at m1=m2=1e160 because total-mass overflows even though (m1-m2)/(m1+m2) ratio is well-defined - rescale by max(|m|), 5 LOC; F-7 MED IdealGas docs say T>=0 but no guard, returns negative pressure on negative T silently; F-10 MED HeatEquation1DStep only Dirichlet BC, add Neumann sibling out[0]=u[0]+2r(u[1]-u[0]) for zero-flux ~15 LOC; F-2 LOW GravitationalForce overflows numerator G*m1*m2 at extreme masses before the quotient does, distribute as (G*m1/r)*(m2/r); F-3 LOW ProjectilePosition catastrophic cancellation on descending branch ~7 digits at touchdown not the 15 docstring claims; F-1/F-8/F-9/F-11 LOW silent-wrong-finite for out-of-domain inputs (OrbitalVelocity M<0 silent NaN, StefanBoltzmann T<0 returns +T^4, CarnotEfficiency Tc<0 returns >1 such as 2.0 for Tc=-100/Th=100, SnellRefraction n2=0 right answer wrong reason); F-6/F-12 doc-only on edge-case behavior. Cross-cutting C-2: design rule 5 ("precision documented not assumed") consistently violated by precondition-stated-not-enforced pattern across 6 functions, sweep needed - either enforce with explicit NaN sentinel or document the actual asymptotic behavior, no silent wrong-but-finite returns; C-3: golden coverage 3/28 functions (11%), violates design rule 1, all closed-form algebra so trivially fillable. Seven sized new APIs from missing-features list: M-1 stress tensor as Voigt [6]float64 (makes loss-of-symmetry unrepresentable, same trick quaternion/color use) plus closed-form Cardano PrincipalStresses via Smith 1961 reformulation (all three roots simultaneously via cos(arccos(...)/3), ~14 digits across full input range, more accurate than any generic eigensolver near degenerate eigenvalues because it avoids sequential-deflation cancellation, ~120 LOC); M-2 angular-momentum r x p and I.omega (~40 LOC, reuses geometry cross-product); M-3 Van der Waals + Redlich-Kwong + Peng-Robinson with critical-point closed form (~150 LOC, dominant numerical hazard is repulsive nRT/(V-nb) and attractive a/V^2 term cancellation on critical isotherm with same shape as F-3, mitigate with combined-fraction form when |t1+t2|<eps*max(|t1|,|t2|) detected); M-4 special-relativity gamma via Goldberg 1991 trick 1-beta^2 = (1-beta)(1+beta) to recover full precision near light barrier where naive form loses half its digits at beta=1-1e-15, ~80 LOC for gamma + Lorentz boost + relativistic momentum/KE + velocity addition; M-5 Hamiltonian1D = p^2/2m + V(q) plus Poisson bracket as closed-form primitives ~20 LOC, push Verlet/Yoshida symplectic integrators to chaos/ where they belong with ODE solvers; M-6 six elastic-modulus interconversion identities G=E/(2(1+nu)), K=E/(3(1-2nu)), lambda=E*nu/((1+nu)(1-2nu)), and three inverses, ~30 LOC with documented +Inf at nu=0.5 incompressible and nu=-1 auxetic limits; M-7 CriticalPointVDW(a,b) closed form Tc=8a/27Rb, Vc=3b, Pc=a/27b^2, ~15 LOC depends on M-3. Single highest-leverage commit: F-5 Pendulum damping fix - only place in package where implementation contradicts a textbook formula and silently does wrong physics (every textbook gives -damping*omega not -damping*sin(theta)), every other finding is numerical hardening on already-correct math. Direct answers to audit headlines: stress-tensor symmetry - package has no tensor type yet so the question is forward-looking, recommendation is Voigt [6]float64 with closed-form Cardano principal-stress (Smith 1961) accurate to 14 digits across full input range; EoS stability - IdealGas is unconditionally stable (no subtraction no iteration), low-T/high-P concerns only bite once M-3 lands and the mitigation is the combined-fraction form on the critical isotherm. Report at agents/111-physics-numerics.md ~320 lines.

112 | 2026-05-07 | physics-missing | physics is a 28-fn freshman-physics surface; six audit-prompt headlines (relativistic kinematics, Lorentz boosts, Lagrangian/Hamiltonian, Noether currents, classical EM stress tensor, GR primitives) have zero coverage anywhere in repo (verified em/, fluids/, orbital/, prob/, linalg/, chaos/, constants/). Tier 1 ~700 LOC 32 fns ship v0.11-v0.12: T1.1 Lorentz primitives 120 LOC 7 fns (gamma via Goldberg 1991 (1-beta)(1+beta) trick recovers full 15 digits at beta->1 vs naive form losing half precision, KE = (gamma-1)mc^2 cancellation at beta<<1 mitigated by Synge form (gamma^2 beta^2)/(gamma+1)mc^2 or Taylor at beta<1e-4, returns +Inf at |beta|=1 NaN at |beta|>1, includes Lorentz boost 1D + general 3-velocity 4-vector boost + relativistic momentum/energy/KE + velocity addition + relativistic Doppler longitudinal+transverse), T1.2 four-vectors+Mandelstam 80 LOC 6 fns (Minkowski inner with (+,-,-,-) signature default, proper time, s/t/u Mandelstam invariants from p1+p2 / p1-p3 / p1-p4, Compton scattered wavelength lambda-prime = lambda + (h/mc)(1-cos theta)), T1.3 Lagrangian/Hamiltonian 100 LOC 8 fns (L = T-V, H = p^2/2m+V, Action S = integral L dt delegating to calculus/Simpson, Euler-Lagrange residual via central FD, Hamilton equations qdot = dH/dp pdot = -dH/dq, Poisson bracket {f,g}, Legendre transform with 1D Newton solve for qdot - keeps physics zero-dep on autodiff, callers hand-code gradients for higher accuracy, symplectic integrators stay in chaos per agent 111 design rule), T1.4 Noether currents 60 LOC 4 fns (energy from time-translation E = qdot*dL/dqdot - L, momentum from space-translation, angular momentum 1D for planar, generic infinitesimal-symmetry current - pedagogical formula machinery), T1.5 inertia tensor + rigid body 120 LOC 8 fns (sphere 2/5 mr^2 / shell 2/3 mr^2 / cylinder along+perp / rod center+end / plate, parallel-axis I = I_cm + md^2, perpendicular-axis Iz = Ix+Iy planar, Euler equations Iomega-dot + omega x (Iomega) = tau principal-axis form, off-axis case via Voigt [6]float64 from agent 111 M-1 sharing closed-form Cardano principal-axes solver), T1.6 Tsiolkovsky rocket 30 LOC 3 fns (delta_v = ve*ln(m0/mf) with log1p((m0-mf)/mf) cancellation fix near m0 ~ mf, burn time, Isp -> ve), T1.7 Maxwell-Boltzmann 50 LOC 5 fns (Boltzmann factor exp(-E/kT), speed PDF 4*pi*v^2*(m/2*pi*kT)^(3/2)*exp(-mv^2/2kT), most probable sqrt(2kT/m), mean sqrt(8kT/pi*m), RMS sqrt(3kT/m) - random-variate sampler points to prob/distributions.go), T1.8 heat capacities + entropy of mixing 50 LOC 5 fns (Cv = (f/2)R, Cp = ((f/2)+1)R Mayer relation, gamma = Cp/Cv = (f+2)/f, entropy of mixing -R*sum n_i*ln x_i with xlogx helper for x_i->0, Gibbs free energy of mixing), T1.9 cycle efficiencies 40 LOC 4 fns (Otto 1 - r^(1-gamma) compression ratio, Diesel 1 - (1/r^(gamma-1))*(rc^gamma-1)/(gamma*(rc-1)), Brayton 1 - rp^((1-gamma)/gamma) pressure ratio, Rankine enthalpy form), T1.10 real-gas EoS 150 LOC restating agent 111 M-3 (Van der Waals + Redlich-Kwong + SRK + Peng-Robinson + virial expansion + critical point closed form Tc=8a/27Rb Vc=3b Pc=a/27b^2 + Joule-Thomson coefficient, dominant hazard cancellation between repulsive nRT/(V-nb) and attractive a/V^2 on critical isotherm needs combined-fraction form). Tier 2 ~600 LOC ship v0.13: T2.1 continuum stress+strain 150 LOC 9 fns (Tensor3Sym = [6]float64 Voigt {xx,yy,zz,yz,xz,xy} makes asymmetry unrepresentable like quaternions/colors, Cauchy + PK1 = J*sigma*F-inv-T + PK2 = F-inv*P, small strain epsilon = 1/2(grad_u + grad_u^T), Green-Lagrange E = 1/2(F^T F - I) large deformation, Almansi spatial, Hooke isotropic 3D sigma = lambda*tr(eps)*I + 2mu*eps, Hooke anisotropic with full Voigt 21-coef C[6][6], invariants I1/I2/I3, principal stresses via Cardano Smith 1961), T2.2 yield + plasticity 100 LOC 5 fns (von Mises from tensor sqrt(3/2 deviator dotted with itself), Tresca from tensor delegating to PrincipalStresses, Mohr-Coulomb |tau| - sigma*tan(phi) - c, Drucker-Prager sqrt(J2) + alpha*I1 - k, J2 flow rule deps^p = dlambda*df/dsigma), T2.3 elastic-modulus identities 30 LOC 6 fns from agent 111 M-6 (G = E/(2(1+nu)), K = E/(3(1-2nu)), lambda = E*nu/((1+nu)(1-2nu)) plus 3 inverses, +Inf at nu=0.5 incompressible nu=-1 auxetic), T2.4 quantum statistics 50 LOC 4 fns (Fermi-Dirac 1/(exp((E-mu)/kT)+1) and Bose-Einstein 1/(exp(...)-1) with stable 1/(1+exp(-x)) sigmoid form for occupation to handle E-mu much greater than kT overflow same trick as softmax-stable, partition function Z = sum exp(-E_i/kT), chemical potential ideal gas mu = kT*ln(n*lambda^3/V) thermal de Broglie), T2.5 classical EM tensors 120 LOC 6 fns lives in physics/em_classical.go not em/ to keep two-tier abstraction (Poynting S = E x B/mu_0, EM energy density u = 1/2(eps_0*E^2 + B^2/mu_0), momentum density g = S/c^2, Maxwell stress tensor T_ij = eps_0*(E_i*E_j - 1/2*delta_ij*E^2) + (1/mu_0)*(B_i*B_j - 1/2*delta_ij*B^2), angular momentum density l = eps_0*r x (E x B), full energy-momentum tensor T-mu-nu 4x4), T2.6 vibrations + mode shapes 80 LOC 5 fns (string mode sin(n*pi*x/L), string frequency (n/2L)*sqrt(T/mu), Euler-Bernoulli beam mode frequencies with beta_n*L roots {4.730, 7.853, ...} cantilever-fixed-fixed, Rayleigh damping C = alpha*M + beta*K delegating to linalg, log decrement ln(x1/x2)). Tier 3 ~400 LOC ship on demand: T3.1 Schwarzschild + GR primitives 150 LOC 6 fns (metric diag(-(1-rs/r), 1/(1-rs/r), r^2, r^2*sin^2(theta)), Schwarzschild radius 2GM/c^2, Christoffel symbols 9 nonzero closed-form, geodesic eq RHS d^2x/dtau^2 = -Gamma*u*u, gravitational redshift, Shapiro delay - horizon r=rs documented as div, refer caller to MTW for Kruskal-Szekeres), T3.2 Einstein tensor 80 LOC 3 fns (Ricci tensor from Christoffel + derivatives, Ricci scalar R = g-mu-nu*R-mu-nu, Einstein G-mu-nu = R-mu-nu - 1/2*R*g-mu-nu, formula realizers not solvers - caller passes Christoffel pre-computed, geodesic integration in chaos), T3.3 N-body primitives 70 LOC 4 fns (O(N^2) direct accelerations with fixed output buffer, Plummer softening r/(r^2+eps^2)^{3/2}, total energy + total angular momentum diagnostics - tree codes/Barnes-Hut/FMM/P3M out of scope, integration loops in chaos Verlet+Yoshida or orbital Wisdom-Holman per agent 110), T3.4 Compton + Klein-Nishina 50 LOC 3 fns (Compton shift, scattered energy E-prime = E0/(1+(E0/m_e*c^2)*(1-cos theta)), Klein-Nishina differential cross-section), T3.5 Joule-Thomson + advanced thermo 50 LOC 4 fns (J-T inversion temperature 2a/Rb, adiabatic index, ideal-gas sound speed sqrt(gamma*R*T/M), Clausius-Clapeyron dP/dT = L/(T*delta_V)). File layout post-expansion: physics swells from 4 -> 14 files each <=200 LOC (mechanics + relativity + lagrangian + rigidbody + tensor + continuum + elasticity + statmech + cycles + realgas + em_classical + gr + thermo + materials + optics), surface 28 -> ~95 functions. Constants additions: speed of light + electron/proton/neutron mass + Planck (reduced) + Boltzmann + elementary charge + vacuum permittivity/permeability + Compton wavelength + fine structure - most likely already there, physics should _ = ... import not redefine. Out-of-scope deliberately: quantum mechanics operators (Hilbert spaces, Pauli matrices, spinors, second quantization belong in separate quantum package), lattice QCD/gauge theory (10k+ LOC out by 3 orders of magnitude), FEM solvers (mesh-based belongs in fem/ or aicore consumer), plasma MHD solvers (defer integrators, keep closed-form Alfven/plasma-frequency/Debye constants), lattice Ising/Potts (prob Monte Carlo consumer), symplectic integrators (chaos per design rule - physics provides only Hamiltonian+Poisson primitives integrators consume), autodiff-coupled Lagrangian solvers Diffractor.jl-style (FD-based primitives keep zero-dep, aicore wrapper layers autodiff), GR numerical relativity BSSN/Z4c (4 orders of magnitude out, T3.1+T3.2 give primitives a numerical relativist would consume nothing more), unit-aware types defer to agent 114 physics-api (adding Quantity struct breaks numbers-in-numbers-out contract every reality package follows, right place for unit awareness is thin aicore wrapper consuming physics outputs and tagging like aicore does for prob distributions). Single highest-leverage commit: T1.1 Lorentz primitives 120 LOC - audit prompt headline ask, closed-form zero-iteration IEEE-754-clean with Goldberg trick, unblocks T1.2 four-vectors + T3.1 Schwarzschild gamma factor + T3.4 Compton + whole aicore-side physics-reasoning queries that currently return no relativistic support; second-highest T2.1 continuum tensors with Cardano principal stresses extending agent 111 M-1 ~150 LOC unblocks structural-mechanics demos + T2.2 yield criteria. Sprint plan v0.11 T1.1+T1.6+T1.7+T1.8+T1.9+M6 ~370 LOC ~600 goldens, v0.12 T1.2+T1.3+T1.4+T1.5+T1.10+M3 ~500 LOC ~700 goldens, v0.13 T2.1+T2.2+T2.4+T2.5+T2.6 ~500 LOC ~600 goldens, v1.0 T3.1+T3.2+T3.3+T3.4+T3.5 ~400 LOC ~400 goldens. Total ~1,800 LOC implementation + ~2,300 vectors goldens to bring physics to credible advanced-undergraduate-through-first-year-grad coverage. Comparison SymPy.physics ~30k LOC; reality 1.8k LOC delivers 80% surface every classical/relativistic physicist asks for at MIT-clean zero-dep. Cross-cutting golden-file gap from agent 111 C-3 (3/28 = 11%) balloons to 11/123 = 9% if rate held - PR gating must require >=30 vectors per new function (CLAUDE.md design rule 1), Tier 1 alone ~960 vectors ~3000 LOC of JSON, sprint plan must not let implementation race ahead of goldens, cross-ref agent 110 testing.AllocsPerRun(100,fn)==0 enforcement gate. Reference libraries surveyed: SymPy physics.mechanics + physics.units + physics.qft + physics.vector, Modelica Mechanics.MultiBody + Media.IdealGases, Diffractor.jl primitives autodiff-coupled, Feynman Lectures vol I and II, Goldstein Classical Mechanics 3rd ed Lagrangian/Hamiltonian, Jackson Classical Electrodynamics 3rd ed Maxwell stress + Poynting, Misner-Thorne-Wheeler Gravitation Schwarzschild + Christoffel, Sadd Elasticity, Reichl Modern Course in Statistical Physics, Tu Atmospheric and Oceanic Fluid Dynamics. 113 | 2026-05-07 | physics-sota | Surveyed 18 modern physics libraries (Modelica.Mechanics + Modelica.Media equation-based DAE with Pantelides index reduction, SymPy.physics.{mechanics,units,vector,optics,quantum} symbolic, Brian2 string-DSL neuro ODE templating, Manim/manim_physics + VPython/GlowScript + PyCav educational visualizers, Cirq + QuTiP + OpenFermion quantum gate sims, LAMMPS + GROMACS classical MD, JAX-MD + TensorPhysics + Brax + MuJoCo MJX + Diffrax differentiable physics on XLA, DiffTaichi + NVIDIA Warp DSL/codegen) and isolated SIX engineering tricks portable to reality without IR/JIT/GPU/CAS/symbolic-engine: T1 SymPy.units-style compile-time dimension lint exposed as a `testutil/dim_lint.go` golden-file helper parsing existing per-param SI annotations from docstrings (verified `physics/thermo.go` `IdealGas` already declares (mol)/(K)/(m^3)) into a 7-vector $[L,M,T,I,\Theta,N,J]$ dim algebra with one regex per param + 30 derived-unit constants, ~200 LOC catches NASA-Mars-Climate-Orbiter unit-mismatch class at `go test` with zero runtime cost no Quantity wrapper required preserving "numbers in numbers out" charter and 60 FPS hot path; T2 Voigt 6-vector for symmetric tensors (Modelica.MultiBody + FEniCS + Abaqus + ANSYS + LS-DYNA + OpenRadioss canonical, Voigt 1910) packs $\sigma_{xx},\sigma_{yy},\sigma_{zz},\sigma_{yz},\sigma_{xz},\sigma_{xy}$ into a [6]float64 + Hooke law as 6×6 stiffness with 21 indep coefs triclinic / 9 orthotropic / 5 transverse-isotropic / 2 isotropic, makes asymmetry unrepresentable like reality already does for quaternions/colors/Cholesky, eliminates the 33% redundancy that breaks symmetry preservation under composition (round-trip $\sigma\to\sigma'\to\sigma''$ accumulates $10^{-16}$ noise on off-diagonal pairs per op), independently flagged by 111 M-1 + 112 T2.1 making it the cross-confirmed SHIP-NOW recommendation; T3 LAMMPS `fix nve` + GROMACS leapfrog + Brax pipeline drift-bound contract — Verlet at timestep h oscillates total energy O(h²) but does not drift secularly per shadow Hamiltonian Hairer-Lubich-Wanner 2006 (RK4 by contrast linear-drifts $10^{-8}$ rel on 7-day GPS orbit vs Verlet's $\sim 10^{-12}$ bounded oscillation), engineering trick is not the integrator (4 lines) but **shipping the drift bound as a regression test golden file** like `{"system":"harmonic_oscillator","steps":1e6,"dt":0.01,"max_energy_drift":1e-13}` so future contributors can't silently swap RK4 in without breaking CI, lives in chaos/ not physics/ per 111 design rule (physics primitives, chaos integrators); T4 Brian2 parse-time RHS dimensional check (`dv/dt = -(v-El)/tau : volt` errors at load if RHS units don't match annotation) does not port as DSL but its lint scales to ODE residuals as a sibling extension to T1 (`AssertDimConsistent(rhsFunc, yDim, tDim)` checks $[dy]=[y]/[t]$), DEFER until T1 lands + chaos consumer wants it ~50 LOC marginal; T5 Cirq/QuTiP/OpenFermion module-level singleton gate-tensor caches (`cirq.X` = `numpy.array([[0,1],[1,0]])` allocated once at import never reallocated hashed by identity, prevents 100k+/ms gate applications GC-thrashing) is exactly the pattern reality already uses in `signal/window.go` Hann/Hamming/Blackman + `color/illuminants.go` D65/D50/A whitepoints + `prob/erf.go` Chebyshev coefs, applies NOW to 112 T1.5 moment-of-inertia tensors as `var inertiaCoef = [...]float64{SolidSphere:0.4, HollowSphere:2/3, SolidCylinderAxial:0.5, SolidCylinderTransverse:1/12, RodCenter:1/12, RodEnd:1/3, ThinPlate:1/12}` reducing 8 separate functions to one dispatched + 8 consts, applies LATER to Pauli/gamma matrices when 112-out-of-scope quantum/ pkg lands; T6 JAX-MD + Brax + MuJoCo MJX + Diffrax converged immutable-State pattern (`apply_fn(state,...) -> new_state` for XLA trace purity + grad-through-trajectory without explicit checkpointing) ports as documented contract for 112 T1.3 + chaos Verlet/Yoshida — `func VerletStep(s HamiltonianState, gradV func([3]float64)[3]float64, m, dt float64) HamiltonianState` return-by-value canonical form + `VerletStepInto(s *HamiltonianState, ..., out *HamiltonianState)` zero-alloc perf path same idiom as `linalg.MulMat`/`MulMatInto`, ~10 LOC of doc commitment unblocks aicore-side AD wrappers Diffrax-style without modifying chaos/. Six anti-patterns explicitly REJECTED on zero-dep grounds: Modelica DAE Pantelides index reduction (5000 LOC + needs CAS), SymPy CAS dependency (numerical lib wrapping symbolic engine breaks zero-dep), Brian2 string-DSL parser (runtime-parse attack surface + moves dim errors from build to runtime), JAX-MD/Brax/MuJoCo XLA (JIT compiler couples to TF/JAX), NVIDIA Warp / DiffTaichi codegen (GPU compiler), LAMMPS/GROMACS neighbor-lists + Ewald + PME (spatial-decomposition for $N>10^5$ several KLOC each, no consumer in reality where Pistachio particle systems are $N\le 10^4$ direct-$O(N^2)$-tractable at 60 FPS; 112 T3.3 already correctly bounds $N$-body to direct + Plummer softening ~70 LOC). Direct audit-prompt headline answers: PyCav (~2k LOC pycav.{mechanics,optics,quantum}) headline algorithm is canonical undergraduate physics nothing new but engineering trick is per-scenario JSON config consumed by VPython — reality already has this pattern via `testutil/` golden files, architectural validation closed-form + JSON test vectors IS the educational substrate consumer apps just add a thin scenario layer; Manim physics ships Pendulum/RadialWave/LinearWave + pymunk 2D rigid-body + Lens/Ray on Cairo backend, ZERO PORTS visualization vs math math-library at opposite ends of consumer pipeline, lesson is what visualization needs from a physics library is exactly what reality already provides closed-form deterministic functions Manim's `Pendulum.angular_position(t)` IS `physics.PendulumPosition(theta0,L,t,g)` per 112 T1.3; Modelica engineering is DAE solver stack which is out of scope but the equation-vs-implementation citation idiom + Voigt + NASA polynomial property fits do port; Brian2 LIF/HH ODE templating is the DSL parser which is out but its dim-check lint ports as T4. Sprint integration with 110/111/112: T1 dim-lint v0.11 testutil ~200 LOC genuinely new line item only T1 introduces this review, T2 Voigt v0.13 already in 112 T2.1 sharpened by this review as Modelica/FEniCS/Abaqus-canonical, T3 symplectic drift bound v0.12 already in 110 sharpened with golden-file invariant pattern, T5 inertia singleton v0.12 already in 112 T1.5 sharpened with Cirq-style dispatch table (1 fn + 8 consts vs 8 fns), T6 immutable State contract v0.12 ~10 LOC of architectural doc commitment, T4 deferred. Single highest-leverage commit T2 Voigt 6-vector triple-confirmed by 111 M-1 + 112 T2.1 + this review without it continuum mechanics ships wrong with it ships correctly-by-construction; second-highest T1 dim-lint only genuinely-new addition this review proposes ~200 LOC catches NASA-class unit-mismatch bugs at `go test` zero runtime cost. Bottom line reality/physics is architecturally correct closed-form algebra zero-dep golden-file-tested citation-anchored, the 12 surveyed libraries that do MORE (Modelica DAE / SymPy CAS / Brax-MJX XLA / JAX-MD AD / LAMMPS $N$-body) are doing categorically different things reality should not clone, the 6 that overlap reality's band (Modelica.Media polys / SymPy.units lint / SymPy.optics ABCD / PyCav scenarios / Cirq gate-tables / Manim closed-forms) all teach the same lesson closed-form + golden files + dimension lint + Voigt for symmetric tensors + immutable State for integrators = the entire portable surface and reality has 4 of 5 with T1 the missing piece. Report at agents/113-physics-sota.md ~397 lines.

114 | 2026-05-07 | physics-api | physics's 28 fns are all scalar-in-scalar-out with no vec3 or tensor types anywhere (only geometry/ uses [3]float64 in reality), parameter ordering follows textbook-formula reading-order de facto but is not codified so 112's incoming ~95 fns risk inheriting the inconsistency, and physics.OrbitalVelocity(M,r) name-collides with orbital.OrbitalVelocity(mu,r,a) per 109. Five concrete API findings: (1) KineticEnergy/SpringForce/ProjectilePosition take scalar v/x even though every consumer carries vectors — fix is additive *Vec siblings using [3]float64 not redesign, ~6 LOC each; (2) param-order rule must be codified before 112 Tier-1 ships, recommend textbook-formula reading-order with (state,params,fundamental-consts) tiebreaker as 5-LOC CLAUDE.md addition — status quo is more consistent than it looks, only NewtonSecondLaw(F,m) and Pendulum(theta,L,g,damping) are technical violations and both have defensible reasons; (3) Voigt 6-vector [6]float64 {xx,yy,zz,yz,xz,xy} unanimously confirmed by 111 M-1 + 112 T2.1 + 113 T2 + this review for continuum stress, reject struct-with-named-fields (breaks 4-lang golden JSON shape) reject []float64 slice (heap breaks 60-FPS) reject engineering-shear-doubled Voigt (breaks sigma:eps duality), use unscaled Voigt with 1/2 explicit at Hooke/energy site; (4) unit-bearing types correctly out-of-scope per Reality numbers-in-numbers-out charter, adopt 113 T1 dim-lint at testutil/ as build-time RHS-vs-LHS dim check parsing existing per-param SI docstring annotations into 7-vector [L,M,T,I,Theta,N,J] algebra zero runtime cost catches NASA-Mars-Climate-Orbiter class; (5) physics.OrbitalVelocity(M,r) collides with orbital.OrbitalVelocity(mu,r,a) plus accumulates G's 2.2e-5 relative uncertainty at every call site whereas astrodynamics consumers want pre-measured mu to 11 digits — rename to CircularOrbitVelocityFromMass(M,r) signaling pedagogical-undergrad-form. Direct audit answers: KineticEnergy(m,v) shape is correct (KE too short violates Go idiom + Reality noun-phrase convention; KineticEnergy(p) form forces caller to compute p=m*v adding LOC and losing precision when p comes from elsewhere), m-before-v matches textbook 1/2 m v^2 reading order rule (B), velocity arg singular today add *Vec siblings per finding (1); tensor representation Voigt 6-vector [6]float64 unanimously per finding (3); naming KineticEnergyRotational/KineticEnergyRelativistic/PotentialEnergyGrav/PotentialEnergySpring/LinearMomentum suffix-disambiguates with partner concept no abbreviations; constants/'s flat const float64 vs physics's func returns float64 are both correct for their layers connection point is 049's deferred constants/convert subpkg. Cross-pkg consistency table: physics is on unfavorable side of every vote (vec3 absent, named-tuple returns vs array, mixed param order) except universal SI-units-in-docstrings — fix is additive without breaking 22 scalar-in-scalar-out fns, only 4 fns need gentle changes (ProjectilePosition+ElasticCollision return shape to [2]float64, OrbitalVelocity rename, docstring additions). Twelve sized recommendations: A1 codify param order rule 5 LOC no break, A2-A6 add KineticEnergyVec/LinearMomentum/KineticEnergyRotational/PotentialEnergySpring/PotentialEnergyGrav 34 LOC additive filling energy-momentum gap matrix that today every aicore reasoning site computes inline, A7-A8 ProjectilePosition+ElasticCollision return [2]float64 16 LOC gentle break, A9 OrbitalVelocity rename 4 LOC, A10 Tensor3Sym=[6]float64 alias + accessors 30 LOC, A11 vec3/Voigt convention doc in physics/doc.go 25 LOC, A12 dim-lint owned by 113. Single highest-leverage commit A1 param-order rule 5 LOC prevents inconsistency in ~95 future fns inheriting implicit pattern; second-highest A11 vec3/Voigt convention doc 25 LOC same logic at type-shape level. Sprint plan: pre-v0.11 A1+A10+A11+A12 (~260 LOC including 113 T1 lint), v0.11 alongside 112 Tier-1 A2-A9 (~54 LOC), v0.13 alongside continuum use Tensor3Sym from A10 throughout. Total physics-api work ~115 LOC over 3 milestones zero breaking changes to 22 scalar-in-scalar-out fns. Disjoint from 111 (numerics F-1..F-12 + M-1..M-7) 112 (T1.1..T3.5 ~1800 LOC backlog) 113 (T1..T6 SOTA tricks). Report at agents/114-physics-api.md ~370 lines.
115 | 2026-05-07 | physics-perf | Existing 28 freshman-physics functions are zero-alloc by inspection; perf cliff entirely forward-looking; 3 dominant findings: Voigt-6 representation + 12 named contraction primitives (REJECT einsum DSL parser overhead) + Cardano closed-form principal-stresses; ~370 LOC marginal across 3 milestones.

116 | 2026-05-07 | prob-numerics | One CRITICAL: regularizedGammaLowerSeries (mathutil.go:200) is series-only with no continued-fraction Q(a,x) branch, so chiSquaredCDF (mathutil.go:187) / GammaCDF (distributions.go:397) / PoissonCDF (distributions.go:339) all return the wrong value in the right tail where x > a+1; cascade hits ChiSquaredTest p-values (hypothesis.go:186) at exactly the small-p regime that matters, then the 1-CDF subtraction in hypothesis.go:186 destroys remaining digits, then if pValue<0 {pValue=0} band-aid at hypothesis.go:188 hides the bug — package's documented ~1e-12 precision claim is structurally false; fix is ~50 LOC dispatch mirroring the symmetry-flip already correct in RegularizedBetaInc (mathutil.go:72). Three HIGH: (1) zero LogPDF/LogPMF/SF/LogCDF/LogSF surface across the entire roster — every PDF computes logPDF then exponentiates and underflows to 0 in the deep tail, while NormalCDF (distributions.go:47) lacks a NormalSF that returns 0.5*erfc(+z) directly so right-tail survival is computed as 1-CDF and loses all digits, ExponentialCDF (distributions.go:164) uses 1-exp(-λx) instead of -expm1(-λx) for small λx, copula/pdf.go got LogPDF right but distributions.go did not; (2) NormalQuantile (distributions.go:67) caps at Acklam's 1.15e-9 with no Halley refinement step despite NormalCDF + NormalPDF being right there for ~6 LOC of cubic convergence to ~2 ulp, and StudentTQuantile (copula/studentt.go:60) uses linear-converging bisection with eps=1e-10 capping at ~10 digits when 15 are available; (3) RegularizedBetaInc + Acklam coefficients are duplicated byte-equivalent between prob and prob/copula (copula/studentt.go:120-240) — promote studentTCDF from unexported in prob to exported StudentTCDF/StudentTPDF/StudentTLogPDF/StudentTQuantile and let copula/ import prob (it already does at copula/gaussian.go:7). Three MEDIUM: betaCF non-convergence at mathutil.go:142 silently returns last f instead of NaN (Welch t-test, Beta CDF, binomial CDF inherit silent wrong values for pathological a,b); math.Log(1-x) at distributions.go:259 + mathutil.go:77 + distributions.go:455 should be math.Log1p(-x) for boundary stability; ExponentialCDF distributions.go:164 should use -math.Expm1(-λx); no LogSumExp helper anywhere in package, Archimedean copula math.Pow(u,-θ) (archimedean.go:86, pdf.go:55, hfunctions.go:62) overflows to 1e150 for theta=50, u=1e-3 swamping the smaller term in float sum even though the closed-form is mathematically stable. Two LOW: Welch t-test pValue clipping at hypothesis.go:65,142 (`pValue = 2*(1-CDF(|t|)); if pValue>1 {pValue=1}`) hides the small-p numerical bug instead of computing 2*CDF(-|t|) directly for tail accuracy; ClampProbability MinProb=0.01/MaxProb=0.99 (prob.go:12-15) is far too aggressive — modern Bayesian code routinely operates in (1e-300, 1-1e-15), the constants quietly crush 2 orders of magnitude of legitimate posterior information at every aggregation step in LogOddsPool/BayesianUpdateChain. MISSING from roster (per MASTER_PLAN line 116): Student-t (only unexported in prob), F-distribution (none — needed for ANOVA), chi-squared PDF/quantile (CDF only and broken per critical above), negative-binomial, geometric, multinomial, Dirichlet, general MVN (only bivariate/trivariate CDFs in copula/gaussian.go via Drezner-Wesolowsky GL10 and Plackett conditional reduction GL16 — both best-in-class numerics in the package, Genz QMC for n>3 deferred to v2), Box-Muller, Marsaglia ziggurat (no samplers anywhere), erfcinv, expm1 used zero times, log1p used exactly once at copula/pdf.go:88, no logsumexp anywhere. Single highest-leverage commit: regularizedGammaUpperCF + dispatch on x<a+1 ? series : 1-upperCF mirroring RegularizedBetaInc's symmetry-flip pattern — fixes chi-squared/Gamma/Poisson CDFs, eliminates the if pValue<0 band-aid, ~50 LOC. Best-in-package examples already correct: RegularizedBetaInc dispatch (mathutil.go:72), BivariateNormalCDF Drezner-Wesolowsky GL10 (gaussian.go:149-196), ClaytonLogPDFFn pure log-space form (copula/pdf.go:73-92). Report at agents/116-prob-numerics.md ~242 lines.

(2) TFP's Bijector composition (Exp/Log/Affine/SoftPlus/Sigmoid/Chain/Stack/Invert) + TransformedDistribution wrapper — ~250 LOC pure composition generates LogNormal, Logit-Normal, every constraint reparameterisation, every normalising flow, and is the load-bearing prerequisite for Stan-style MCMC parameter-unconstraining (no-bijector-no-NUTS, period); (3) Distributions.jl's truncated() wrapper — single 60-LOC generic that turns Truncated(d Distribution, a, b float64) into Distribution; eliminates ~10 hand-rolled truncated entries from 117 T1.2 because pdf normalises by cdf(b)-cdf(a), cdf shifts, quantile composes; (4) Distributions.jl's MixtureModel + TFP's JointDistributionSequential — pure composition over Distribution, ~80+150 LOC, joins the Bayesian-modelling face on reality without writing a tensor framework; (5) NumPyro effect-handler pattern (substitute/condition/reparam/mask/scale/block) — pure functional composition ~200 LOC, requires bijectors first, joins modern PPL frontier without tensor framework. Total foundation ~940 LOC (1+2+3+4+5 plus dependencies). Convergent secondary findings: statsmodels GLM/IRLS engine ports as ~250 LOC family-agnostic loop where Family is link+variance callback tuple (Logit/Probit/CLogLog/Identity/Log/Inverse/Power × Gaussian/Binomial/Poisson/Gamma/InvGauss/NegBin/Tweedie); statsmodels state-space backbone (KalmanFilter as unified (Z,T,R,Q,H) engine) ports as ~400 LOC unblocking SARIMAX/VARMAX/UC/DynFactor/MarkovSwitching all at once and superseding the hand-rolled ARIMA in timeseries.go; R d/p/q/r naming convention with lower.tail/log.p flags ports to method-on-Distribution (PDF/CDF/Quantile/Sample + LogPDF/LogCDF/LogSF/SF as overridable methods); Stan NUTS-with-dual-averaging-and-mass-matrix-adaptation realistic budget is ~400 LOC not 117's ~250 (the dual-averaging Nesterov-2009 logic + mass-matrix sample-covariance estimation cost ~150 LOC each); BlackJAX's Kernel/Adapter/Algorithm three-layer factoring is the cleanest MCMC API in the survey and converges with Turing.jl's Sampler/Adaptor/Transition triplet — both winning libraries adopt the same factoring so reality should too. Stan-math's contiguous-arena AD-stack pattern is the canonical reference for slot 015 autodiff-perf's tagged-union+sync.Pool+Reset proposal — same primitive serves both perf gap and MCMC requirement. Cross-references confirmed: 014 autodiff-api's autodiff.Func is independently required by both TFP structure-aware MCMC (§5.3) and Stan AD-stack pattern (§7.3); 017 calculus AdaptiveGaussKronrod (Tier 1) is the dependency for GenericMoment/GenericEntropy/GenericFit harness and for closed-form-CDF-less distributions in 117 T2.1 (NIG, GHyp, Stable, Tweedie); 023 changepoint-sota's R1 Hazard+ObservationModel orthogonality split is structurally analogous to scipy's _pdf+_argcheck separation. Correctly out of scope (architectural mismatch with Go-first / golden-file / no-tensor-framework reality): JIT/tracing (TFP/JAX/NumPyro Python-overhead workaround), GPU/TPU dispatch (Pistachio is 60 FPS commodity hardware not GPU cluster), sparse-tensor distributions, R formula DSL (defer to future prob/formula sub-package), lme4 mixed-effects (defer until linalg sparse Cholesky lands per slot 092), Stan-language compiler (reality is Go library not DSL), SGLD/SGHMC (niche). Same kind of structural decision R's d/p/q/r made in 1996, scipy's rv_continuous made in 2003, Distributions.jl's Distribution{F,S} made in 2014. 120 | 2026-05-07 | prob-perf | Zero in-package samplers today (only MarkovSimulate at markov.go:99) so no per-call alloc churn yet, but the ~400-LOC sampler roster from 117 T1.3 lands into a package with no perf scaffolding — three HIGH structural items must be set BEFORE that lands or the perf characteristics will be locked in wrong: (H1) MarkovSimulate inner loop at markov.go:122-136 is the canonical O(K) cumulative-walk discrete sampler, fine for K<16 but quadratic-aggregate for the typical K=20-200 regime-switching matrix Sentinel uses; Walker-Vose alias method (1974/1991) preallocates two length-K tables once at matrix construction then samples in O(1) via two RNG calls and one branch, ~25× faster steady state for K=50, dispatch on K (linear<16, alias≥16) matches numpy's random.choice heuristic, ~30 LOC per row using Vose's stable two-stack variant; (H2) 117 T1.3 lists Box-Muller and Marsaglia ziggurat as peers — they are not, ziggurat is 8-12× faster (ziggurat: 1 RNG + 1 table lookup + 1 branch in 97% fast path; Box-Muller: 3 transcendentals per pair = ~140 cycles), CRITICAL nuance is do NOT reimplement ziggurat — Go stdlib already ships rand.NormFloat64 and rand.ExpFloat64 as ziggurat, design must DELEGATE for standard Normal/Exponential and reimplement only for Gamma (Marsaglia-Tsang squeeze 2000), Beta (Gamma/(Gamma+Gamma)), Poisson (PTRS for λ≥10 / Knuth for λ<10), Binomial (BTPE for np>10 / BINV for small np), Student-t (Bailey 1994); critical anti-pattern to avoid is putting the ~4 KB ziggurat tables as fields on NewNormalDist per slot 119 — tables are constants of the algorithm not state of the distribution, must be package-level var initialised once at init time; (H3) BetaPDF/PoissonPMF/GammaPDF/BinomialPMF all recompute LogGamma(α)+LogGamma(β)-LogGamma(α+β) on every call (distributions.go:258, 313, 375, 454) — typical Bayesian likelihood has fixed (α,β) and varies only x, so an MCMC inner loop with N=10⁵ datapoints burns ~24 ms/iteration in algebraically-identical LogGamma recomputation, slot-118/119's NewBetaDist(α,β) constructor migration solves this naturally by caching logB once, 3× speedup free with the API migration that's already planned, this is the highest-leverage perf delta in the package; (H4) zero batch APIs anywhere — every existing function is scalar in/out, Pistachio's 60 FPS budget eats Go-call overhead (~5 ns/call) plus prevents constant hoisting of 1/(σ·√(2π)) and 0.5/σ², a PDFBatch(out, xs []float64, μ, σ float64) form is 3-5× faster from constant hoisting alone, also natural home for autodiff dual-number gradients per slot 014 (HMC/NUTS prerequisite). Seven MEDIUM: (M1) betaCF/regularizedGammaLowerSeries CF loops at mathutil.go:108-141, 216-222 have no per-call early-out heuristic and recompute (a,b)-only invariants per call; permutation-test loops with fixed (df/2, 1/2) and varying x can hoist the (a+2m-1)*(a+2m) factor table, ~30% per-call savings; (M2) MannWhitneyU at nonparametric.go:120 allocates 2× per call (pool struct-of-16-bytes + ranks []float64), wrong SoA/AoS choice for sort cache behaviour, no workspace-arg form, GC dominates in permutation-test inner loops, fix is MannWhitneyURanks(out, x, y) per CLAUDE.md design rule 3; (M3) LogOddsPool at prob.go:196 allocates default-weights slice when nil instead of branching in main loop, BayesianUpdateChain calls this thousands of times; (M4) ARIMA at timeseries.go:134-274 allocates 4-6 slices per call (series/centered/autocorr/coefficients/residuals/newA), rolling-window pattern Sentinel uses calls ARIMA every N samples, workspace-arg ARIMABuf form is 5-10× faster on GC side, also differencing loop reallocates diff per outer-d iteration instead of decrementing single buffer; (M5) conformal SplitQuantile always copies+sorts full N-element calibration scores at split.go:58-60, 165-167, two optimisations are SplitQuantileSorted(sortedScores, alpha) for O(1) when caller can guarantee sorted, plus Quickselect O(N) expected vs O(N log N) full sort for single-quantile case = 3× faster, FW C# byte-equivalence requirement satisfied as long as rank-th element matches; (M6) NormalPDF at distributions.go:37 reads 1/(σ·√(2π)) from formula every call instead of const invSqrt2Pi=0.3989422804014327 hoisted at package init, 1-2 ns/call but called everywhere, pattern repeats across BetaPDF/GammaPDF/BinomialPMF (all five-line constructor caches per H3); (M7) Acklam standardNormalQuantile at distributions.go:76-127 is switch-on-region rational which kills batch SIMD potential, AS241 Wichura-1988 is single-branch + ~5e-16 worst-case (vs Acklam's 1.15e-9) — addresses 116-HIGH-2's no-Halley-refinement complaint AND the perf gap in one ~120 LOC port. Six LOW: (L1) PoissonCDF subtraction-from-1 at distributions.go:339 destroys digits in right tail, fix is direct upper-CF call (also addresses 116-CRITICAL); (L2) EffectiveSampleSize math.Pow(0.5, stepsBack/halfLife) per element at adaptive.go:86 should be decay-recurrence w_{i+1}=w_i·2^(-1/hl), 150× faster on the loop; (L3) MarkovSimulate closure-captured nextRand at markov.go:116-119 has 3 ns/call closure overhead vs 0.5 ns inline, also LCG should be PCG-32 or splitmix64 for variance-reduction in long simulations (stdlib math/rand/v2 already provides); (L4) ProbabilityBin make-buckets at prob.go:438 not a hot path; (L5) Acklam coefficients duplicated in prob and prob/copula per 116-HIGH-3, perf-irrelevant but risks divergence; (L6) LogGamma at mathutil.go:23-26 wraps math.Lgamma forcing stack write of discarded sign, inline Stirling for x>12 (most common in hot loops where x=α,β,n+1) is 15% faster, cumulative with H3 caching ≈5× on BetaPDF/GammaPDF. Lookup-table accelerations: Erfc/NormalCDF already stdlib intrinsic no win; PoissonPMF/BinomialPMF batch via Pascal-recurrence pmf[k+1]=pmf[k]·λ/(k+1) eliminates LogGamma cost for batch eval = 5-10× faster than independent log-PMF, standard scipy/numpy trick; future Lanczos g=7 n=8 Gamma faster than stdlib general-case dispatch ~40 LOC port. Batched-RNG plan: BatchRand{buf []uint64, pos int, src} of size 256-1024 amortizes source-fill cost, vectorized splitmix64 generates 256 floats in ~80 ns (0.3 ns/sample) vs 3 ns/sample unbuffered, 10× win in the RNG-dominated regime which is exactly the ziggurat regime. Highest-leverage commit (~250 LOC additive): lock in constructor-cached log-norm-constant pattern via slot-118/119 NewBetaDist migration BEFORE 117 T1.3 lands its sampler roster — cuts hot-path FLOPs 2-3× per H3, provides natural home for ziggurat-delegation tables per H2, alias-method preconstruction per H1, batch workspace per H4. Cross-package coupling: autodiff slot 014/015 batch LogPDFBatch is natural carrier for dual numbers (free HMC/NUTS gradients); linalg Cholesky (slot 056) already there for MVN sampling, only sampler missing; calculus AdaptiveGaussKronrod slot 020 will need same workspace-arg pattern as M4; signal slot 145 modified-Bessel-I/K missing repo-wide for Skellam/NIG/GHyp/GIG, ν-keyed cache pattern parallel to (α,β)-keyed Beta cache. Report at agents/120-prob-perf.md ~280 lines.

131 | 2026-05-07 | signal-numerics | signal/ FFT/IFFT/PowerSpectrum/Convolve/Window kernels are numerically clean (1/N at correct end-of-IFFT location, conjugate-trick correct, butterfly read/write order correct, window endpoints pin to 1e-15) but the topic title is mostly N/A: NO IIR (only first-order EMA), NO biquad/DF-II vs DF-I (no biquads exist), NO Hilbert (no analytic-signal envelope at all), NO RFFT/Hermitian-symmetry helper, NO Welch/PSD/STFT/Correlate/FIR/filter-design — these absences dominate the audit; the one real numerics concern is FFT twiddle-factor multiplicative recurrence (one cos/sin pair per stage, then complex-multiply N/2 times within the stage) which drifts as O(halfSize·ulp): ~1e-13 at N=1024 (well below the documented 1e-9 budget), ~7e-12 at N=65536, and breaks the documented 1e-9 only around N>=2^22 — fix is per-butterfly direct trig or math.FMA in the inner butterfly; one docstring lie — MovingAverage claims "uses running sum" but the body recomputes the W-element sum every output (O(N·W) not O(N)), the implementation is actually MORE numerically robust than a true running sum would be (no catastrophic cancellation drift over long signals), so the right fix is the docstring; PowerSpectrum returns squared magnitude not PSD (no 1/N, no window-energy, no 1/fs corrections — naming is correct, document the consumer-side normalization burden); window functions use the symmetric (n-1) form (correct for filter design, wrong for FFT-frame periodicity — the NumPy fftbins=True alternative is not provided, document the convention); IFFT roundtrip tolerance 1e-10 at N=16 is loose, true error is ~1e-13 and tightening would catch real twiddle regressions earlier; no NaN/Inf golden cases (a single NaN in FFT input pollutes the entire output spectrum because butterflies mix all bins — pin this behavior); zero blocking bugs, recommendations are docstring fixes plus optional FMA in the butterfly.

cross-language pinning targets I1-MinEntropy uniform-distribution H_inf=log2(n)-1e-15 + I3-StatisticalDistance Le-Cam-1986 inequality-with-Hellinger 1-sqrt(1-H2) leq TV leq H*sqrt(2-H2) golden-pin-1e-12 + I7-UniversalHashOneTimeMAC Carter-Wegman-1979-Theorem-2 collision-prob-leq-1/m-pin-empirical-over-1e6-trials-3-sigma + I16-Shamir-1979 (3,5)-threshold-secret-100-prime-7919 reconstruct-from-any-3-shares 1e-15-exact-modular-arithmetic + I19-stub LeftoverHashLemma-Bound m=k-2*log2(1/epsilon) Impagliazzo-Levin-Luby-1989-formula-pin; precision hazards I2 RenyiEntropy alpha-near-1-needs-log-sum-exp-otherwise-NaN + I3 StatisticalDistance-q_i-zero-when-p_i-nonzero-must-return-1.0-not-Inf + I4 HILL-entropy-incomputable-stub-must-return-ErrUncomputable-not-fake-bound-honesty-gate-146-G1 + I5 UniversalHashFamily a-must-be-nonzero-otherwise-h-collapses-to-constant-b-degenerate-hash + I7 UniversalHashOneTimeMAC ONE-time-only-key-reuse-breaks-Wegman-Carter-1981-info-theoretic-bound + I8 LeftoverHashLemma m-leq-k-2*log2(1/epsilon)-strict-inequality-not-leq + I12 SimulatorTranscriptDistance per-position-empirical-histogram-needs-min-1000-samples-otherwise-finite-sample-bias-dominates-bound + I15 Pedersen-bound-formula-only-stub-Commit-must-return-ErrNotYetWired-until-T1-EC + I16 Shamir prime-must-be-larger-than-secret-otherwise-information-leak + I20 leftover-hash-over-field gates-on-2-universal-property-of-h-which-Carter-Wegman-1979-h(a,b)(x)=((a*x+b)-mod-p)-mod-m-satisfies-only-if-p-prime-and-a-uniform-not-just-non-zero; out-of-scope deferrals MPC-Yao-garbled-circuits-GMW-BGW-SPDZ-defer-future-reality/mpc-package + Universal-Composability-Canetti-2001-out-of-scope-pure-formal-methods-no-Go-expression + Lattice-based-commitments-BGV-Module-LWE-defer-no-polynomial-ring-Z[x]/(x_n+1)-substrate-yet + Post-quantum-zero-knowledge-defer + zkVM-RISC0-SP1-Cairo-defer-blocked-on-Tranche-2 + Recursive-SNARKs-Halo2-Nova-SuperNova-defer-blocked-on-T2-IPA + Plookup-Caulk-cq-LogUp-lookup-arguments-defer-blocked-on-T2-KZG + STARK-FRI-Reed-Solomon-defer-blocked-on-T1-NTT + Bulletproofs-IPA-Sigma-2010-defer-blocked-on-T1-EC; partial-overlap-with-175-resolved 175 took zkmark-x-crypto-axis-substrate-T1-BIGINT-T1-FIELD-T1-EC-T1-PAIRING-T1-HASH-T1-NTT-T1-MSM-T1-POLY-T1-MERKLE-T1-TRANSCRIPT-Z1-Z22 this took zkmark-x-info-axis-information-theoretic-security-MinEntropy-StatisticalDistance-LeftoverHash-CarterWegman-Pedersen-hiding-bound-Shamir-secret-sharing-VRF-VDF disjoint primitives by design shared-base-only crypto.ModPow + crypto.ModInverse + crypto.IsPrime architectural-co-recommendation F1 zkmark/randomness.go 15-LOC-io.Reader-injection MUST-land-before-Tranche-2-commits-C-sharp-port AND F2 info/mdl + info/lz are free diagnostics for ZK proofs ship I10+I11 today AND F3 statistical-distance-bound is single missing piece every Tranche-2 candidate needs ship I3 today;
